title: Detecting Cyber Attacks in Power System AGC Using a Drifted Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Process
authors: Mingqiu Du, Xiaozhe Wang, Qinglai Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: The Automatic Generation Control (AGC) system, reliant on real-time measurements over communication networks, is susceptible to stealthy false data injection attacks (FDIAs), risking equipment damage and economic losses. We propose a robust FDIA detection method using maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) of a drifted multivariate Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) process. Independent of load observability, in various cyber-attack scenarios, the proposed FDIA detection method delivers accurate and rapid detection of sophisticated FDIAs, outperform-ing traditional Unknown Input Observer (UIO) methods, which miss detections, and Long Short-Term Memory Autoencoder (LSTM-AE) approaches, which suffer from prolonged detection times.

title: A Method for Restoring Dunhuang Murals Based on Improved Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Hongbo Wang, Yang Wang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Due to long-term natural weathering and environmental erosion, the murals of the Dunhuang Mogao Caves commonly suffer from degradation issues such as fading, flaking, cracking, and large-scale structural damage. Existing deep learning-based image inpainting methods frequently suffer from structural discontinuities, pattern distortion, and local semantic inconsistencies when processing images with complex line drawings and distinctive cultural patterns, such as Dunhuang murals. To address these challenges, this paper proposes HT-GAN, a Dunhuang mural restoration framework that integrates Hankel-Tucker low-rank structural modeling with generative adversarial learning. This framework enhances structural continuity and pattern consistency within complex damaged regions through explicit low-rank structural constraints. Specifically, the proposed method models local mural features as Hankel tensors and utilizes Tucker decomposition to extract low-rank structural correlations. These correlations are then embedded into the feature learning process of the generator, enabling the joint optimization of structural priors and texture generation. Furthermore, a two-stage progressive generation mechanism consisting of "coarse-to-fine" restoration is developed. The coarse restoration stage aims to reconstruct the overall contours and low-frequency semantic structures of the damaged regions, while the fine restoration stage incorporates dilated convolutions, skip connections, and PatchGAN adversarial constraints to further enhance local texture details and edge realism. Experiments were conducted on a Dunhuang mural dataset, and a comprehensive evaluation was performed using objective metrics including PSNR, SSIM, and MSE. Quantitative results indicate that the proposed method achieves a PSNR of 41.13 dB, an SSIM of 0.9779, and a low MSE of 0.000440 on the validation set, significantly outperforming several state-of-the-art image inpainting methods in terms of structural continuity, pattern consistency, and visual naturalness. Ablation studies further validate the indispensability of both the Hankel-Tucker module and the two-stage generation mechanism. This work provides a novel approach that balances structural controllability and visual realism for the non-destructive digital restoration of cultural heritage images, such as the Dunhuang murals.

title: Analysis and Reduction Methods for Floating Potential and Voltage Stress in IPT System
authors: Xingjian Zhou, Xin Gao, Dongxue Li, Chunbo Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: In high-power inductive power transfer (IPT) systems, kilovolt-level winding voltages can induce hazardous floating potentials on ungrounded ferrites and aluminum shielding structures, thereby degrading insulation reliability and increasing the risk of electrical breakdown. To investigate this issue, a distributed-parameter model incorporating partial inductances and parasitic capacitances was developed to analyze the floating potential coupling mechanism in magnetic couplers. Based on the proposed model, the effects of parasitic-capacitance distribution on floating potentials were investigated through finite element analysis (FEA) and experiments. To intrinsically suppress floating potentials, a distributed compensation topology with a parallel wound single-layer winding structure was proposed. By symmetrically balancing winding-terminal potentials and distributing compensation capacitors, the proposed topology significantly reduced floating potentials and winding voltage stress. Experimental results demonstrated that the ferrite and aluminum-shield potentials were reduced to 4% of the inverter output potential, the maximum ground-referenced voltage was reduced by 71%, and the winding-terminal voltage stress was reduced by 52%, accompanied by reduced dielectric loss associated with parasitic capacitive coupling. Experimental verification using an 80-kW prototype further confirmed the feasibility of the proposed method for high-power IPT systems.

title: Feedback-Guided Hierarchical Transmission for Multi-Robot Mapping under Bandwidth Constraints_supp1-3699241.mp4
authors: Jinglei Li
publish: N/A
abstract: As multi-robot mapping systems expand into data intensive environments, efficient data transmission methods are essential for maintaining global consistency and safety under scarce and time-varying bandwidth. In this work, we propose a Feedback-Guided Hierarchical Transmission framework for centralized multi-robot mapping. In this framework, the submap representation is structured with three layers: L1 extracts the pose-and-factor skeleton to ensure topological consistency with minimal overhead; L2 streams geometric details, prioritized by semantic-weighted entropy reduction to refine the occupancy map; L3 delivers semantic labels to support high-level under-standing. To optimize transmission, the backend performs centralized occupancy fusion and employs Truncated Least Squares Graduated Non-Convexity (TLS–GNC) pose-graph optimization and a Lambda-Field risk model to produce guidance signals. These signals dynamically reshape the utility curves of the layers to prioritize loop-closure consistency and safety-critical regions. Experiments are conducted on S3DIS and ScanNet indoor RGB D datasets to validate the effectiveness of the proposed frame work. The performance evaluation under bandwidth sweeps and packet loss tests indicates that our method reaches joint geometry-and-semantics accuracy with fewer bytes, recovers loop closures faster, and lowers estimated collision risk in rendezvous regions compared with non-hierarchical baselines.

title: A New Axial-Flux Dual-Rotor Variable Reluctance Resolver
authors: Murat Onsa, Metin Aydin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: This paper presents a novel axial-flux dual-rotor variable reluctance resolver (AF-VRR) developed for high-precision rotor position sensing in permanent magnet motor drive systems. The proposed topology employs a single toroidal excitation winding located around the stator core, enabling simultaneous excitation of both stator surfaces and providing a compact dual-rotor configuration. The winding layout is designed to ensure symmetric magnetic flux distribution and accurate sine–cosine signal generation. An initial electromagnetic design is developed based on resolver design constraints and evaluated using both two-dimensional (2D) linearized and full three-dimensional (3D) finite element analysis (FEA). A prototype of the proposed resolver is manufactured and experimentally tested. The measured results show good agreement with simulation results. The proposed AF-VRR demonstrates a compact structure, reduced winding complexity, and reliable signal generation, making it suitable for integration in electric motor drive systems.

title: High-frequency monitoring of sea-fog/low-clouds in the Arctic summer daytime from multi-satellite synergy
authors: Huiyun Ma, Zengwei Liu, Jiedong Liu, Junjie Yan, Huihui Feng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: It is crucial to detect sea-fog/low-clouds precisely for potential navigation in the Arctic. This study developed a novel model for high-frequency detection of daytime sea-fog/low-clouds in the Arctic with the aid of nine polar-orbiting meteorological satellite observations. Methodologically, the sea surface could be first extracted by considering the visible and near-infrared characteristics under different solar zenith angles (SZAs). Then, the gray-level co-occurrence matrix (GLCM) and random forest (RF) approaches were adopted to distinguish sea-fog/low-clouds and mid-high clouds through the difference in vapor absorption in thermal infrared bands. The validation results showed that our algorithm presented high performance over different satellite platforms. The overall probability of detection (POD), false alarm ratio (FAR) and critical success index (CSI) were 75.39%, 12.35% and 68.17%, respectively. The research results are important for the safety of shipping in the Arctic and the research mechanism of formation and development of Arctic sea-fog/low-clouds.

title: Precision Measurement Method for High-order Aspheric Surface Based on Pinhole Point Diffraction Interferometry and Optimized Sub-aperture Stitching
authors: Leqi Geng, Bing Li, Zhuo Zhao, Zhenchuan Hu, Jiasheng Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address the challenges of insufficient accuracy and poor versatility faced by conventional interferometers in measuring high-order aspheric surfaces, this paper proposes an optimized annular sub-aperture stitching method based on a reverse global planning strategy. The method initiates sub-aperture division from the edge region with the highest asphericity, aiming to maximize the effective measurement range of each individual sub-aperture and setting the minimization of the number of sub-apertures (N) as the core optimization objective. The algorithm features a concise structure, high stability, and ease of automation, enabling a significant extension of the interferometer's dynamic measurement range. Simulation studies demonstrate that the method effectively reduces the required number of sub-apertures, thereby suppressing the propagation and accumulation of mechanical positioning errors during the stitching process. Furthermore, by integrating this method with pinhole point diffraction interferometry (PPDI), a measurement system combining high accuracy and large measurement range is constructed. Experimental measurements of a convex high-order aspheric surface yield stitched results that are in excellent agreement with full-aperture measurements, with the peak-to-valley (PV) deviation of only 2.66 nm and the root-mean-square (RMS) deviation of only 0.44 nm. A detailed uncertainty analysis of the system confirms the reliability and reproducibility of the measurement results. This study not only provides an efficient method for the precision measurement of aspheric surfaces with large deviations but also offers a new technical pathway for enhancing the measurement capability and automation level of interferometers.

title: On Integrating Friction Dynamics Into Cosserat Models for Tendon-Driven Continuum Manipulators_supp1-3694254.mp4
authors: Filipe de Carvalho Pedrosa
publish: N/A
abstract: This article presents a dynamic modeling framework for tendon-driven continuum manipulators, with a focus on cardiac catheters. Tendon compliance and tendon–sheath friction produce nonuniform tensions and motion-history-dependent dynamics (hysteresis), which, if neglected, degrade model fidelity and control. We develop a Cosserat-based formulation that integrates tendon compliance and a dynamic LuGre friction law into the governing nonlinear PDEs. This allows geometrically exact spatiotemporal computation of tendon–tension transmission without assuming initial friction states. Experiments on two clinical catheters demonstrate that incorporating friction dynamics reduces distal-tip error from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.62\pm 1.01$</tex-math></inline-formula> mm (frictionless) to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.99\pm 0.65$</tex-math></inline-formula> mm (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\approx 39\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> improvement), and achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.31\pm 0.91$</tex-math></inline-formula> mm mean absolute error for out-of-plane spatial actuation of a four-tendon ICE catheter. Comparative analysis against a Coulomb-based capstan model highlights the significance of dynamic friction, which produces rate-dependent tension transmission and more accurate modeling of hysteresis effects.

title: A Novel Stereo Tracking Technology for Intraoperative Intervertebral Motion Compensation in Robot-assisted Laminectomy
authors: Wenyuan Sun, Xiaoyang Zou, Yidan Chen, Guoyan Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Robot-Assisted Laminectomy (RAL) has become an emerging approach for treatment of lumbar spinal stenosis. However, the spine is an articulated structure that can be deformed under contact force during grinding. Patient-image registration performed at the start of RAL cannot account for intraoperative intervertebral motion, resulting in degraded surgical accuracy and compromised safety. To tackle this issue, we propose a novel stereo tracking technology for intraoperative intervertebral motion compensation in RAL. Our tracking technology is a marker-based method, consisting of customized markers, a coding system, and a labeling and position tracking framework. Specifically, we first propose a new type of markers featured by binary-coded spatial patterns, referred to as SurgTag marker. Each marker in the codebook has a unique digital IDentification (ID) code and a fixed set of keypoints. By rigidly attaching a marker with a unique ID to each vertebra during RAL, we can simultaneously track multiple vertebrae. We then introduce a two-stage SurgTag-marker-based tracking framework consisting of a marker labeling stage followed by a keypoint detection stage. In theory, we formulate both marker labeling and keypoint detection as one-to-one set prediction problems and further propose respectively two different types of DEtection-TRansformer (DETR)-based detector: Marker-DETR and Keypoint-DETR. Marker poses are then estimated based on detected keypoints, enabling tracking and compensation for intervertebral motion. Comprehensive experiments were conducted for evaluation. The proposed method achieved on average a submillimeter translation error and a sub-degree orientation error on both a synthetic dataset and an ex-vivo dataset. Additionally, surgical robot with the proposed technology achieved a submillimeter grinding error, demonstrating its effectiveness for improving surgical accuracy. The code will be publicly available at https://github.com/Wenyuan-Sun/StereoTracking.

title: Neuro Chair: A Smart IoT-Based Ergonomic System for Real-Time Stress and Posture Monitoring
authors: R.M.D.E. Rajapaksha, R.M.H.L. Rathnayaka, A.J.M.S. Sithumini, S.A.H.D.M. Perera, Anuradha Jayakody, Dinith Primal
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper presents NeuroChair, a smart IoT-based ergonomic chair system for real-time posture and stress monitoring using multi-sensor data fusion and machine learning techniques. The system integrates physiological and pressure-based sensors to capture both biomechanical and physiological signals. Data are processed through an edge device and transmitted via the MQTT protocol to a cloud-based platform for further analysis. An XGBoost-based classification model is utilized to detect posture abnormalities and stress conditions. Experimental results demonstrate high accuracy and robust performance in both stress detection and posture classification tasks. The system provides real-time feedback through an interactive dashboard, promoting improved workplace ergonomics and user well-being. The proposed approach highlights the effectiveness of combining subjective and sensor-based data for scalable and intelligent health monitoring.

title: Grama Medai: A Portable AI-Enabled Diagnostic Laboratory for Rural Healthcare
authors: Pavithra M, Naveen Kumar S, Rakesh Varsan S, Reshma Dharshini S, Sutiksn A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Access to timely, affordable, and accurate healthcare diagnostics remains a major challenge in rural regions of India, where advanced medical facilities are largely concentrated in urban centers. Rural populations often face delayed detection of chronic diseases, such as diabetes, anemia, hypertension, and respiratory disorders, due to inadequate infrastructure, shortage of trained professionals, and long travel distances for basic laboratory testing. These barriers lead to delayed treatment and limited adoption of preventive health care. This study presents an enhanced version of Grama MedAI, a portable AI-enabled diagnostic laboratory designed for early disease detection and predictive risk assessment in resource-limited settings. The system integrates non-invasive biosensors with finger-prick capillary blood testing to measure vital parameters and key biomarkers. The data are processed locally using embedded hardware and analyzed using machine learning algorithms to generate a multi-level risk classification. Operating offline with optional cloud support, the system enables continuous monitoring and provides a scalable and cost-effective solution for improving rural healthcare delivery.

title: Evaluation of Computer Vision-Driven Autonomous Water Landing of Amphibious UAVs Using YOLOv8 and MATLAB
authors: Elumalai Kannan, Surendar Ganesan, Balasubramanian Esakki, Nirmal Prabhath Ch
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Traditional sensors, such as GPS, barometric, and ultrasonic sensors, have limitations in accurately reflecting and dynamically capturing data from water surfaces, particularly during the unsafe landing of a UAV at precise locations. To overcome these limitations, a computer vision-driven landing zone detection framework using a YOLOv8 deep learning model is being trained on a custom dataset and imported within a MATLAB simulation environment. In this research, a MATLAB-based simulation architecture for identifying safe water landing zones in a camera-based virtual environment is designed. The custom datasets are annotated and trained using the YOLOv8 architecture with the best.pt weights are deployed in MATLAB for real-time inference. In the MATLAB environment, the virtual camera sensor processes input video streams, providing real-time object detection and classification and enabling the identification of safe landing zones over the water body under varying environmental conditions, including dynamic water and lighting conditions. The proposed system achieves high detection accuracy in safe-zone landing detection and demonstrates the effectiveness of the computer vision-based autonomous water-landing system for amphibious UAVs. The results show that the planned methods are suitable for applications, such as environmental monitoring, search and rescue, and maritime surveillance operations involving amphibious UAVs. Future work will focus on improving simulation-based validation for real-time embedded hardware deployment using an NVIDIA Jetson Nano, integrating an RGB camera and mmWave radar.

title: SmuggleDoS: Evasive DDoS Attacks using HTTP Request Smuggling
authors: Deeraj U, Pradeep Kumar Tiwari, Vasudevan A.R., Amit Praseed
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Application Layer Distributed Denial of Service attacks are one of the major threat to web security now. The low attack volume of these attacks, coupled with the myriad ways in which they can be launched make these attacks challenging to detect. HTTP(Hypertext Transfer Protocol) Request Smuggling is an attack that make use of the differences in how front-end and back-end servers parse HTTP requests. Request smuggling has been extensively used to deliver a wide variety of attack payloads including cache poisoning, information stealing and XSS(Cross-Site scripting). In this work, we present SmuggleDoS, a novel application layer DDoS attack that uses HTTP request smuggling to deliver DDoS(Distributed Denial-of-Service) attack payloads. Configurations where the front-end server or firewall gives importance to the Content-Length header, while the back-end server give importance to the Transfer-Encoding header are especially vulnerable to SmuggleDoS. Experiments demonstrate that when multiple legitimate HTTP requests are smuggled inside an outer HTTP request, such front-end servers or firewalls log the request as single request, while the back-end servers perceive and process multiple requests, effectively defeating rate-limiting based DDoS mitigation mechanisms.

title: An Empirical Comparison of Traditional ML and GPT-Based LLMS for Turkish E-Commerce Review Moderation
authors: Muhammed Abdullah Özdemir, Melih Rıza Koç, Mustafa Buğra Yılmaz, Mehmet Ali Ekmiş, Bilal Ekinci
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: User-generated content, especially customer reviews, strongly influences trust and purchase decisions in e-commerce. As platforms scale, these review streams inevitably accumulate spam, manipulation, and toxic language. While accurate moderation is essential to protect platform integrity, manual filtering is both costly and inconsistent. Conversely, traditional supervised models (e.g., Logistic Regression, SVM, XGBoost) require extensive feature engineering to handle class imbalance and frequently struggle with the contextual nuances of morphologically rich languages like Turkish. To address this gap, this study presents an empirical comparison between traditional machine learning baselines and a GPT-based large language model (LLM) for moderating Turkish e-commerce reviews, utilizing 16,531 labeled realworld samples. We evaluate the models using precision, recall, and F1 metrics, complemented by a false-positive-focused error analysis. Our results demonstrate that the GPT-based approach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(F 1=0.920)$</tex> significantly outperforms the best traditional baseline, XGBoost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{F 1} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 8 5 7})$</tex>. More importantly, the LLM reduces false positives by 54 % relative to XGBoost, effectively preventing the erroneous rejection of harsh but legitimate customer criticism. These findings highlight that LLMs offer a scalable, semantically aware solution for content moderation in operational e-commerce environments.

title: Hybrid Knowledge Graph and Graph Neural Network Framework for Context-Aware Consumer Behavior Modeling in E-commerce
authors: A. Balamurugan, G. Sivakumar, Palagiri Mabjan, Bollam Srivani, Sabina A. Nair, Naveen Nandal
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: More and more people are replacing traditional shopping methods with online payments and purchases because the Internet and web technologies are used all over the world. This Paper Proposes Knowledge Graph and Graph Neural Network framework for Context-Aware Consumer Behavior Modelling in E-commerce. The framework combines deep relational learning through GNN with semantic entity relationships from a knowledge graph. Using a dataset, this model uses TF-ICF Feature extraction for the textual comments and Structural graph representation for entity embeddings. The experimental findings show that the suggested KGGNN model successfully captures intricate patterns of behaviour and achieves a prediction accuracy of 97.12%. The results show that e-commerce consumer behaviour modelling is greatly improved with the help of a framework that combines knowledge graphs with graph neural networks. This framework can improve recommendation systems, targeted marketing, and the general performance of the platform.

title: A Smart LoRaWAN-based Diabetic Monitoring System using Dual Coordinate Descent SVM for Data Transmission Optimization
authors: C. Seetha Lakshmi, A. Angel Cerli
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Due to the exploding number of remote healthcare applications, the need for reliable and energy-efficient data transmission for continuous diabetic monitoring is also on the rise. However, the conventional LoRaWAN based systems have static or rule-based scheduling mechanisms which lead to excessive energy consumption, network congestion and delayed detection of critical health events. The goal of this study is to create an intelligent information communication framework based on adaptation that is efficient in terms of data transmission and safe from a clinical standpoint. This paper proposes a smart Diabetic monitoring system using LoRaWAN with Dual Coordinate Descent Support Vector Machine (DCD-SVM) for Risk Aware and QoS based transmission control. The model dynamically classifies patient states and dynamically alters spreading factor, transmission power, packet interval and routing paths. Extensive NS-3/LoRaSim simulations have shown that the proposed DCD-SVM-AT framework provides a packet delivery ratio of 98.4%, average end-to-end delay is 140 ms, energy consumption is 2.1 J and network lifetime is 2730 s. In addition, it achieves a transmission reduction ratio of 43%, critical event detection latency of 4.6 seconds, and critical event missing rate of 1.1%, which is better than Static LoRaWAN, ADR, QoS-routing, LSTM-AT and XGBoost-AT schemes. These results confirm that lightweight edge intelligence and the combination with adaptive LoRaWAN communication is a promising and powerful method to boost reliability, efficiency and clinical responsiveness of large-scale diabetic monitoring systems.

title: A Neuro-Symbolic Expert System for Medical Diagnosis From Natural Language with Rule-Based Logic Engines
authors: Ndachimya Magaji Edward, Daniel Godwin, Fares Odeh, Aytuğ Sağlam, Moustafa Leila
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence into clinical diagnostic workflows is heavily impeded by the inherent “black box” nature of deep learning. Neural networks struggle to provide the epistemological transparency required for safe medical reasoning, whilst traditional rule-based expert systems fail to process unstructured, real-world patient data effectively. This paper proposes a novel Neuro-Symbolic architecture that physically decouples natural language perception from deterministic medical logic. This system is engineered specifically for low-resource clinical environments, such as those found in West Africa. Differentiating overlapping endemic febrile illnesses-specifically Malaria, Typhoid, Dengue, and Lassa Fever-presents a rigorous mathematical challenge due to near-identical initial clinical presentations and severe lack of advanced laboratory infrastructure. Our proposed three-tier architecture isolates a Large Language Model (LLM) strictly to an information extraction role. The neural layer parses unstructured clinical vignettes and outputs structured medical facts paired with semantic certainty factors. An integration middleware then validates these facts against a machine-readable JSON Knowledge Base grounded in peer-reviewed clinical parameters, significantly mitigating the risk of AI hallucinations. Finally, the structured data is processed by a custom Evidence-Weighted Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) inference engine. By applying MYCIN-style probabilistic mathematics, the engine calculates diagnostic certainty whilst simultaneously generating a real-time, auditable logical trace. Empirical evaluation on an out-of-distribution dataset demonstrates that whilst pure LLMs force opaque classifications, this framework successfully bounds diagnostic uncertainty, prioritising clinical safety and epistemological transparency over raw, unverified accuracy.

title: AI Image Classification Detection Brain Tumor using C-RNN
authors: Tina Victoria, Nandhini.V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This work presents an integrated system that combines automated brain tumor instance segmentation with AI-assisted clinical guidance using deep learning and generative models. The proposed framework employs a customized Mask R-CNN architecture with a ResNet-50 Feature Pyramid Network backbone to detect tumor regions from MRI scans and generate pixel-level masks, bounding boxes, and calibrated confidence scores. The system supports identification of multiple tumor instances within a single scan and incorporates uncertainty-aware filtering to reduce unreliable predictions. Uploaded MRI images are preprocessed and passed through the segmentation pipeline, and the outputs are visualized as overlay masks with annotated confidence values to enhance interpretability. Instead of using confidence as a clinical certainty indicator, the system applies it as a contextual parameter for generating educational medical guidance using the Gemini generative model. The generated responses describe commonly adopted treatment pathways such as surgical options, radiotherapy, chemotherapy, and supportive care while maintaining non-diagnostic and assistive intent.The framework is implemented through a Streamlit interface that enables model loading, real-time inference, visualization, and structured text generation in a unified workflow. The end-to-end pipeline operates within interactive latency suitable for demonstration and decision-support research environments. Overall, the system highlights the feasibility of combining instance segmentation, uncertainty-aware AI reasoning, and generative medical explanations to support medical imaging analysis, educational use, and future deployment-oriented clinical assistance tools.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Assisted Design of Active and Reactive Power Control for GFM Inverters with Various Grid Strengths
authors: Shyamal S. Chand, Arman Ali, Majid Farhangi, Maurizio Cirrincione, Branislav Hredzak
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This study proposes a technique for the optimal parameter selection of a virtual synchronous generator (VSG)-regulated grid-forming inverter through a twin delayed deep deterministic reinforcement learning (TD3-RL) agent, applicable to both strong and weak grid scenarios. The methodology utilizes voltage fluctuations at the point of common coupling (PCC) due to grid impedance and incorporates integral-time errors of both active and reactive power as observational inputs. The TD3-RL agent is designed to modify the inertia and damping coefficients of the active power controller, as well as the proportional-integral gains of the reactive power controller. The objective is to pinpoint the optimal parameter configuration that improves inverter response across a wide range of grid impedances. Since the policy provides the same controller parameters for all grid impedances, it can be seamlessly used in the existing VSG architecture without requiring a trained policy. The efficacy of this approach was evaluated against conventional empirical frequency-droop control and the standard VSG parameter calculation method across grids with different short-circuit ratios (SCR). The proposed RLVSG consistently showed enhanced performance under all tested conditions.

title: DeepVesselNet-Based Retinal Vessel Segmentation with Enhanced Preprocessing
authors: Adith Sreejey A S, Rochit L, Pranavaswaruban S, Karthik S, Saladi Saritha, Yepuganti Karuna
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation is a critical task in the diagnosis and treatment of ocular diseases such as diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and hypertension. Accurate segmentation of fine vascular structures enables early detection of pathological changes and supports quantitative retinal image analysis. In this study, we present an improved implementation of DeepVesselNet for robust vessel segmentation in fundus images. The framework is evaluated on benchmark retinal imaging datasets, including DRIVE and STARE, demonstrating consistent performance across different imaging conditions. Experimental results show a Dice Coefficient exceeding 0.80, with competitive Intersection over Union (IoU), sensitivity, and specificity metrics. The proposed system provides a scalable, accurate, and deployment-ready solution for retinal vessel analysis, making it a strong candidate for integration into real-world ophthalmic diagnostic workflows.

title: Design and Optimization of a High-Gain Multi-Input Multilevel Inverter for Harmonic Mitigation
authors: T. Jarin, D. Navin Sam, N. Senthilkumar, Gaydaa AlZohbi, B. Deepanraj
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: This work proposes a high-gain multi-input multilevel inverter based on switched diode capacitor voltage accumulator (SDCVA) topology for renewable energy and photovoltaic applications. The proposed configuration utilizes two identical or different low-voltage DC sources to create a higher DC-link voltage with reduced switch count, low voltage stress, and high efficiency. In this topology, an H-bridge inverter synthesizes a seven-level output voltage, and it is easy to increase the voltage levels by increasing the input sources. Multilevel inverters inherently have dominant lower-order harmonics; therefore, this paper implements selective harmonic elimination using metaheuristic optimization techniques. Three different metaheuristic methods have been investigated. Mathematical modeling and closed-loop stability are ensured by transfer-function analysis and Bode plots along with PI controller tuning. Performance evaluations show reduced voltage stress, low current ripple, high efficiency, and significant total harmonic distortion (THD) reduction. Among them, PSO has the minimum THD of 1.71% that satisfies the IEEE harmonic standard. The results confirm that the proposed SDCVA-based MI-MLI with optimization-based selective harmonic elimination (SHE) provides robustness, efficiency, and scalability for high-gain power conversion applications.

title: IoT-Based Solar Power Monitoring System
authors: Venkatesan M, Vasudevan D, Marthandan T, Gajalakshmi K, S Karthick, Karthikeyan A
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The solar power system is integrated with an IoTbased monitoring setup comprising ESP32 microcontrollers, current sensors, voltage sensors, temperature sensors, and solar panels to enable real-time monitoring and optimization of solar energy generation. ESP32 microcontrollers serve as core processing units, facilitating communication and data analysis. Current sensors monitor electrical flow, while voltage sensors track voltage levels within the system. Temperature sensors ensure optimal performance by monitoring component temperatures. Solar panels incorporate sensors to monitor sunlight intensity and efficiency. The system offers real-time monitoring, remote accessibility, and data analytics. Users can optimize energy production, detect faults, and schedule maintenance remotely. Data analytics provide insights into energy generation, consumption trends, and system efficiency, aiding informed decision-making. In summary, the proposed system provides a comprehensive solution for monitoring and optimizing solar power systems, contributing to sustainable energy management and maximizing solar energy utilization.

title: Backscatter Device-Aided Integrated Sensing and Communication: A Pareto Optimization Framework
authors: Yifan Zhang, Yu Bai, Shuhao Zeng, Riku Jäntti, Zheng Yan, Christos Masouros, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems potentially encounter significant performance degradation in densely obstructed urban and non-line-of-sight scenarios, thus limiting their effectiveness in practical deployments. To deal with these challenges, this paper proposes a backscatter device (BD)-assisted ISAC system, which leverages passive BDs naturally distributed in underlying environments for performance enhancement. Specifically, the additional reflective signal paths provided by these ambient devices are exploited to enhance sensing accuracy and communication reliability, respectively. In this system, we define the Pareto boundary characterizing the trade-off between sensing mutual information (SMI) and communication rates to provide fundamental insights for its design. To derive the boundary, we formulate a performance optimization problem within an orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) framework, by jointly optimizing time-frequency resource element (RE) allocation, transmit power management, and BD modulation decisions. To tackle the non-convexity of the problem, we decompose it into three subproblems, solved iteratively through a block coordinate descent (BCD) algorithm. Specifically, the RE subproblem is addressed using the successive convex approximation (SCA) method, the power subproblem is solved using an augmented Lagrangian combined water-filling method, and the BD modulation subproblem is tackled using semidefinite relaxation (SDR) methods. Additionally, we demonstrate the generality of the proposed system by showing its adaptability to bistatic ISAC scenarios and MIMO settings. Finally, extensive simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed system and its superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art ISAC schemes.

title: Next-Gen Mobile Forensics Tool: Timeline Reconstruction from Android Digital Evidence
authors: Supitha Pathirana, Rasan Dushmantha, Chamara Jayarathna, Behan Thilakarathna, Amila Senarathna, Ayesha Wijesooriya
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Rapid proliferation of Android devices has created a critical need for forensic tools capable of automated, largescale evidence analysis. Existing tools often operate in isolation, placing a heavy interpretive burden on investigators. This paper proposes a novel four-component mobile forensic pipeline that integrates machine learning and transformer-based correlation to automate the digital evidence lifecycle. The system implements a high-speed parallel acquisition engine coupled with an XGBoost classifier that achieves 80.12 % accuracy in log severity triage, outperforming keyword-only and Naive Bayes baselines by 16.7 and 8.9 percentage points respectively. To address data fragmentation, the pipeline performs cross-source timestamp normalisation and utilises transformer-based semantic embeddings to reconstruct chronological event chains within a hybrid MongoDB and Neo4j architecture. A dual-engine scoring system, combining behavioural analysis and deterministic rules, triages applications achieving a macro F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 7 4}$</tex>, surpassing single-engine alternatives. An air-gapped Large Language Model (LLM) generates forensic narratives to preserve the chain of custody. A hybrid decision matrix classifies reconstructed events, presenting results through a real-time analyst dashboard with full audit trails. Experimental evaluation on physical Android devices demonstrates measurable improvements in acquisition throughput and timeline reconstruction completeness compared to sequential baselines. This end-to-end pipeline contributes a reproducible, audit-transparent tool that addresses measurable gaps in current Android forensic workflows.

title: A Three-Phase AI Framework for Distributed Database Optimization
authors: Shatha Abdulkhaleq, Hayri Sever
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Distributed databases require intelligent mechanisms to manage data partitioning, replication, and anomaly monitoring under dynamic workloads. This paper proposes a three-phase AI-driven framework that jointly optimizes these tasks using production-scale telemetry from the Alibaba Cluster Trace v2022 dataset containing 244,628 microservice node profiles across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> temporal windows. In Phase 1, a Random Forest classifier predicts optimal data fragmentation levels <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2-8$</tex> shards) using workload risk modeling, achieving 97.88% classification accuracy with cross-validation stability <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma=0.0042$</tex>. Phase 2 introduces reinforcement learningbased adaptive replication control formulated as a Markov Decision Process, where a Q-learning agent learns optimal scaling policies and reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> state-action coverage with an exploit reward of 53.13. Phase 3 employs a temporal LSTM autoencoder to detect abnormal node behavior using integrated telemetry and replication signals, achieving AUC-ROC of 0.9344, F1-score of 0.897, and an anomaly detection rate of 86.82 %. By integrating workload-aware fragmentation, reinforcement-driven replication, and sequence-based anomaly detection within a unified pipeline, the proposed framework demonstrates that AI can effectively automate critical operational decisions in large-scale distributed database systems while maintaining high accuracy and robust temporal generalization.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Underwater Image Enhancement in Marine Infrastructure Inspection and Archaeological Exploration
authors: Bhanumathi M, Rathinapriya V, Anandha Mala G S
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Underwater imaging, which is used for marine infrastructure inspection and underwater archaeological exploration, is greatly affected by turbidity, the absorption of light depending on its wavelength, scattering, and also improper illumination. In order to overcome these obstacles, a hybrid deep learning framework is proposed in this paper that combines multi-scale convolutional feature extraction, attention-guided residual learning, and physics-aware color and illumination correction in a modular architecture. The proposed method differs from traditional methods in that it breaks down the enhancement into different specialized tasks, thus making it possible to have robust restoration even in different underwater environments. The architecture is highly versatile and can be trained on unpaired as well as paired datasets of underwater images, thanks to the domain-consistency refining module that preserves the structure even without the reference of ground-truth. In addition, approximate depth estimates as optional auxiliary input can be utilized to further enhance the color fidelity along with sensor-agnostic deployment. The experimental results on marine infrastructure and underwater archaeological datasets show that the proposed method is superior compared to traditional, CNN-based, attention-based, and diffusion-based enhancement methods in terms of UCIQE, Entropy, Edge Preservation Index (EPI), and CIEDE2000. Computational analysis validates the practicality of the deployment, with 22–28 FPS on edge devices and up to 35 FPS on GPU platforms being the result. The deployment comes with seamless integration into cloud and distributed data infrastructures which are necessary for large-scale inspection workflows.

title: Video Surveillance Model for Anomaly Detection and Alert Generation using Deep Learning
authors: Y. Surekha, G Lalitha Kumari, Thirandi Ramya, Paidimukkula Lavanya, Tatiparti Meghana, Valluru Divya Charitha
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: In today’s world, the number of accidents and crimes is rapidly increasing, which is a serious concern for the safety of the public. Unfortunately, most of these incidents go unnoticed or are reported only when someone witnesses them, which causes a significant delay. By the time authorities take action, the damage may already have occurred, reducing the chances of providing immediate help.After receiving the report, police generally reach the location, conduct an enquiry, and mainly rely on monitoring CCTV footage to understand what exactly happened. This traditional process is time-consuming, and valuable time is lost that could otherwise be utilized to save lives or catch criminals effectively.To solve this problem, we use deep learning techniques to automatically detect accidents or crimes using video surveillance footage. Our model continuously monitors CCTV footage, and if any suspicious or unusual activity occurs, it immediately generates an alert that is sent to the authorities.

title: Multi-Sensor Fusion With Transformer-based Deep Learning for Real-Time Fetal Health Monitoring
authors: V.Sharmila Packiavathy, KAnita Davamani
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Monitoring of fetal health is of great significance in pregnancy risk reduction in the mother and the baby. Even though cardiotocography (CTG) is a popular method, the interpretation of cardiotocography signals is rather subjective and inconsistent, and most of the available AI solutions are based on cardiotocography signals and do not use maternal physiological signals. This drawback lowers credibility in practical clinical practice. This paper presents Fusion-TransFHR, a multi-sensor fusion model with the help of deep learning based on Transformers to monitor the health of a fetus in real-time. The system incorporates the fetal heart rate, uterine contractions, fetal motion, the uterine EMG, and the most important maternal variables of ECG, oxygen saturation, and blood pressure. The modality-specific features are extracted by a dual-stream encoder, and a proposed Cross-Modality Fusion Attention and Temporal Transformer Encoder is able to extract long-range temporal dynamics, as well as more profound maternal-fetal physiological interactions. The experimental result of Fusion-TransFHR attains an accuracy level of 96.84 which compared to CNN, MLP and hybrid baseline for specifically identify the suspect and Pathological classes. The system is low-latency inferred with respect to providing real-time monitoring and scalable deployment whether in a clinical or a remote setting. Its design is consistent with the strategy in healthcare innovation in the future and demonstrates the relevance to SDG 3, SDG 9, and SDG 10 as it can enhance predictive fetal care and accessibility.

title: A Dynamic Edge-Cloud Intelligence Framework for Real-Time Abnormal Activity Detection using Vision-Language Reasoning, Super-Resolution Enhancement, and Reinforcement Learning Optimisation
authors: Gokula Krishnan R, Prabakaran J
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: With the rising number of surveillance cameras being rolled out in addition to the rising pressure on real time and monitoring systems, the problem of identification of abnormal activities is becoming an important concern. The article presents a paper about the edge-cloud video analytics system where a vision-language model, ESRGAN and DQN are used to identify abnormal actions with live video with great success and dynamically. The visual elements identified by any lightweight deep learning on edge devices are enhanced by ESRGAN so that resolutions are more precise and richer in detail of the image, making a person more aware of minor anomalies. The optimization of the distribution of edge-cloud computing resources and trade-off between computational load, latency, and the quality of the detection is performed dynamically by the use of a Deep Q-Network (DQN). Vision-language will provide semantic interpretation of visual events and they enhance robustness of complicated situations. Experimental testing demonstrates that there is a high-quality and rapid performance rate of detecting of 99.7%, which involves low latency in the dynamic settings. This model shows the potential of super-resolution, reinforcement learning and vision-language models of the smart and proactive video surveillance systems.

title: Iot Payments: Bridging the Gap Between Connectivity, Compliance and Commerce
authors: Boris Robev, Ioannis Patias
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Digital payments have become essential in contemporary society, making it imperative for individuals to adapt their habits and embrace these methods. Beyond offering greater security than cash-based transactions, cashless payment processing saves time and enables precise tracking of all completed transactions. The ongoing digital transformation of information technologies has also expanded the availability of digital payment services in rural areas. At the same time, these innovations are reshaping the service and product delivery models of financial and payment-oriented organizations. In this context, the development of the Internet of Things (IoT), which refers to the growing network of physical devices connected to the internet, is transforming how companies, consumers, and even entire cities communicate through interconnected devices. Many IoT applications additionally require integrated payment processing. As embedded IoT devices become part of everyday life, their role in facilitating payment transactions is increasingly evident. IoT-initiated payments (payments started by connected devices rather than humans) have the potential to provide efficient and secure transaction handling - whether between devices themselves or between devices and people. The abstraction of IoT-based payments introduces the need for complex integrations between IoT technologies and the payments domain, including communication networks, security and compliance requirements, data collection and management, and varying levels of autonomy (how much independence a device has to make decisions) within heterogeneous (varied and diverse) systems. This study examines the potential challenges and influencing factors associated with implementing IoT-based technological devices, focusing on methodologies, standards, and compliance considerations relevant to conventional payment processing systems.

title: Smart Indian Farmer Assistant: A Tri-Modal AI Framework for Precision Agriculture Using MobileNetV2, XGBoost, and Multimodal Language Models
authors: Varshan K., Vishnu Vatsan K. S., Arulselvan M., Kowsalya K.
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The agriculture sector of today is at a crossroads and has to tackle a tripartite challenge: delayed pathological diagnosis, empirical treatment, uncertainty regarding crops to be grown, and acute lack of available agronomic knowledge. In this scenario, this paper examines the "Comprehensive Smartphone-Based System" designed to serve as a Virtual Agricultural Scientist. The proposed technique is based on the integration of MobileNetV2 for offline disease detection, XGBoost for soil-parameter-based crop recommendation, and Gemini 1.5 Flash for reasoning-based validation. Experimental validation on a dataset of over 54,000 images shows a classification accuracy of 98.24% with an inference latency of only 26 milliseconds. At the same time, the XGBoost model is also able to predict the best crops with an accuracy of 99.65%. In addition, in situations where the confidence in models is not very high, the Gemini model provides opportune "second opinions," correcting errors or resolving ambiguous symptoms in 68% of such cases. Notably, these modules for diagnosis and consultation are designed to function in an offline manner, providing constant support to farmers in conditions of limited connectivity.

title: Do-It-Yourself Approach to Oscillating Hydropower
authors: Matej Sečnik, Marko Hočevar, Tone Godeša, Tadej Brodarič, Gašper Rak, Francesco Nascimben, Giovanna Cavazzini
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This work addresses the development of a hidden hydropower energy harvester for open streams, driven by the von Kármán flow instabilities. It uses a vertically oscillating cylinder, fully immersed in the flow, coupled to an axial-flux permanentmagnet generator and modular power electronics. A new design logic was introduced, that prioritizes component availability and manufacturability over efficiency. The entire system is designed to be built using readily available tools and parts in a hands-on or do-it-yourself way. Besides presenting the mechanical design of the energy harvester, in the present paper we focus on axial flux generator and electronics. The maximum generator efficiency was 58 % at a rotational frequency of 5 Hz. The developed power electronics integrate three-phase rectification, a buck converter, and a UPS module that combines Li-ion battery charging with a DC-DC boost stage to deliver a regulated 5 V USB output.

title: A Secure Adaptive Fractal Watermarking Scheme for Visual Authentication
authors: B Akshitha Yadav, Ch Rupa, K Sushmasri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The increasing demand for the sharing of digital images has further increased the concerns regarding the unauthorized replication and manipulation of images, thus requiring the development of advanced and adaptive image protection mechanisms. The traditional static watermarking techniques, such as neural network watermarking, blind image watermarking, DCT-based frequency domain watermarking, LWT-based watermarking, and CNN-based watermarking, involve the uniform embedding of static watermark information into images. These techniques have been known to face limitations in terms of adaptability, visibility, and robustness against manipulation attacks, thus making them less reliable for practical applications. In this context, a dynamic and fragile image watermarking system is proposed that combines fractal Sierpinski triangle patterns and YOLO-based object detection. The fractal pattern ensures the imperceptibility of the watermark embedding process while being extremely sensitive to even the slightest changes, thus making it possible for tamper detection and authentication. Object-aware embedding makes it possible to embed watermarks into meaningful regions of images, thus improving robustness, traceability, and ownership verification. The experimental results demonstrate the highest imperceptibility of up to 94.2% visual fidelity and confirm the superiority of the proposed technique over traditional static watermarking techniques in terms of adaptability, security, and resistance to manipulation attacks.

title: Design and Implementation of a High-Efficiency Transformer-Less Inverter for Low-Voltage Applications
authors: M.Pradeepa, S.Santhiya, V. Sri Abilesh, K.Muniraj
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: In recent power system research, transformer-less inverters are widely used applications in renewable and grid-connected systems for transmission and distribution processes. In previous inverter-based systems, these systems suffer from increased high-power losses, higher switching losses, and irregular voltage conversion, which reduce overall circuit efficiency. To overcome this limitation, a novel Adaptive Leakage Current Suppression Optimization (ALCSO) controller algorithm is proposed for a transformer-less inverter to improve power quality. The input source is solar, a primary renewable energy source for DC power generation, with a buck-and-boost converter in operation. The ALCSO algorithm continuously regulates the switching value, reduces common-mode voltage variations, and provides reliable power conversion. The transformer less inverter operates under specific conditions, using an optimized switching framework with proper voltage gain and current level. In the experiment output, summarized Rise Time (s) 0.018, Settling Time (s) 0.042, Overshoot (%) 3.2, Tracking Noise (V) 0.85 for reliable grid connected output. The result provides a reliable, compact, and key solution for low-voltage renewable grid-connected applications

title: Predictive Self-Healing for Multi-Agent LLM Pipelines Using Model Predictive Control
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Amit Kumar Padhy, Sandeep Shivam, Viswanathan Ranganathan, Arun Kumar Elengovan, Deepak Kole
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Multi-agent Large Language Model (LLM) systems are increasingly deployed for complex reasoning tasks, yet their reliability under dynamic conditions remains inadequately addressed. Existing fault tolerance mechanisms are predominantly reactive, responding to failures only after cascading effects have begun. This paper presents a predictive self-healing framework that applies Model Predictive Control (MPC) to multi-agent LLM orchestration. Our approach models the agent pipeline as a dynamical system, employs a Kalman filter for state estimation, and computes optimal control actions-including replica scaling, load redistribution, and circuit breaker activation-to maintain system stability proactively. Experimental evaluation on a threestage LLM pipeline demonstrates that our MPC-based controller achieves 96.8 % success rate under stress conditions, reduces P99 latency by 68 % compared to reactive approaches, and recovers from failures 73 % faster than Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler. Energy analysis shows a 22 % reduction in GPUhours consumed, yielding proportional carbon footprint savings at scale. These results indicate that control-theoretic methods are a promising direction for reliable and energy-efficient multi-agent LLM deployment.

title: Self-Adaptive Graph Analytics for Real-Time Fraud Detection in Transaction Networks
authors: R.Mahalakshmi, Saranya S, S.Thiruselvi, D. Kerana Hanirex, Sheela.K, S. Arunarani
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Financial fraud in large scale transaction networks has become more sophisticated, and traditional static and rule-based systems are not anymore adequate to deal with dynamic and evolving fraudulent behaviors. The issue is that conventional graph-based and machine learning models struggle with the changing topology in real-time financial data streams which must be adaptable to fast changes in topology and concept drift. This study proposes a Self-Adaptive Graph Analytics (SAGA) framework that is able to dynamically model transaction networks with temporal graph learning, reinforcement-driven topology adaptation and continuous online feedback. The goal is to increase accuracy in detecting fraud in near real-time and keep the inference time low. The methodology uses the temporal graph neural networks(TGNN), meta-learning based self adaptation and graph based anomaly scoring mechanism to evolve with the dynamics of transactions continuously. Experimental evaluation using Elliptic Bitcoin Transaction Dataset shows that SAGA achieves an F1 score of 0.90 and an AUC score of 0.96 and outperforms 5 state-of-the-art temporal and static graph baselines with an inference latency of less than 12 ms per transaction. The outcomes prove SAGA has the ability to effectively capture evolving fraud patterns, reduce false alarms, and enable real time operational deployment. This study concludes by stating that the adaptive graph analytics is a promising way towards scalable, transparent and resilient fraud detection in modern financial systems.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Solar Energy Production Forecasting: Explainable Artificial Intelligence Approach
authors: Faruk Kirmizizambak, Emre Biçek
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The production performance of photovoltaic systems depends not only on historical data but also directly on complex environmental dynamics such as temperature, solar radiation, wind, and cloud cover. In this study, a unique dataset was created by integrating production records from the Yulara Solar system in Australia with CAMS-based particulate matter measurements. Four different deep learning architectures were established: LSTM, GRU, Hybrid_CNN_LSTM and Hybrid_CNN_Transformer. All models were evaluated under the same input size and similar training strategies. WMAPE, MAE, RMSE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>, and MAPE metrics were used in the performance comparisons of the models. The results were evaluated according to the WMAPE metric. The most successful model in this study was the Hybrid_CNN_Transformer model. The highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> value was observed in the GRU model. This finding shows that model success can vary depending on the performance metric used. XAI methods were used in the evaluation of the results. It has been observed that intraday cyclical time variables, radiation-based inputs, and air pollution data have an impact on model decisions. The combined use of hybrid deep learning architectures and explainable artificial intelligence approaches demonstrates a supportive effect in terms of both accuracy and interpretability in solar energy forecasting.

title: Improved Variational Graph Autoencoder for Link Prediction on Real-World Telecom Graphs
authors: Mugdha Arun Sathe, Pooja Shyamsundar, Ojas Naik, Navrati Saxena
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Mobility management in emerging O-RAN deployments increasingly leverages predictive intelligence to reduce handover failures and improve radio resource utilization. Recent studies have shown that Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), particularly variational graph autoencoders (VGAEs), are well suited to link-prediction formulations of user-cell association forecasting. In this work, next-cell selection is cast as a link-prediction problem on a bipartite user-cell graph derived from a real-world Huawei RW deployment and enriched with edge features. The proposed attention-based VGAE achieves an accuracy of 0.9127 and an F1-score of 0.9190 on the 10k-UE RW graph, improving over a baseline accuracy of 0.80 and F1-score of 0.83, and thereby providing substantially more reliable next-cell predictions for proactive handover.

title: Deciphering Object Concepts: Hierarchical Cross-Modal Relational Reasoning for Mining Object-Attribute-Affordance Associations
authors: Yuxuan Wang, Muli Yang, Yihang Zhu, Jiexi Yan, Fen Fang, Xulei Yang, Cheng Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Object Concept Learning (OCL) aims to recognize high-level attributes and affordances of objects and to infer the causal relationships between them. The key is to accurately model the many-to-many mapping between objects and concepts: While an object may possess multiple concepts, a concept can also belong to multiple objects. Existing methods primarily rely on attention mechanisms to capture label correlations, which limits their ability to comprehend high-level concepts and to perform effective causal reasoning. Inspired by the human cognitive process of progressive understanding, a Hierarchical Cross-Modal Relational Reasoning (CORE) framework is proposed to enhance the understanding of object concepts through hierarchical interaction and reasoning between visual and textual modalities. Specifically, a coarse-to-fine relational reasoning module is developed, in which multi-step learnable prompts are employed to progressively localize the conceptual information of objects, thereby improving the accuracy of object-concept mapping. Subsequently, to facilitate the modeling of causal relationships between object attributes and affordances, a counterfactual reasoning mechanism is introduced. By constructing counterfactual samples and distinguishing the predictive outputs of factual and counterfactual parts, the model's ability to capture causality among concepts is enhanced. Significant performance gains and extensive visualization analysis demonstrate the superiority of our method.

title: Robustness of SAM: Segment Anything under Corruptions and beyond_supp1-3698837.pdf
authors: Yu Qiao
publish: N/A
abstract: Segment anything model (SAM) is notable for its ability to cut out any object and its impressive zero-shot transfer performance with prompt guidance. However, its robustness under corruption remains underexplored. Prior work indicates that SAM is biased towards texture rather than shape. We first evaluate its robustness against style transfer (synthetic corruption), followed by 15 common corruptions, and find that SAM demonstrates robustness in these scenarios. Moreover, we assess SAM's resilience to local patch occlusion and adversarial patch attacks, finding it resilient to occlusion but vulnerable to patch attacks. We observe that patch attacks disrupt the feature map and misguide the attention mechanism, whereas patch occlusion does not cause such misguidance. Given that visible patch attacks are easily detectable, we further investigate robustness against global adversarial attacks that remain imperceptible to humans by employing basic attacks and a new mask-aware loss. Our findings suggest that SAM's vulnerability primarily stems from its more reliance on high-frequency signals compared to low-frequency ones. Moreover, we further investigate the targeted transferability of adversarial examples and propose a contrastive learning-driven approach with a plug-and-play modulation module, enabling more effective transferability across different SAM models. In addition, we investigate a range of defense strategies to enhance the robustness of SAM, as well as stronger and more adaptive attack methods. Overall, this work provides a comprehensive study of SAM's robustness across diverse scenarios and is expected to offer valuable insights into its practical applicability and effectiveness in real-world settings.

title: Enhancing Depression Detection on Social Media with DSM-V Guided RAG and Fine-Tuned DeepSeek-R1_supp1-3699501.docx
authors: Shouliang Qi
publish: N/A
abstract: Depression significantly contributes to global disability, yet remains underdiagnosed due to lack of awareness. Social media platforms provide rich, real-time data reflecting users' emotional and psychological states, making them promising resources for automated depression detection. However, general-purpose Large Language Models (LLMs) often perform suboptimally in clinical domains, lacking interpretability and domain specificity. To address these limitations, this study introduces a knowledge-augmented framework combining Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) guided by clinical psychiatric criteria from the DSM-V with a fine-tuned DeepSeek-R1-7B model. We constructed a domain specific knowledge base comprising 329 expert-reviewed, retrieval-friendly DSM-V-derived entries. By retrieving relevant DSM-V-TR diagnostic criteria alongside user posts, our framework provides clinically grounded contextual information that may support human review by relating model predictions to established psychiatric criteria. Experiments on three balanced social media datasets showed that the DSM-V-guided RAG module, when combined with fine-tuned DeepSeek-R1-7B, achieved improved performance over the compared settings under the current experimental protocol. The retrieved DSM-V entries also provided clinically grounded contextual information that may help interpret model predictions. These findings suggest that DSM-V-guided knowledge augmentation is a promising direction for improving the transparency of benchmark-based depression detection on social media.

title: FlexPWL: A Flexible, Scalable, and Multiplier-Free Approach for Activation Functions on FPGA
authors: Ebrahim Fard, Janier Arias-Garcia, Hao Zhang, Seokbum Ko
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: The hardware implementation of nonlinear activation functions (AFs), such as the Sigmoid and Hyperbolic Tangent (Tanh), presents a significant bottleneck for deploying Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) on resource-constrained edge devices. Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs), as a leading platform for edge AI, face challenges in efficiently executing these functions due to their complex mathematical nature and limited computational resources. This paper proposes a novel, hardware-friendly piecewise linear (PWL) approximation method for implementing Sigmoid and Tanh functions. Our approach introduces new mathematical formulations for computing the y-intercept that significantly reduce Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Maximum Absolute Error (MXE) at no additional hardware cost. By consolidating the features of recent works, the proposed architecture eliminates a costly segment address encoder, supports pipelining for low-latency and high-frequency operation, and avoids the complexity and low precision of other methods. Moreover, it offers high configurability in bit width, number of segments, and input ranges, enabling adaptable deployment across diverse hardware and precision targets. A unified architecture is presented for both AFs, maintaining identical FPGA resource usage across functions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms prior state-of-the-art designs by up to 18.52× in accuracy, 437.29× in latency, and 3.71× in frequency, and achieves LUT and flip-flop savings of up to 11.63× and 12.42×, respectively.

title: Adaptive Downsampling and Refinement for Fast Iterative Closest Point
authors: Neha Bhatt, Zhaodong Zhou, Krunal Mehta, Jun Chen
publish: IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Letters
abstract: Iterative closest point (ICP) and its variants rep resent a fundamental technique for rigid registration between two point sets, with wide applications ranging from robotics to 3D reconstruction. The primary limitations of ICP include slow convergence due to its linear convergence rate, along with the per-iteration nearest-neighbor search, which scales linearly with source cloud size and dominates runtime on dense datasets. To address these issues, this paper proposes a modified ICP method that achieves fast convergence while maintaining robust alignment. Specifically, we introduce an adaptive threshold-based downsampling technique that selectively reduces the number of source points processed during early iterations, decreasing the computational cost for each iteration. This is followed by a full cloud refinement stage designed to recover alignment accuracy and mitigate any degradation introduced by the downsampling phase. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves notable reductions in computational time across the majority of benchmark datasets, including Stanford mesh models and LiDAR point clouds, while maintaining comparable registration accuracy relative to the baseline method.

title: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
authors: N/A
