title: MLGA-Mamba: Multiscale Locally Gated Attention and Dual-Stream Decoder Guided Vision Mamba for Change Detection of Fast-Growing Urban Regions
authors: Fei Song, Xiaolin Wei, Fei Luo, Daiyang Xiao, Tao Lei, Qiang Cheng, Jia Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Urban land cover and land use (LCLU) changes are a dynamic and frequently rapid process that serves as a constitutive element of urban development. Nevertheless, the precise identification of urban changing ground objects at different scales remains challenging in high-resolution (HR) remote sensing images because of the difficulties associated with effectively modeling discriminative features across temporal and spatial variations. In this paper, a novel multiscale locally gated attention (MLGA) and dual-stream decoder guided vision mamba (MLGA-Mamba) is proposed for change detection of fast-growing urban regions from HRremote sensing images. Specifically, the collaborative integration of the MLGA and multi-angle SSM as the encoder facilitates the large-scale spatial feature extraction and multiple-receptive field local details through a parallel multibranch design, while leveraging an adaptive learnable gating mechanism to suppress background noise interference. Simultaneously, the dual-stream task-specific decoder with prior knowledge (DSTSD) is derived to distinguish changes by constructing a multiscale semantic-aware decoder with spatial-temporal parallel and cross relationship modeling, and a difference-aware decoder with scene semantic guided (DAD). To dynamically facilitate the spatial-temporal interaction of DSTSD, DAD infers priors from invariant scene semantic features through top-K minima selection and feature clustering. Experiments are conducted on the prevalently utilized LEVIR-CD, CDD and SYSU-CD datasets with urban buildings and green spaces across various scale. Compared with other state-of-the-art methods, the MLGA-Mamba provides favorable performance in terms of accuracy and generalization capability.

title: Spectral Decomposition and Responsive Scaling: A Dual-Pattern Approach for Efficient Serverless Auto-Scaling
authors: Pengwei Wang, Lin Sun, Haoquan Qi, Yichen Zhong, Zhijun Ding, Shun Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Auto-scaling is a critical mechanism for adapting to dynamic workload fluctuations in serverless environments, ensuring that sufficient instances are available to handle incoming requests. However, existing approaches usually rely on fixed parameter configurations or single-scale prediction methods, which are inadequate for timely instance adjustments in response to workload variations. This limitation exacerbates the cold start problem while also causing either excessive resource allocation or inefficient utilization. In this study, we propose a FRequency-Adaptive Prediction (FRAP) method that utilizes Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) to perform spectral decomposition of request frequency into high-frequency and low-frequency patterns. The high-frequency component, representing bursty fluctuations, is predicted using an Improved Fourier Series Fitting (IFSF) method, whereas the low-frequency component, reflecting long-term workload trends, is predicted using the Holt-Winters (HW) method. This dual-pattern modeling enables accurate identification of intrinsic characteristics. Based on this, we design a three-layer responsiveness-aware scaling model that integrates Instantaneous Response (IR) Model, Comprehensive Prediction (CP) Model, and Long Term Adjustment (LTA) Model. By dynamically adjusting the weights assigned to each model, the proposed FRAP-based Dynamic Weighted Scaling (FRAP-DWS) strategy generates adaptive scaling decisions, thereby reducing the occurrence of cold starts. Experimental results on the Azure Functions dataset demonstrate that our method improves the quality-price ratio by at least 16.7% compared to SOTA approaches.

title: Impedance modeling and sensitivity analysis of fully differential inductive displacement sensor
authors: Jinghua Hu, Changlong Li, Zhenzhong Su, Jie Xu, Shibo Dong, Dong Wang, Wei Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Inductive displacement sensors are widely used in magnetic bearing control systems due to their simple structure, easy integration, and high cost-effectiveness. The frequency of the sensor operates above a few kHz, so non ideal factors such as iron core reluctance and eddy current effects cannot be ignored. However, existing studies do not fully consider non ideal factors, which limits the accuracy of the sensor impedance model. In this paper, the Schwarz–Christoffel transformation is used to compensate for stator-pole fringing effects, complex permeability is introduced to account for eddy-current and hysteresis effects, and a leakage coefficient is adopted to model flux leakage. An improved sensor impedance model and output characteristic model were established. An experimental platform is built to validate the proposed modeling method. The results show that the prediction error of sensitivity does not exceed 4% at different frequencies, verifying the correctness of the model. In addition, this article proposes a highly sensitive radial and axial integrated full bridge inductive displacement sensor, which has higher anti-interference ability and output sensitivity compared to the traditional half bridge differential circuit structure. Meanwhile, a quadrature phase-locked demodulation circuit based on analog switches has been developed, which has high excitation frequency capability and low cost.

title: Monocular Images based Freeboard Measurement of Inland Ships
authors: Tianlei Wang, Yan Fang, Hanming Zou, Dekang Liu, Jiuwen Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Freeboard measurement based on computer vision has significant practicality for the intelligent surveillance of shipping transportation. However, existing methods fail to adapt to this task due to the complex background of inland waterway and the irregular shape of ship side shell caused by the hull inclination. To address these issues, a novel visual measurement paradigm for the freeboard estimation of inland ships (ISFE) is proposed at the absolute scale of the scene from monocular images. ISFE consists of a pixel-level height estimation stage and a horizon line-based real-world height estimation procedure, requiring solely the record of the camera optical center height. Specifically, a deck and water line detector (DWLD) incorporating an enhanced Mask R-CNN and a novel unsupervised line regression is proposed for pixel height estimation. Subsequently, a data-driven horizon line detector is developed as an intermediary to establish the relationship between the real-world height and the monocular image. Experiments on a real-world freeboard measurement dataset are conducted and the results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed ISFE. The source code is available at https://github.com/TianleiWang/ISFE.

title: 6D Tip Wrench Estimation for Continuum Robots: A Koopman-UKF-Wrench Decomposition Approach_supp2-3697156.mp4
authors: Lingyun Zeng
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper presents a method for comprehensive 6D estimation of tip wrench for generally deflected static elastic rods. Current methods for load estimation are restricted to estimating lateral (point or distributed) forces for (quasi-)planar deformation; estimation of tangential force and moment, i.e., the full 6D wrench, remains largely unreliable due to the ill-posed nature of the problem. To address this challenge, this paper begins by proposing a high-fidelity static rod model that leverages Koopman Operator theory. Building on this model and utilizing shape feedback, a computationally efficient three-step wrench estimator is proposed: (i) a Koopman-UKF local moment observer, (ii) a static equilibrium solver, and (iii) a rod model propagator. Then, a 2D wrench screw system, identified as the insensible wrench in the initial estimation, elucidates error sources and informs strategies to enhance accuracy by incorporating additional feedback, such as the rod tip material frame and base axial force. Ultimately, the framework delivers accurate 6D tip wrench estimation with quantified uncertainty. Simulation and experimental evaluations validate its effectiveness, demonstrating mean errors of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$53.14\,$</tex-math></inline-formula>mN (1.94%) and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.65\,$</tex-math></inline-formula>mNm (7.18%) for a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$159\,$</tex-math></inline-formula>mm-long Nitinol tube undergoing complex out-of-plane deformations, outperforming three replicated state-of-the-art methods. Additionally, its applicability to more complex continuum robots is demonstrated through load estimation on a Parallel Continuum Robot.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Compact, MR-Compatible Origami Continuum Robot with Variable Length_supp1-3699218.mp4
authors: Qiqiang HU
publish: N/A
abstract: Continuum robots have recently gained attention for their great value in medical applications. This paper presents a novel design approach for MR-compatible medical continuum robots with the ability to variable length and controllable motion at compact size and light weight. The robot employs a small-scale origami continuum structure with an outside diameter of 15 mm, is driven by three tendon-shape memory alloy (SMA) actuators, and senses the motion of actuators by a thin linear potentiometer (Thinpot). The robot system is lightweight at 76.7 g and compact, with the proximal sensing and actuation module measuring only 34 mm in diameter and 90 mm in length. It is first demonstrated that the Thinpot is insensitive to magnetic disturbance and MR-compatible and can realize accurate feedback control of the large stroke of SMA actuators, even in a real 1.5T MR environment. Further, the bending angle and tip position of the robot can be controlled based on an inverse kinematic model. The robots equipped with different tools will show versatility in biomedical applications, such as endoscopy and needle insertion.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Enabled Persistent Surveillance with Energy-Aware UAV-UGV Systems for Disaster Management Applications_supp5-3696943.mp4
authors: Md Safwan Mondal
publish: N/A
abstract: Integrating Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) with Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) provides an effective solution for persistent surveillance in disaster management. UAVs excel at covering large areas rapidly, but their range is limited by battery capacity. UGVs, though slower, can carry larger batteries for extended missions. By using UGVs as mobile recharging stations, UAVs can extend mission duration through periodic refueling, leveraging the complementary strengths of both systems. To optimize this energy-aware UAV-UGV cooperative routing problem, we propose a planning framework that determines optimal routes and recharging points between a UAV and a UGV. Our solution employs a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework built on an encoder-decoder transformer architecture with multi-head attention mechanisms. This architecture enables the model to sequentially select actions for visiting mission points and coordinating recharging rendezvous between the UAV and UGV. The DRL model is trained to minimize the age periods (the time gap between consecutive visits) of mission points, ensuring effective surveillance. We evaluate the framework across various problem sizes and distributions, comparing its performance against heuristic methods and an existing learning-based model. Results show that our approach consistently outperforms these baselines in both solution quality and runtime. Additionally, we demonstrate the DRL policy's applicability in a real-world disaster scenario as a case study and explore its potential for online mission planning to handle dynamic changes. Adapting the DRL policy for priority-driven surveillance highlights the model's generalizability for real-time disaster response. More details are available on our website: https://sites.google.com/view/ecucps.

title: A Millimeter-Wave Notched Mirror-Grid Dielectric Waveguide Resonator Antenna
authors: Wenke Jiang, Guanghui Xu, Wei Wang, Yanbin Luo, Xianlong Lu, Zhixiang Huang, Xianliang Wu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this paper, a millimeter-wave (mm-wave) notched mirror-grid dielectric waveguide resonator antenna (GDWRA) is presented. The proposed antenna consists of a grid dielectric waveguide resonator (GDWR) with a notched mirror-line dielectric waveguide structure, and a differential feeding network. Due to notched mirror-line dielectric waveguide structure, electromagnetic fields are effectively confined by surrounding metallic walls for high gain. Simulations and measurements show that the antenna achieves a -10 dB impedance bandwidth of 11.6% (26.9-30.2 GHz) with a peak gain of 11.3 dBi. The overall profile height is only 0.12λ₀. With its integrated layout, low profile, and high gain, the proposed antenna is suited for mm-wave integrated system applications.

title: Super-Aligned Carbon Nanotube-Based Resistor Array for High-Speed and Polarization Infrared Scene Simulation_supp1-3694063.avi
authors: Yang Zhao
publish: N/A
abstract: The resistor array generates infrared radiation by Joule heating to simulate complex infrared scenes, helping evaluate the performance of the infrared imaging system. However, it remains challenging to simultaneously achieving resistor arrays with high-speed response and polarized emission. Here, we present a super-aligned carbon nanotube (SA-CNT) based RA, leveraging its ultralow thermal mass for high-speed response and intrinsic optical anisotropy for polarized emission without external optics. We fabricated a RA comprising <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times 15$</tex-math> </inline-formula> pixels that exhibits both high-speed thermal response and polarization-dependent emission. Scaling predicts that reducing the pixel pitch to the state-of-the-art <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$46~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m could enable sub-2 ms response time and frame rates exceeding 500 Hz, which remains a projection, as thermal dynamics are not strictly linear under scaling. Polarization measurements reveal pronounced anisotropic thermal emission consistent with Malus’s law, yielding a polarization radiance ratio of 1.6. Our approach opens up new ways for next-generation infrared scene simulation system. [2026-0040]

title: Journal of Microelectromechanical Systems Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: Journal of Microelectromechanical Systems

title: Effects of ultrafast phase modulation on dynamics and stability criteria of passive mode-locking
authors: Xuecheng Wu, Dongyuan Li, Menghao Fang, Fengqiu Wang
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: Dispersion or chirping, plays an important role in stabilizing optical solitons as found in mode-locked lasers. However, the effect of a transient chirping on passive mode-locking has rarely been investigated. Here, we demonstrate that an intracavity electro-optic phase modulation (PM) operating about a high-order harmonic frequency of the cavity can cause deterministic and reproducible fluctuation dynamics. Specifically, the offset frequency Δf = |f<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PM</sub> − N f<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rep</sub>| synthesizes a slow periodic driving force, yielding soliton fluctuations whose frequency is locked to the detuning Δf. When Δf is increased, the soliton response evolves from breathing to wavelength oscillation. More interestingly, for the on-resonance case where Δf ~ 0, we observe a clear stability threshold for the cavity solitons, as set by the strength of the transient chirp exerted by the phase modulation. Beyond the threshold RF drive power of 14 dBm, passive mode-locking is no longer sustained, and the laser exhibits signatures of active mode-locking, as evidenced by changes in both RF and optical spectra. Our results provide new routes for active control of soliton dynamics and may provide new insights into understanding the balancing between different physical factors in mode-locking.

title: Adaptive Duty-Ratio-Modulated Direct Torque Control Based on Extended Virtual Voltage Vectors for PMSM Drives
authors: Yiwei Hao, Gaolin Wang, Jiayi Zhao, Guoqiang Zhang, Qiwei Wang, Li Ding, Guangdong Bi, Dianguo Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Duty-ratio-modulated direct torque control (DDTC) suffers from the limited phase resolution of voltage vectors and strong sensitivity to motor parameter variations, degrading its torque and flux control performance for permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives. To address the issue, an adaptive duty-ratio-modulated direct torque control strategy based on extended virtual voltage vectors is proposed. By analyzing the coupling effects of the duty ratio on torque and flux, an efficient vector combination mechanism is developed, in which the torque deadbeat response and the flux ripple are adopted as optimization criteria. This method dynamically extends virtual voltage vectors with continuously adjustable phases, achieving coordinated optimization of the output torque and flux. To overcome the dependency of duty-ratio calculations on accurate motor parameters, the torque production strategy is investigated to vectorially project torque control errors. Leveraging a voltage-vector phase-mapping scheme, the torque slope undergoes an equivalent linear correction and is integrated into the duty-ratio model to achieve adaptive regulation, thereby effectively suppressing torque ripples and enhancing the robustness. Experimental results on a 2.2-kW PMSM drive platform validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: An OMO Learning Analytics Workflow Linking Facial-Expression-Based Emotions to Learning Outcomes
authors: Yi-Wen Liao, Yen-Cheng Tseng, Chuan-Sheng Lin, Ya-Han Liang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: Understanding learners' emotions during technology-mediated instruction is important for timely learning analytics and instructional support, especially in Online-Merge-Offline (OMO) learning contexts where instructors may struggle to interpret learners' states in real time. This paper investigates the relationship between learning emotions and learning effectiveness by integrating an AI-supported course platform with an emotion recognition workflow. In a 2024 artificial intelligence course at a technology university in southern Taiwan, learners' facial-expression evidence was captured during video-based learning activities and used to infer emotion categories. We analyzed emotion-change dynamics using derived variables (e.g., frequency and related measures) and examined their associations with assessment outcomes. Correlation analysis shows that the emotion-change measures are internally consistent but do not exhibit significant linear associations with learning outcomes in this dataset. In contrast, outcome improvement patterns differ across inferred emotion categories, providing a complementary category-level view of learning performance. These findings suggest that emotion categories may offer more distinguishable evidence for learning analytics than emotion-change frequency measures alone in the studied OMO context.

title: Modeling Electric Vehicle Adoption at the ZIP Code Level: A Bass Diffusion and Machine Learning Approach
authors: Angelina Li, Zuzhao Ye, Nanpeng Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: With the gradual transition from internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles to electric vehicles (EVs), accurate forecasts of EV adoption are essential for planning local charging infrastructure and assessing impacts on the power grid. However, most existing forecasts are conducted at national or state levels, which lack the spatial granularity needed to guide local power system upgrades. Moreover, constructing reliable models for EV adoption is challenging in areas without sufficient historical data. In this study, we analyze New York State's EV registration data using the Bass Diffusion Model and estimate the model parameters, including innovation, imitation, and market potential, at the ZIP code level. We then model how demographic, socioeconomic, and infrastructural characteristics influence these parameters using multiple linear regression and neural networks, enabling the estimation of Bass model parameters for regions with limited EV history. The proposed framework achieves high predictive accuracy and can be applied to other regions to generate fine-grained EV adoption forecasts for grid planning and infrastructure investment.

title: Interference-Aware Complex Phasor Message Passing Network for Joint Beamforming Optimization in Multi-User IRS-Assisted 6G Systems
authors: Mustafa Ihsan Mustafa, Osman Nuri Ucan
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper proposes an Interference-Aware Complex Phasor Message Passing Network (CPMPN) for joint transmit beamforming and IRS phase shift optimization in multi-user IRS-assisted wireless systems. The main novelty lies in a complex-valued graph representation that preserves amplitude and phase information, an interference-aware message passing mechanism for adaptive channel interaction weighting, and a feasibility-preserving phase output layer that guarantees valid IRS configurations while jointly predicting transmit beamforming vectors and IRS phase shifts. Evaluations using the DeepMIMO ray-tracing dataset show that the proposed CPMPN achieves an average achievable sum rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$26.3 \text{bps} / \text{Hz}$</tex>, outperforming deep unfolding, Transformerbased beamforming, and graph neural network methods. The model also approaches the semidefinite relaxation upper bound with an optimality gap of approximately 3 %, while enabling low-complexity single-pass inference, demonstrating strong performance and scalability for practical 6G IRS systems.

title: An Integrated Fuzzy-Entropy and Fuzzy-Improved-MULTIMOORA Approach for the Selection of Energy Storage Technologies
authors: Moshood Akanni Alao, Olawale Popoola
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: The integration of variable renewable energy sources into power systems necessitates the deployment of sustainable energy storage technologies (ESTs). However, selecting the optimal EST is complex due to multiple, often conflicting, technical, economic, environmental, and social criteria, compounded by inherent data uncertainties. To address this challenge, this paper proposes integrated multicriteria decision-making (MCDM) framework. The model combines Fuzzy-Entropy for objective criteria weighting with an improved Fuzzy-MULTIMOORA method, which incorporates a unified aggregation score to robustly rank alternatives under fuzziness. The approach is applied to a case study in South Africa, evaluating twelve ESTs against fourteen critical criteria. The results demonstrate that Pumped Hydro Storage (PHS) is the most suitable technology, followed by Compressed Air Energy Storage (CAES) and Hydrogen Storage, while Sodium Sulphur (NaS) battery ranks the least. This study validates the proposed methodology as an effective tool for decision-makers in planning sustainable energy infrastructure, balancing multifaceted requirements and uncertainty for long-term grid flexibility and stability.

title: Exploration and Practice of AI-Based Full Lifecycle Management for Teaching Equipment
authors: Ren Lu, Tong Ming
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: With the deepening advancement of educational digitalization strategies, university Teaching equipment management is transitioning from "construction-focused" to "operation-focused". This study focuses on integrating artificial intelligence (AI) with equipment full lifecycle management (PLM). Recent research progress and evolution paths of AI-empowered equipment operation management are systematically reviewed. A framework deeply integrating AI and PLM for intelligent equipment operation is proposed. This framework centers on two dimensions: the Artificial Intelligence for Operations (AIOps) and full-process control covering equipment planning, procurement, operational use, maintenance, and disposal. Practice demonstrates superior performance compared to traditional approaches and basic AI-assisted solutions. Specifically, in high-load scenarios, the proposed AIOps+PLM integration model enhances operational and maintenance efficiency by approximately 75.5% and 50.1%, respectively. Economically, a full lifecycle cost analysis on multimedia teaching equipment reveals cost reduction rates of 30.4% and 21.2% against the traditional and AI-assisted modes, respectively. Enhanced efficiency in university teaching equipment asset management and teaching quality assurance is thereby achieved.

title: A Structured Checkpoint Framework for Early Integration and Validation of Schedule and Cost Baselines in Construction Planning
authors: Taleb Alnajar, Qais Amarkhil, Anwar Alroomi, Sami Maalouf
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Schedule delays and cost overruns in construction projects are often identified during the execution phase, when corrective actions become costly and disruptive. This study develops a structured checkpoint framework to validate cost and schedule baselines and align them with the project scope during the planning phase, supporting earlier detection of misalignments. A qualitative exploratory approach was used, integrating standards-based analysis with a two-round Delphi evaluation by six construction experts. Drawing from literature and the U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO) Schedule and Cost Assessment Guides and the Project Management Institute's (PMI) PMBOK® Guide, 125 criteria were extracted, of which experts validated 81 checkpoints organized into three categories: (1) Schedule Validation, (2) Cost Validation, and (3) Integration Validation linking scope, schedule, and cost. The ten critical (highest-ranking) integration checkpoints were identified using the Relative Importance Index, with results emphasizing the central role of the work breakdown structure in cross-baseline alignment. The framework provides measurable baseline-validation criteria that can be used by future automated or AI-assisted tools for baseline verification and are expected to reduce rework and resource waste in construction planning.

title: Enhanced YOLOv8n for Vehicle and Road Object Detection
authors: Atharv Milind Pujari, Om Anant Kulkarni, Ojas Kumar, Samarth Sunil Deshpande, Bhavna Chandramani Julme
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Real-time object detection is a crucial task in Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS). The detection becomes increasingly difficult in complex road conditions, which may involve dense traffic, multiple overlapping vehicles, and miscellaneous road objects. These complex traffic conditions are regularly observed on Indian roads, which consist of a combination of different traffic scenarios. This paper presents a modified YOLOv8n model for accurate vehicle and road object detection in complex road conditions. Special attention mechanism such as Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) is integrated into the bottleneck and Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN)-inspired attention mechanism is integrated into the Concat layer of the YOLOv8n model. The modified YOLOv8n outperforms the baseline YOLOv8n model and achieves an mAP@0.5 score of 81.7% and an mAP@0.5:0.95 score of 66.67%. The modified YOLOv8n shows a 1.7% improvement compared to the original YOLOv8n model. The modified YOLOv8n shows high potential for integration into real-time vehicle detection systems. It shows consistent performance across varying traffic scenarios and unstructured road conditions, commonly observed in Indian traffic conditions.

title: AI-Driven Real-Time Intrusion Detection and Automated Mitigation for Industrial IoT Process Control Sensors
authors: Moulishwaran A, Visumathi J
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This research proposes an Optimized XGBoost-based Intrusion Detection System, O-XGB IDS, to provide improved accuracy, reduce latency, and guarantee scalability in diverse IoT environments. The developed model integrates Bayesian optimization of hyperparameters along with adaptive feature selection to improve the classification performance. For this, experimental validation is conducted on multiple benchmark datasets like IoT-23, RT-IoT2022, and MQTT-IoT-IDS2020 by comparing different machine learning models such as Decision Tree, Random Forest, SVM, and Logistic Regression. These results demonstrate that the proposed O-XGB achieved the highest accuracy in intrusion detection of 98.93%, precision of 98.40%, and a 29% faster response time compared with other ensemble-based models. It has been confirmed that the optimized XGBoost framework furnishes a robust, low-latency, highly reliable defense mechanism for real-time IoT cybersecurity applications, opening up new research dimensions toward intelligent and adaptive intrusion detection systems.

title: A Smart Healthcare Solution for Medication Adherence and Expiry Monitoring
authors: Nandhana P N, Tephila Praisy B, Keerthika S, L.Kalaivani
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The issue of non-adherence to medication and the unintentional use of expired medications is of great concern, particularly with patients with chronic conditions and elderly people. Such issues frequently occur because of the loss of memory, difficult drug regimes, and the absence of effective check-up measures. This paper is devoted to designing and developing a smart medicine organizer, which has an expiry tracking system to guarantee that patients take their medicine on time and ensure patient safety. The designed system involves a 3D-printed medicine organizer, a control unit, which is made on the basis of the ESP32 microcontroller, and alerting and dispensing mechanisms. The organizer automatically stores and tracks medicine expiry dates and schedules drug intake by using a real-time clock module. The system notifies the user when the time to take medicine comes and dispenses the required tablet into a receiving plate, to remind the patient to take medication on time. A detection mechanism is based on a Hall sensor that measures the intake of medicine and the condition of compartments, and the visual and audible indicators are performed by a display unit, LED indicators, and a buzzer. The system is user-friendly, affordable and energy saving, which is why it is applicable in home healthcare. Through experimentation, it is found that the proposed organizer will dramatically decrease overall human intervention, eliminate the use of expired medicines and increase medication compliance. Hence, the created system is a powerful solution to the assistance of the elderly and chronically ill patients that will lead to safer and more credible medication management.

title: Early Detection of Parkinson's Disease via Spiral Drawings and MRI Images with Deep Transfer Learning and Genetic Feature Optimization
authors: Pappula Sarala, Chilukuri Neethu Reddy, Mohammad Asad, Mekapothula Navya Sri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Parkinson’s disease (PD) alters both structure and function of the brain making it harder to diagnose the condition. Because of this, one of the biggest challenges facing doctors is diagnosing Parkinson’s disease early. This paper discusses a novel, non-invasive, interpretive, computer-aided diagnostic technique designed to help clinicians detect Parkinson’s disease in its early stages using brain MRIs scan images and hand-drawn spiral designs. The system uses deep transfer learning models, including VGG19, ResNet50, and InceptionV3, to analyse spiral drawings for motor patterns and brain MRIs for Parkinson’s disease. In this work, a genetic algorithm (GA) enhances feature selection, minimizes overlap, and improves classification. The optimal characteristics for trustworthy ensemble classification and prediction are categorized by Support Vector Machines, Random Forest, and k-NN algorithms. A soft voting mechanism is used to combine the results to achieve high accuracy. LIME is used to show the clinical model’s predictions in a way that makes them transparent and reliable. Early disease detection is possible using the suggested model. This demonstrates that the model can be utilized to assist the medical professionals to enhance patients’ wellbeing.

title: Intelligent Detection of Bank Transaction Fraud Using Multilayer Perceptron Model
authors: Vadivel M, Murugan S, Ayalapogu Ratna Raju, Mohan Krishna Seerla, Nirmala G, Manimaran Naghapushanam
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The emergence of digital banking has heightened the frequency and intricacy of fraudulent transactions, presenting substantial dangers to financial institutions and consumers. This research introduces an advanced fraud detection system for LOL Bank Pvt. Ltd. using a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) model. The system uses an extensive dataset of banking transactions, including client information, transaction metadata, merchant details, account balances, and device characteristics. Feature engineering methods were used to extract significant patterns, allowing the MLP to discern nuanced and complex signs of fraudulent activity. The proposed MLP model, characterized by many hidden layers and ReLU activation, has exceptional efficacy in identifying fraudulent transactions. Experimental assessment indicates an accuracy of 99.53 %, precision of 99.47 %, recall of 99.60 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 99.53 %, demonstrating the model's proficiency in successfully identifying fraud while reducing false positives. The system processes transactions in real time, delivering actionable notifications to bank personnel and assisting in the prevention of financial losses. The findings underscore the efficacy of MLPbased methodologies in safeguarding digital banking operations by identifying non-linear and complex patterns in transaction data, consequently bolstering consumer confidence and operational efficiency.

title: A Computer Vision Approach for Identifying Chemically Ripened Fruits to Ensure Public Health
authors: Deepa Deepa, Pratheeka KG, Shaikh Saniya Ameer, Siana Rebello
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Accelerating banana ripening with calcium carbide carries health risks tied to toxic residues. The chemical is usually sprayed during transport and storage to hasten the yellowing process. However, checking the safety of large batches of fruit the old-fashioned way is still tricky, since hand-held assays tend to give variable readings. We introduce a non-invasive, image-based technique to automate the detection of artificially ripened bananas. Our system leverages deep learning to spot subtle variations in fruit color and texture. Preprocessed images are first fed into a hybrid feature extractor that combines two pretrained convolutional networks, and the resulting feature maps are passed to an ensemble of machine learning classifiers. The final prediction is the averaged output of these classifiers. We evaluated the approach on a curated dataset of bananas labeled to indicate whether ripening was induced or natural. Performance remained strong even when the ripening technique left only tiny, hard-to-see traces. The research demonstrates a scalable way to enhance food safety without requiring manual sample testing or hazardous reagents. Its role is to track market trends and to equip consumers with the information they need to make informed choices.

title: An End-To-End on-Prem Vulnerability Assessment Framework Using Hybrid CPE Matching and Structured Mitigation Generation
authors: Griventh Griffith Agustin, Fatih Achmad Al-Haritz, Yohan Muliono
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Vulnerability scanners frequently produce findings that are difficult to operationalize due to inconsistent local software identifiers and the absence of actionable remediation guidance. This paper presents an end-to-end onpremises vulnerability assessment framework that performs local software inventory collection, correlates entries to standardized Common Platform Enumeration (CPE) identifiers via a hybrid matching approach, and generates structured mitigation guidance for direct operational use. The hybrid matcher combines trigram-based candidate retrieval with semantic selection using a self-hosted Large Language Model (LLM), ensuring sensitive asset data remains within the organization boundary. A consensus-based caching mechanism reduces repeated inference costs over successive scans. Mitigation output is constrained to JSON format with schema validation and fallback handling to ensure UI stability. Experiments show a 93% reduction in scan latency after cache warm-up and a 95 % JSON validity rate, supporting the practicality of on-premises LLM-assisted vulnerability management in privacy-sensitive environments.

title: A Deep Q-Network-Based Opportunistic Routing Protocol for Energy-Efficient Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: S. Sivasakthi Selvan, Khush Parikh, Rutajit Laskar, Baranidharan B
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) require efficient routing mechanisms to ensure reliable data delivery under strict energy constraints. Opportunistic routing improves transmission reliability by exploiting broadcast characteristics; however, existing reinforcement learningbased approaches primarily rely on tabular Q-learning, which suffers from poor scalability, slow convergence, and limited generalization in dynamic network environments. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a Deep Q Network (DQN) based Opportunistic Routing protocol for WSNs. The proposed approach replaces traditional Q-tables with a neural network-based value approximation, enabling continuous state representation and adaptive next-hop selection. The routing decision considers residual energy, packet delivery probability, hop distance, and congestion level to balance reliability and energy efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed DQN-based routing protocol achieves a higher packet delivery ratio, lower energy consumption, reduced end-to-end delay, and fewer hop counts compared to conventional routing protocols and Q-learning-based methods. These results validate the effectiveness of deep reinforcement learning in improving routing performance and scalability in wireless sensor networks.

title: An Intelligent Decision Support System Using Explainable Machine Learning for Sustainable Treatment of Liquid Household Hazardous Waste
authors: Abhik Raj, R. Lavanya, Spandan Choubey
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Liquid hazardous household waste represents a significant challenge for both environmental protection and public health due to improper handling, storage, and disposal practices. Such waste exhibits complex physicochemical properties that make classification and treatment selection difficult using traditional manual approaches. Existing waste management systems largely rely on predefined regulatory rules and expert judgment, lacking automation, scalability, and interpretability. This paper proposes an explainable artificial intelligence-based framework for liquid hazardous household waste classification, treatment recommendation, and risk assessment. The framework integrates domain-informed data generation aligned with chemical safety standards, multiparameter feature modeling, machine learning-driven classification, SHAP-based explainability, hazard scoring, risk categorization, and interactive visualization. A Random Forest classifier is employed to predict appropriate treatment strategies based on key liquid properties including pH level, flammability, toxicity, reactivity, corrosiveness, viscosity, and container integrity. A comparative evaluation with other machine learning models is conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. To enhance transparency, SHAP provides both global and instance-level explanations, enabling interpretable decision-making in safetycritical environmental applications. Additionally, a quantitative hazard scoring mechanism is introduced to assess severity and classify waste into low, moderate, high, and critical risk levels. Experimental results, obtained using cross-validation, demonstrate an accuracy of 97.92 percent while maintaining strong interpretability. The framework further incorporates visualization techniques such as hazard profiles, radar charts, correlation heatmaps, and SHAP plots to improve analytical understanding. Overall, the proposed system provides an intelligent, interpretable, and scalable solution for liquid hazardous waste management with applications in environmental monitoring and regulatory decision support.

title: Predictive Task Scheduling for Latency Minimization in Vehicular Fog Computing using Optimized Deep Belief Learning
authors: Naveen Kulkarni, Arunkumar Gopu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The Vehicular Fog Computing (VFC) system provides an innovative solution which enables ITS applications to achieve their required latency performance. The combination of fast vehicle movement and network environment fluctuations creates difficulties for scheduling tasks efficiently because reactive scheduling methods result in longer task completion times and more failed tasks. The research presents Optimized Deep Belief Learning (ODBL) as a predictive task scheduling system which will reduce VFC environment latency by addressing present system limitations. This paper proposes to select vehicular fog nodes through analysis of historical vehicle tracking data and wireless signal power and available resources at fog nodes which determine their processing abilities. The ODBL system operates differently from traditional schedulers which use heuristic and reinforcement learning methods to make decisions based on current network conditions because it allows predictive scheduling that maintains node availability until task completion. The simulation framework contains complete models which show vehicle movement and network behavior to evaluate the scheduler performance. The proposed ODBL-based scheduler produces better results than current baseline methods according to experimental data which shows a maximum boost in task completion success rate and decrease in average latency and enhanced Quality of Service (QoS) performance during high mobility scenarios. The research results show that predictive learning-based scheduling methods produce the best possible solution for VFC systems which require minimal latency in their operations.

title: Impact of High vs. Low Agile Process Maturity on Cross-Functional Collaboration and Project Success in Enterprise Software Development
authors: Velmurugan S, Sundaram K Meenakshi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Large scale systems, remote teams, long software development lifecycle and long term maintenance are increasingly common in enterprise software development. Agile methodologies are popular in such environment to increase flexibility and speed of delivering results but, the level of Agile process maturity may vary a lot, which may impact the effectiveness of collaboration and success of a project. Agile implementations with a low maturity focus on rapid delivery and autonomy, but do not necessarily provide any form of coordination, role definition and dependency management in more complex enterprise scenarios. Conversely, agile environments with high maturity have set processes, governance systems and cross-functional integration practices that facilitate alignment and the reliability of delivery. This paper constitutes an empirical research on the relationship between high and low Agile process maturity and cross-functional teamwork and project success in building enterprise software. The data at the project level is based on massive enterprise teams and is analyzed using project-level data to measure collaboration effectiveness, predictability of delivery, quality results, and stakeholder satisfaction. Quantitative analysis indicates that increased Agile maturity correlates with increased cross-team coordination, less rework and less variability in project outcomes whereas lower maturity practices achieve more short-term benefits and greater collaboration efficiency and delivery performance variability. The results show that Agile process maturity is a key factor of sustainable project success in enterprise software setting.

title: Measuring Cloud Misconfigurations in the Wild: An Empirical Multi-Cloud Study
authors: Akshitha Segireddy, Rahul Karne, Akhil Joshi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Misconfigured clouds continue to be one of the most common ways security breaches happen in public cloud environments; however, the true extent of misconfigurations is very much misunderstood. Most studies have been confined to single providers, synthetic configurations, or snapshots that do not account for how misconfigurations develop and mature through time. This paper will introduce the world’s first longitudinal observatory of internet-scale cloud misconfigurations, which covers all major cloud providers. This paper is designed and implemented a provider-agnostic measurement framework that will identify exposed storage, identity, compute, and service configuration(s), observable from the public internet, and monitor their lifecycle over multiple months. Through our study of millions of resources hosted on cloud infrastructure, we found that a significant portion of the misconfigurations studied persisted for extended periods, frequently reappeared after remediation, and exhibited distinct risk characteristics per provider. Further, we were able to differentiate between misconfigurations that were "exposed" and misconfigurations that were actually "practically exploitable," demonstrating a critical gap between visibility and real security risk. To enable reproducibility and future research, we are releasing an open misconfiguration dataset, a unified taxonomy, and an extensible scanning framework. The results of our research will provide the first empirical, cross-cloud view of misconfiguration risk, as well as actionable insights for both researchers and practitioners.

title: XFMA-Net: Explainable Forensic Multi-Scale Attention Framework for Image Forgery Detection
authors: Bandita Das, Suvendra Kumar Jayasingh, Rashmi Ranjan Sahoo, Bunil Kumar Balabantaray, Sambit Kumar Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: In the digital editing age, it is crucial to determine image forgeries because the authenticity and integrity of visual images are being at a higher compromise. The presented work introduces the XFMA-Net (EfficientNet with Built-in Multi-Scale Attention), the innovative architecture of deep learning that can be used to recognize different image forgeries such as splicing, copy-move, and retouching. XFMA-Net is built based on the EfficientNet-B7 architecture and consists of multi-scale feature fusion and attention mechanisms, which improves the representability of the features and increases its effectiveness as a forgery detector. The model was assessed using the CASIAv2.0 dataset, exhibiting enhanced performance relative to current methodologies. XFMA-Net attained an exceptional accuracy of 98.3%, precision of 97.8%, recall of 98.2%, and F1-score of 98%, markedly surpassing contemporary methodologies. Alongside robust quantitative outcomes, XFMA-Net prioritises model interpretability by facilitating attention-guided visualisation of manipulation-sensitive areas through Grad-CAM-based localisation. These visual elucidations underscore the areas that predominantly affect the model’s determinations, offering transparent and interpretable forensic proof of image manipulation.

title: 5G-Enabled Digital Twin and AI-Driven Autonomous Energy Management Architecture for Predictive and Vehicle-to-Grid Optimization in Next-Generation Electric Vehicles
authors: Saranya V, R. Dharani
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Digital Twin (DT) technology and Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology are redefining the next generation of Electric Vehicles (EV) utilizing intelligent energy management, predictive intelligence, and autonomous control frameworks. As of 2026, rather than focusing on the energy transition, most of the current implementations of digital twins are aimed at battery health monitoring, state estimation, and predictive maintenance, and AI models are used for energy optimization and range prediction. However, the current solutions are mostly working in semi-integrated architectures without fully closed-loop real-time adaptive autonomy and scalable vehicle-to-grid (V2G) coordination capabilities. This study proposes the unified Digital Twin and AI-powered autonomous energy management architecture for the next-generation EVs. The proposed framework includes multi-scale electrochemical-thermal modeling of the battery, edge-cloud decision-making artificial intelligence (AI), and a predictive intelligence mechanism for proactive energy control. In contrast to the traditional Battery Management Systems (BMS) based on module-level state estimation and empirical heuristics, the proposed battery management system supports cell-level adaptive monitoring & state monitoring, physics-informed recalibration, and learning-based continuous optimization. The architecture has the ability for real-time adaptive battery management, early fault detection, optimized energy distribution, smart fast charging control, and bidirectional V2G interaction. By integrating simulation-driven optimization, reinforcement learning strategies, and intelligent control policies in a closed-loop digital twin ecosystem, the framework targets improving battery lifetime, improving energy efficiency, guaranteeing operation safety, and increasing grid resilience. The proposed integrated approach is a scalable, resilient, and also future-ready solution for smart electric mobility systems beyond 2026. The framework is validated using real-world lithium-ion battery degradation data and electric vehicle operational datasets to demonstrate predictive accuracy and system robustness.

title: Information Sufficiency Limits in Metaheuristicbased IIR System Identification Under Multi-Level Observation Reduction
authors: Ridvan Firat Cinar, Burcu Bektas Gunes, Cebrail Turkeri, Serdar Ekinci
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This study examines information-induced limits of parameter identifiability in IIR system identification under multi-level observation representations. A fixed populationbased optimization framework is employed to isolate information effects from algorithmic variability, while the observation model is progressively reduced in information content. Using a persistently exciting white Gaussian input, three observation scenarios are analysed: full time-domain measurements, magnitude-only spectral observations, and band-limited magnitude representations. Although numerical convergence is achieved under all scenarios, parameter estimation accuracy deteriorates systematically as observation information decreases, as reflected in the distribution of relative parameter errors. Fisher information-based indicators are evaluated as complementary sensitivity measures and reveal consistent reductions in local information volume, while also illustrating their limitations under nonlinear and many-to-one observation mappings. The results indicate that identifiability loss originates from structural information insufficiency in the observation model and cannot be compensated by optimization alone.

title: A Machine Learning–based Framework for Indian Domestic Flight Ticket Price Prediction using Real-World Aviation Data
authors: Akash Badhan, Priyank Arora, Rishabh Garg, Vimal Dubey, Ramandeep Kaur
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: It is imperative to accurately predict airline ticket prices to support passenger-centric decision-making and smart airline analytics, especially in intensely expanding aviation markets. The given paper suggests an end-to-end machine learning model based on the prediction of domestic flight ticket prices in India on the basis of a real-life dataset that consists of more than 10,000 domestic flight records in India that were gathered in 2019. The dataset contains airline, route, temporal and operational characteristics in detail that make it possible to model the airfare pricing behavior in a comprehensive manner. The preprocessing and feature engineering done are large scale to obtain meaningful temporal, duration-related and route-related features. The systematically evaluated algorithms include multiple regression models, which are linear regression, ensemble, and gradient-boosting algorithms. Experimental data confirms that ensemble and boosting-based models are always more effective in comparison to linear models, making them significantly lower in terms of the probability of errors in predictions and good-of-fit, which is why they are effective in the representation of nonlinear changes in prices. The feature importance analysis also shows that the flight duration, number of stops, airline name, and the time of the journey are all predominant factors that affect the prices of tickets. The presented framework can offer both precise forecasts and readable information on airfare change, which can serve as a scalable and feasible solution to comprehend and predict the pricing of Indian domestic flights.

title: From Pixels to Policies: An AI-Driven Workflow for Quantifying Agricultural Drought Impact on Food Security
authors: C Thiyagarajan, Meganathan Elumalai, Pavunraj D, Sangeetha A, Vijayakumar K, Ezhilvendan M
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Agricultural droughts severely impact crop yields, posing a direct threat to global food security. Traditional assessment methods are often slow, spatially sparse, and disconnected from actionable policy frameworks. To bridge this gap, this study presents an end-to-end, AI-driven workflow that transforms remote sensing data into timely and policy-relevant insights. Our method integrates multisource satellite data, including Sentinel-2 (NDVI, NDWI) and MODIS-derived indices, with climate data from CHIRPS and soil moisture information. A hybrid deep learning model, combining convolutional and long short-term memory networks (CNN-LSTM), was developed to first classify drought severity and subsequently predict consequent crop yield impacts. Validated across major agricultural regions (Punjab, India, and Nebraska, USA), the model achieved 92.3% accuracy in drought severity classification and a yield prediction root mean square error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.67 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex>, significantly outperforming conventional approaches. The system culminates in a near-real-time dashboard that quantifies the drought impact and generates specific, localized policy alerts for food security interventions. This study demonstrates a scalable pipeline from pixels to policies, enhancing the responsiveness and precision of drought mitigation and food security strategies.

title: Non-Contact Electrostatic Cleaning of Photovoltaic Panels Using Corona-Induced Charge Transfer
authors: Mohammad R. Ahmed, Arif M. Rahman, Aaron T. Ohta
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This paper presents a novel non-contact electrostatic dust removal technique designed to clean dust from surfaces without modifying them, making it especially applicable to photovoltaic (PV) systems. Dust particles are first charged by a negative corona discharge generated by needle electrodes at DC voltages in the range of −5 kV to −22 kV. The charged dust particles are then dislodged using electrostatic force from a metal plate biased at voltages between +5 kV and +22 kV. Dust removal efficiencies of up to 82.56 % were achieved. In addition, this noncontact electrostatic dust removal technique restored up to 97 % of a PV panel's power output, demonstrating this technique's potential as an effective, waterless, and contactless dust mitigation solution.

title: A Modular Graph Coloring Abstraction for Register Allocation in LLVM: Design and Evaluation
authors: Enes Abdulhalik, Mehmet Emin Baytekin, Gizem Sungu Terci, Alp Arslan Bayrakci, Alihan Turan
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Graph coloring is one of the widely adopted methods for register allocation. However, the theoretical results based on spill metrics may be misleading as justified in this paper. For that reason, we present a modular abstraction by introducing RAGraph library in order to be able to plug in any register allocation algorithm based on graph coloring to LLVM framework. The optimizations applied by default LLVM register allocators are also adapted by RAGraph to achieve best possible results. As a case study, we adapt one of the promising evolutionary graph coloring algorithms for register allocation, namely BitEA, as a replacement for the register allocator inside LLVM compiler. The results demonstrate very similar performance with the best default register allocator of LLVM.

title: A Retrieval-Augmented Agent for Context-Aware Waste Classification Across National Systems
authors: Katja Crusius, Joan Puteri Zheng
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Accurate household waste sorting is critical to achieve circular economy goals and reducing environmental impact, yet waste-management regulations vary significantly between jurisdictions. This paper presents a conversational agent built on a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architecture that guides users in the correct disposal of household items. The system currently supports two distinct national contexts: the United States, representing one of the largest countries in the world by population, and Germany, known for its highly specific and structured recycling regulations. The agent is built on a RAG structure which includes query router, document retriever, relevance grader, web searcher, answer generator, and chatter agents for the best possible performance. We evaluated the system using 246 test cases covering English and German recycling questions in the United States and Germany. The agent correctly classified 88.7% of materials into the appropriate bin, demonstrating its effectiveness in supporting users with recycling decisions.

title: Resource Efficiency Software Index (RESI): Towards Software Product Comparability
authors: Geerd-Dietger Hoffmann, Verena Majuntke
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Software increasingly shapes ICT resource consumption, yet stakeholders lack a standardized and reproducible way to compare the resource efficiency of software products with similar functionality. We present the Resource Efficiency Software Index (RESI), a measurement-based framework that assigns an interpretable efficiency class (A-E) to products within a software category defined by shared core use cases. RESI evaluates operational resource consumption across four measurable lifecycle phases (INSTALL, IDLE, RUN, UNINSTALL) and formalizes assessment as a five-step pipeline: phase-specific measurement, ordinalization of heterogeneous metrics into comparable scores, weighted aggregation into phase scores, aggregation into a total efficiency score, and mapping to an A-E label. We instantiate RESI for ten widely used web browsers, execute the pipeline under a controlled setup, and report the resulting scores and labels. We probe platform effects by rerunning the pipeline on a consumer laptop and discuss implications for interpreting RESI across hardware environments.

title: Synergy Model of Threats in Defi Smart Contracts
authors: Vladyslav Khvostenko, Olexander Shmatko, Yelyzaveta Sevriukova, Illia Bukatych, Oleksandr Iegrashyn, Iurii Iashunin
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Smart contracts are key elements of decentralized financial protocols, but security incidents are usually caused not by single vulnerabilities, but by combined scenarios in which several threats interact and reinforce each other. The article proposes a synergistic graph model of threats in DeFi smart contracts, which formalizes the set of threats, their impact on information security properties (confidentiality, integrity, availability, authenticity, accountability, and auditability), and reflects the projection of compromised properties onto security domains (CS/IS/SI). The proposed approach differs in that it introduces synergistic links between threats as a separate type of edges in the graph, which allows reproducing characteristic trajectories of combined attacks and explaining the mechanisms of their implementation. In particular, a typical chain of economic attacks is shown, in which an attacker uses an instant unsecured loan to manipulate the market price, which leads to a distortion of the oracle's price data and, as a result, creates conditions for exploiting logical defects in the smart contract or abusing liquidation mechanisms. The proposed model can be used as a methodological basis for risk analysis, prioritization of smart contract audits, and planning of protective measures in DeFi ecosystems.

title: LLM Enhanced Policy Gradient Approach for Cold Start and Diversity Aware News Recommendation
authors: Devisree A, Hency Juliet A, Legapriyadharshini N
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Recommender systems utilize user interactions and item features to generate personalized content; however, they often encounter challenges such as sparse feedback, data sparsity issues, and a tendency toward popularity bias, which limits diversity. To overcome these issues, this paper introduces an LLM-enhanced policy gradient framework that combines transformer-based semantic feature augmentation with reinforcement learning for news recommendation. The proposed approach is novel in its unified integration of semantic enrichment and diversity-aware optimization within a policy gradient model, which effectively mitigates data sparsity issues while maintaining recommendation diversity. The model enhances news representations using LLM-derived features and incorporates a diversity-aware reward mechanism to balance accuracy and content variety. Experimental results on the MIND-Small dataset demonstrate strong performance, achieving nDCG@10 of 0.6095, Precision@10 of 0.2857, Recall@10 of 0.9196, AUC of 0.5418, and MRR of 0.5174. Furthermore, the model shows notable improvements in coldstart scenarios, highlighting its ability to manage sparse interaction data effectively. Overall, the proposed framework delivers accurate, diverse, and robust recommendations suitable for dynamic real-world news environments.

title: A Review on the Security Challenges of Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Md. Shabab Anwar, Mahabub Hasan Mahalat, Mrinal Goswami
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Through automation, increased productivity, data transmission, real-time monitoring, and decision-making, the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) transforms modern industries by integrating a vast array of diverse devices, such as sensors, actuators, and industrial control systems, into a massive connected network. However, because IIoT manages a large amount of security-sensitive data, particularly in crucial environments like defense, energy, power grids, and transportation, this widespread interconnectivity creates new security challenges. Unlike conventional consumer IoT, the security requirements of IIoT are intensive due to their complex structure and formidable risks associated with industrial applications. In this study, we explore the different security requirements of IIoT. We examine the security challenges specific to IIoT and the technological and structural constraints that complicate the mitigation of these security vulnerabilities. In addition, we review and analyze various existing and emerging security measures that aim to address the security challenges. The current work provides insight into IIoT security and highlights the gap in this area for future research.

title: LeafNet: CNN-Based Approach for Tree Species Recognition from Leaf Images
authors: Deepika S, Kavitha Subramani, A Karthikeyan, Jabasheela L, Sathiya V, Maheswari M
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Detecting tree species through leaf identification is essential in botanical identification research, monitoring biodiversity, and conservation ecology. The traditional manual task of identifying tree species through leaf identification is labor-intensive, can be subjective, and makes the chance for classification error increase when working with larger data sets. The proposed LeafNet based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model for automated leaf images of trees and the accurate recognition of tree species. The proposed model implements hierarchical feature extraction learned with training data, these features are used to identify low-level edgeand texture-based information to high-level shape- and venation-based information as a means to support classification across a range of species. Evaluations of our model and training data show that LeafNet model attained 96.7% accuracy over traditional machine learning-based approaches and baseline CNN models. The model reaches a precision of 0.97, a recall of 0.96, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 0.96, which in general indicates comparable performance across classes. The confusion matrix also verifies its strong classification capability with only minor mistakes occurring with visually similar species. LeafNet may help in consuming much larger data sets in ecological study, digital herbarium made, or applied into smart forest operational management systems as a practical and scalable model for richer plant recognition applications.

title: Ultra-Low Power VLSI Architecture for Edge AI-based Biomedical Signal Processing
authors: Jami Venkata Suman, Aenikapati Swetha Priya, Sujatha Kotte, Sonali Ulhas Kadwadkar, Manjusha Yuvraj Patil, Inoyatillo Kholmurodov
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This research presents a new architecture for edge AI-enabled biomedical signal processing using extremely low powered hardware (VLSI). The architectural design involved the integration of the following components: 1) a low-noise analog front end; 2) an energy-efficient analog-to-digit converter; and 3) a small footprint neural accelerator optimized for fixed-point and approximate arithmetic. The architecture achieves low power consumption through the use of dynamic voltage and frequency scaling, fine-grained clock gating, exploiting sparsity within data, and limiting the active processing to clinically relevant times through event-driven activation. The three-layered memory architecture also serves to reduce data movement and use on-chip data compression and adaptive sampling to reduce data throughput. The architecture was designed to execute workloads for ECG, EEG, and PPG signals while providing latency-optimized inference under stringent power budgets. Validation was accomplished through the use of a combination of cycle-accurate simulations combined with silicon-aware energy models to quantify the trade-offs between accuracy, latency, and energy. The results indicate that there are significant energy savings relative to traditional edge designs while meeting all clinical performance targets. Future efforts will include prototypes suitable for mass production and clinical validation.

title: Knowledge Distillation for Large Language Models: A Survey with a Case Study for Human-Robot Interaction and Edge Deployment
authors: Hatice Beril Satıcı, Ahmet Tuğrul Bayrak
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve remarkable performance but require significant resource requirements, making them infeasible for latency-critical applications such as human-robot interaction (HRI) and edge computing. Knowledge distillation addresses this gap by transferring capabilities from large teacher models to compact students while preserving performance. This survey reviews knowledge distillation methods for generative LLMs with an emphasis on enabling real-time, embodied, and interactive AI systems. We systematically analyze methods across white-box approaches (reverse KL divergence, cross-vocabulary alignment, layer-wise distillation) and black-box techniques (adversarial learning, context distillation, self-play), integrating complementary compression methods including quantization (GPTQ, AWQ, SmoothQuant), speculative decoding (Medusa, Hydra), and model merging. We identify generativespecific challenges-divergence selection, vocabulary mismatches, error compounding, and self-distillation risks-and analyze deployment trade-offs across cloud, edge, and robotic platforms. Grounded in psycholinguistic evidence that conversational naturalness requires sub- 500 ms response latencies, we validate our analysis through an empirical benchmark of four distilled models (82M-1.1B parameters) on 20 HRI-specific tasks spanning robot commands, social dialogue, grounded reasoning, and safety-critical scenarios. Our results reveal a three-way trade-off between latency, response quality, and safety alignment, with subbillion models achieving the 500ms threshold while larger models fail it on CPU hardware. We conclude with open problems in latency-aware distillation, safety preservation under compression, multimodal grounding for embodied agents, and hardware-aware optimization.

title: Evaluating COMPASS for Socially-Aware Robot Navigation in Dynamic Indoor Environments
authors: Rahmi Kavak, Özgür Yılmaz
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Socially-aware robot navigation focuses on enabling robots to move safely and efficiently in environments shared with humans, while respecting social norms such as interpersonal distance, predictability, and comfort. As robots increasingly operate in dynamic and interaction-rich environments, evaluating navigation performance beyond purely geometric metrics becomes essential. This study aims to analyze the behavior of the COMPASS framework under dynamic conditions and establish a baseline for extending it toward socially-aware robot navigation. While COMPASS has demonstrated strong performance in structured settings, its behavior in dynamic environments remains less explored. To address this gap, experiments are conducted in warehouse environments with static layouts and dynamic scenarios involving moving, human-like agents. Navigation performance is assessed using both task-oriented and interaction-related metrics, including Success Rate (SR) and Base Contact Rate (BCR). In static environments, the trained policy achieves high success rates (up to 92.0%) with low contact levels (approximately 8–11%), closely matching COMPASS baseline performance. In dynamic environments, even small amounts of motion reduce the success rate, although it remains relatively high (around 84–85%), while BCR increases to approximately 15%, indicating reduced interaction safety. These results indicate that navigation performance achieved in static environments does not fully generalize to dynamic, interaction-rich scenarios. While COMPASS performs effectively in structured settings, its performance degrades as environmental dynamics increase. The proposed evaluation framework provides a reproducible baseline for improving navigation performance in dynamic environments and extending COMPASS toward socially-aware navigation.
