title: A Lightweight and Efficient Backtracking SINS in-motion Alignment Algorithm Aided by GNSS
authors: Xufeng Liu, Binglong Yan, Pengcheng Mu, Qinghua Xiao, Jie Yuan, Lin Wang, Zhikun Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To enhance the alignment accuracy and rapidity of inertial navigation systems onboard unmanned aerial vehicles under dynamic conditions, an in-motion alignment algorithm based on backtracking alignment scheme and invariant extended Kalman filter is proposed in this paper. By reusing computation results from backtracking coarse alignment phase, the algorithm reduces the time required for data collection in the fine alignment phase, outperforming traditional alignment scheme. An optimized data storage structure reduces memory requirements and computation complexity, enabling the algorithm to be effectively deployed on hardware terminal. Furthermore, invariant extended Kalman filter significantly suppresses the nonlinear errors induced by dynamic environments. Simulation experiments indicate that, the RMSE of alignment error is reduced by 10.6% within 600s alignment time, compared with the two-stage initial alignment method comprising indirect coarse alignment and fine alignment. The data storage scheme compresses storage volume and computation time by 96% and 45.7%, respectively, compared with the backtracking alignment algorithm which stores all of the sensors data. The effectiveness and robustness of the proposed algorithm are convincingly validated by multi-trajectory out-field experiments.

title: A Ka-Band Time-Modulated Phase-Invariant Variable-Gain Amplifier With 30-dB Gain Tuning Based on Duty-Cycle Control
authors: Dongfan Xu, Minzhe Tang, Haiyun Gu, Yuxuan Liu, Yilun Chen, Ziyuan Ren, Minghao Fan, Daxu Zhang, Yi Zhang, Duo Li, Zheng Li, Zezheng Liu, Chun Wang, Yudai Yamazaki, Yuncheng Zhang, Kazuaki Kunihiro, Hiroyuki Sakai, Atsushi Shirane, Kenichi Okada
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a time-modulated variable-gain amplifier (VGA) employing a clock-sampling topology governed by a duty-cycle control (DCC) loop. By modulating the effective operation time of the amplifier rather than altering its RF bias conditions, for precise tuning of the clock duty cycle, enabling phase consistency at millimeter-wave frequencies. The proposed DCC loop provides precise and stable DCC, enabling robust time-domain gain modulation and mitigating phase perturbations typically associated with conventional VGA architectures. Implemented in 65-nm CMOS technology, the VGA consumes 4.9–103 mW from a 1-V supply, depending on the gain setting, and occupies a core area of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1150\times 280~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m. Measurements demonstrate a 30-dB continuous gain-control range with a 3-dB bandwidth from 24 to 30 GHz and a peak gain of 29.6 dB at 28 GHz. Across 24–29.7 GHz, the measured rms phase error ranges from 0.01° to 0.18° between the highest and lowest gain modes, validating the phase stability of the proposed approach. In addition, an output 1-dB (OP1dB) compression point of 12.5dBm is achieved, indicating robust large-signal performance. The proposed time-modulated VGA is well-suited for scalable millimeter-wave phased-array systems requiring wide-range gain control with minimal phase distortion.

title: BrainMergeEEG: An Open EEG Microstate Dataset for Studying Developers’ Brain Network Dynamics
authors: Robson Keemps, Kleinner Farias, Willian Bolzan, Rafael Kunst
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Although cognitive neuroscience and neuro-software engineering have advanced, there remains a lack of publicly accessible neural datasets that capture brain activity during complex tasks, such as the merging of refactored source code. This limitation undermines reproducibility and hinders the development of cognitively aware tools for high-cognitive-load scenarios.We introduce BrainMergeEEG, the first open EEG microstate dataset collected during refactored code-merging tasks, a demanding activity in collaborative software development. Our dataset includes recordings from 42 participants who completed resting-state sessions and ran controlled source merge tasks, totaling about 8.0 GB of EEG data. Data are organized by task condition and behavioral performance. To promote transparency and reuse, we provide preprocessing scripts and a fully reproducible experimental pipeline. BrainMergeEEG establishes a neurocognitive benchmark for investigating developers’ behavior during source code merge tasks, supporting future research on brain-aware software engineering. The dataset and related materials are available at https://zenodo.org/records/ 17386294.

title: SRViT: A Robust Online Encrypted Traffic Classification Based On Vision Transformer
authors: Chang Liu, Lei Han, Zulong Diao, Zheng Wu, Xin He, Weibei Fan, Fu Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: The dramatic rise in encrypted traffic brings huge challenges to traditional traffic classification methods. Deep learning-based traffic classification methods have been demonstrated to significantly improve performance. However, the following limitations remain: i) It is challenging to concurrently focus on both global and local information in traffic flows, resulting in the absence of important information. ii) The existing methods relying on temporal information suffer from low robustness in case of packet disordering or loss. iii) The use of multi-layer encryption and random routing in Tor technology poses more challenges for traffic identification. In this paper, we propose a novel ViT-based model for more accurate encrypted traffic classification, called SRViT to overcome the above challenges. Firstly, SRViT proposes a novel mechanism of multi-size patch division to learn comprehensive hidden knowledge and dependencies between packets. Secondly, we propose a self-attention operation with a relative position bias to learn the relative position relationship. After that, an incremental update mechanism is proposed to adapt to dynamic changes in the real traffic environment. At last, the comprehensive experiments on 5 real-world encrypted traffic datasets are carried out. The experimental results indicate that SRViT outperforms the state-of-the-art methods with an average accuracy improvement of 24.62% while keeping higher robustness and execution efficiency.

title: Cross-Force Generalization in Myoelectric Gesture Recognition: A Comparative Study of Data Augmentation and Generalized Models
authors: Yangyang Yuan, Xinyu Jiang, Chenyun Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Hand motion is a natural way for humans to interact with machines. Surface electromyography (sEMG) signals, which can be collected on the skin surface of the forearm, record different muscle activations during various degrees of freedom (DoF) of hand movements. A classification model using forearm sEMG as a human-machine interface can achieve excellent accuracy in recognizing hand motions. However, in practical use, users find it difficult to perform hand motions with consistent force, causing fluctuations in the amplitude of sEMG signals. Accordingly, hand motion recognition models trained on ideal data with consistent force often perform poorly across varying force levels. Thus, establishing a model robust to data from different force levels is highly valuable. In this study, we collected sEMG data of five finger extension and flexion (10 finger DoFs in total) at 10% (low), 20% (medium), and 30% (high) maximal voluntary muscle contraction (MVC) force levels from 19 subjects. Our hypothesis was validated on this dataset, with results revealing that models trained with data from medium or high force levels had better cross-force generalization. Additionally, we used traditional amplitude scaling and GAN-based data generation for data augmentation, improving the recognition accuracy of the benchmark model by up to 4.95%. Furthermore, we established a more generalized model using a combination of data augmentation and fine-tuning. Promisingly, through these methods, we improved the benchmark recognition accuracy from 76.81% to 88.49%, reducing the model sensitivity to force levels of the training data. The results of this study contribute to the establishment of a generalized hand motion recognition model for the cross-force issues of practical use.

title: TVFI-GST: From Regime-Level Importance to Fusion-Aware Traffic Forecasting
authors: Ronghua Zhang, Weiping Ding, Jin Hee Yoon, Jianming Zhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: Forecasting the future state of a single target sensor from multi-sensor observations is a critical task in intelligent transportation systems, supporting congestion warning, signal control, and fine-grained traffic management at key locations. Existing methods under this setting often face three limitations: they characterize feature contributions only at the point or window level, rely on reduction-oriented feature processing that may discard complementary information, and rarely transform extracted structural information into relation-aware cues for downstream prediction. To address these issues, this paper proposes TVFI-GST, a spatiotemporal forecasting framework driven by time-varying structural cues. TVFI-GST first constructs target-semantic phase partitions and models phase-level temporal feature importance. It then employs fuzzy information granules (FIG) as carriers of trend evidence and reliability information to enable reliability-aware granular soft fusion, suppressing redundancy while preserving and reconstructing task-relevant information. Finally, the learned fusion structure is converted into dynamic relational cues and lag-aware context for fusion-aware prediction, forming a closed loop between representation learning and downstream forecasting. In this way, FIGs support phase-level importance modeling, reliability characterization, and information-preserving fusion, elevating temporal feature importance from a local explanatory signal to a stable structural interface for subsequent modeling. Experiments on six real-world traffic datasets demonstrate that TVFI-GST achieves strong overall forecasting performance against competitive baselines, while also offering interpretable structural cues under complex traffic conditions.

title: Electroporation Parameters Based Survival Rate Prediction for Bile Duct Cancer: A Comparative Artificial Intelligence Study
authors: Tuğçe Binen, Muhammet Üsame Öziç, Sevilay Çetin
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The use of electroporation in various medical treatments is becoming popular. In oncology applications, electroporation-based treatment is an effective method for cancer treatment and tumor ablation. In electroporation applications, the cell survival rate is an important indicator for assessing electroporation outcomes and treatment efficacy. Therefore, applying optimal and correct parameters is necessary to provide the required cell viability in the treatment. This study involved the development and comparative analysis of five machine learning-based regression models (Random Forest, Decision Tree, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, and Artificial Neural Networks) to predict survival rates using data from a bile duct cell line originating from the digestive system. The study used a dataset comprising three cancer cell lines (HCCC9810, HuCCT1, and QBC939) and one healthy cell line (HIBEpic). The results revealed that boosting-based methods, in particular, demonstrated superior performance compared to other models. Of the algorithms tested, the Gradient Boosting model produced the most accurate predictions (mean absolute error (MAE): 4.0404, root mean square error (RMSE): <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left.5.2521, R^{2}: 0.9540\right)$</tex>. Furthermore, feature importance analysis revealed that electric field strength was the dominant parameter. In this study, a graphical user interface was also developed to estimate cell survival rates based on input parameters. The interface demonstrates the practical applicability of regression models. These results show that machine-learning-based regression models are reliable for predicting survival rates and could be used as real-time decision-support systems in preclinical planning.

title: Zero-trust Conceptual Architecture for Secure Integration of Large Language Models and Autonomous AI Agents
authors: Oleksandr Kushnerov, Vladyslav Sokol, Olena Akhiiezer, Andrii Cherniavskyi, Maksym Nadtochyi, Dmytro Balagura
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The rapid transition from passive large language models to autonomous agent-based coding systems, including frameworks like Claude Code and OpenClaw, has significantly accelerated software development cycles while simultaneously complicating the enterprise attack surface. Conventional security paradigms relying on perimeter defenses and static role-based access control (RBAC) are inherently designed for deterministic entities, leaving them critically underequipped to mitigate threats posed by non-deterministic algorithmic agents. These autonomous systems frequently generate unpredictable, multi-step sequences of system calls upon ingesting poisoned external environment data or hidden prompt injections. This article proposes a novel Zero Trust architectural framework, complete with rigorous mathematical formalization, specifically engineered for secure multi-agent ecosystems. The architectural core features an intelligent Semantic Gateway deeply integrated with operating system-level coordination modules to dynamically provision ephemeral sandboxing environments. To achieve continuous authorization for asynchronous transactions, the framework utilizes a mathematical structure based on Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks (HGNN). This algorithm continuously computes an agent's dynamic trust tensor by quantifying the topological divergence between its predicted and actual traversal paths within the deterministic space of a corporate repository graph. To empirically validate the analytical efficacy of this advanced architecture, the research team executed a large-scale simulation processing 100,000 asynchronous transactions. The proposed framework demonstrated exceptional predictive power, achieving a true positive rate (TPR) exceeding 98.7 % in the detection and preventive isolation of critical remote code execution vulnerabilities, including sophisticated CVE-2025-61260 exploits. Crucially, this robust security posture was maintained with a minimal false positive rate (FPR) of 1.1 % and an average processing overhead of just 45 milliseconds. Ultimately, this paradigm modernizes the NIST SP 800-207A standard by replacing static binary access controls with a continuous Markov decision process, transforming stochastic AI unpredictability into a quantifiable engineering asset for critical infrastructures.

title: Liver Semantic Segmentation: Enhancing Medical Image Accuracy
authors: Sangeeta Viswanadham, Jaya Lakshmi Nemana, Riju Bhattacharya, Deepak Asudani, Supriya Gupta Bani, Anand Tamrakar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Liver cancer is the fifth most commonly occurring cancer in men and the ninth most commonly occurring cancer in women. To allow appropriate diagnosis and treatment of liver diseases, there is a need to have reliable segmentation of the liver on the imaging. Our proposed method is aimed to refine the liver margins on the outcomes of an imaging procedure, including CT and MRI. The primary objective is to enhance the liver segmentation software and to identify liver tumors correctly. By using the Intersection over Union measure in the model loss, we improved its accuracy in spotting both liver outlines and tumors. Rather than relying on Dice Loss, we used another approach for training—we explain this approach below. We found that using Cross Entropy Loss along with U-Net achieves better results than using Dice Loss. As a result, the level of performance was significantly improved. Our model gets a better Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) score of 0.9256 than the original U-Net with Dice Loss which has a DSC of 0.9115. It illustrates that using our method makes liver segmentation and tumor identification more reliable and accurate. In general, this work helps to diagnose and manage liver diseases and identify both normal, harmless and malignant liver tumors.

title: GNN-VoidGuard: Void-Aware Topology Control in Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks
authors: Ashok Battula, C H Jayaprakash, Dustakar Surendra Rao, Anil Kumar Koona, Voruchu Sai Babu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Underwater acoustic sensor networks support environmental monitoring and offshore infrastructure inspection, but they face harsh channel conditions and irregular three dimensional deployments that create routing voids and disconnected regions. These voids reduce data delivery and waste scarce energy, especially under sparse deployments, node failures, and low SNR channels. Conventional schemes such as depth based routing, vector based forwarding, and heuristic energy aware topology control rely on local rules and do not explicitly model void patterns, which leads to persistent holes and unstable paths. Recent graph neural network based approaches improve link selection but remain largely void unaware. This work proposes GNN-VoidGuard, a void aware topology control framework that uses a graph neural network with depth positional encoding and a void aware loss to learn transmission and power decisions that reshape the connectivity graph. The model is trained on three dimensional simulation scenarios in a 1000×1000×100 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> underwater volume with a central blocked region, 50 to 200 nodes, and up to 20% node failures. GNN-VoidGuard achieves 98.6% connectivity, reduces void occurrence probability to 0.06, and shrinks the average void size to three nodes, while attaining a packet delivery ratio of 97.2%, an average delay of 1.31 s, and an energy cost of 1.19 J per successful packet, outperforming depth based, vector based, energy based, and void unaware GNN baselines. These results show that void aware topology control with GNNs is a promising strategy for next generation underwater sensing systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of XGBoost and Multi Layer Perceptron Neural Network Using TensorFlow Keras for Stress Level Prediction
authors: Hency Juliet A, Legapriyadharshini N, Malathi P, B. Shathya, V. Vijayalakshmi
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: This research aims to assess and compare the effectiveness of XGBoost and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) neural network models, implemented with TensorFlow/Keras, in predicting stress levels from various physiological and psychological indicators. The study focuses on determining which model delivers superior performance in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and overall prediction efficiency for stress classification. This research employed the Student Stress Monitoring dataset obtained from Kaggle, containing 843 anonymized survey responses from college students aged 18-21, encompassing psychological, physiological, environmental, academic, and social stress factors. The analysis involved applying two predictive modeling approaches-XGBoost and a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) neural network developed using TensorFlow/Keras. The results of the research suggest that Deep Learning algorithms possess the ability to offer accurate forecastsStudents Stress level. The significance level of this study is indicated by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}=\mathbf{0. 0 4 2}$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<\mathbf{0. 0 5}$</tex>. This indicates that there is a statistical significance observed between the two algorithms. Findings reveal that the MLP model outperformed XGBoost with a higher accuracy of 89% versus 86.82%, along with improved precision, recall, and F1-scores for most categories. Although XGBoost offered quicker training and prediction times, the MLP model delivered better overall effectiveness in classifying stress levels.

title: RecycleSense: Machine Learning Visual Recognition for Waste Sorting and Recycling Optimization
authors: Talya Nir, Atul Dubey
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Materials recovery facilities (MRFs) aim to maximize the recovery of recyclable materials through manual and mechanical waste sorting techniques. In 2018, 32.1% of the 292.4 million tons of municipal waste generated was recycled, despite the estimate that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 \%}$</tex> of waste in the U.S. is recyclable (EPA). Missed recyclable materials and increased contamination significantly reduce the effectiveness of recycling programs and degrade natural environments. RecycleSense AI uses machine learning and visual recognition to sort waste and detect contamination, optimizing recycling accuracy and reducing greenhouse gas emissions. To create this application, a garbage image dataset was downloaded from a Kaggle repository. The dataset was split into training, validation, and test parts, and the training data was augmented for category balance. To build the machine learning model, two approaches were used: transfer learning on DenseNet201 and featurizing data using ConvNeXtLarge while applying various machine learning techniques (KNN, Random Forest, MLP, and XGBoost). The multilayer perceptron (MLP) model yielded the highest accuracy of 91.6% on the test data. Finally, a function that compared label probabilities was created to determine the level of contamination in the sample. The model was deployed in a Streamlit web application that allows a user to capture or upload images for classification. The application was able to make predictions accurately and determine contamination levels within three to four seconds, proving its ability to improve recycling efficiency. With largescale implementation, it can enhance waste sorting performance at MRFs, optimizing recycling and minimizing climate impact.

title: Transformer-based Multimodal Learning for Neurological Disease Diagnosis using EEG and MRI Fusion
authors: Sathish Mathiyazhagan, K G.Sathiya, Nivedita V S, Arun balaji P, Ramya R, M.Vaidegi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of neurological diseases requires effective integration of heterogeneous brain signals that capture complementary structural and functional information. This study aims to develop and evaluate a transformer-based multimodal learning framework that jointly models electroencephalography (EEG) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) data for neurological disease classification. The proposed architecture employs modality-specific transformer encoders to preserve temporal EEG dynamics and spatial MRI representations, followed by a cross-modal attention mechanism to explicitly align and fuse complementary features. The framework was evaluated using a subject-independent experimental protocol on an open-access simultaneous EEG–MRI dataset. Quantitative results demonstrate that the proposed multimodal transformer achieves an accuracy of 95.6%, F1-score of 94.9%, and AUC of 0.98, outperforming unimodal and early-fusion baselines by up to 17.2 percentage points. These findings indicate that attention-based multimodal fusion significantly enhances diagnostic reliability by reducing false negatives and improving generalization performance. The results confirm that transformer-based cross-modal learning provides an effective and robust approach for multimodal neurological disease diagnosis and offers strong potential for clinical decision-support applications.

title: A Novel Hybrid Geospatial-Temporal Deep Neural Network for Real-Time Environmental Air Quality Prediction using Multi-Source Sensor Fusion
authors: A. Akilandeswari
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Air quality monitoring in the environment is important when it comes to the health of the people, city plan and mitigation of climate change. The article proposes a novel hybrid geospatial-temporal deep neural network model that integrates the utilization of multi-source sensor data in real-time predicting PM 2.5. The key of our ideas are as follows: (1) a spatial-conscious attention model that trains to learn the geographic distribution of pollution (2) recurrent neural network based temporal features models that are temporal, (3) multi-task-based prediction of multiple pollutants (4) uncertainty quantification via Monte Carlo dropout. In 4-year comprehensive data of 50 monitoring stations, there is excellent performance with 5000 observations (R2 = 0.53, MAE = 6.79 mg/m3, RMSE = 8.50 mg/m3) of experimental performance compared to baseline models. The given architecture offers the prediction accuracy growth to 23 and 15 percent in comparison to the conventional Random Forest algorithms and the standard deep neural networks respectively. The model performance is proved on spatial, temporal and residual levels with seven overall visualizations. The article contributes to the sphere of environmental science and artificial intelligence where it provides a scalable architecture of the air quality forecasting, in real-time.

title: Application of Quantum Computing Gates for Enhanced Task Scheduling in Cloud Environments
authors: Prathamesh Lahande, Shree Nigade, Tejas Mandhare
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Cloud task scheduling is a complex optimization problem that has a decisive influence on the use of resources, execution latency, and scalability of distributed infrastructures. In the modern cloud computing environments, most cloud applications utilize classical computing gates for all computations. However, with the progress of Quantum Computing (QC) in the domain of task scheduling, it is important to apply and compare the performance of QC gates to ensure ideal QC-circuits are designed to enhance the scheduling of tasks. This paper investigates implementing C-NOT, Hadamard (H), and Phase-T gates of QC as part of a quantum-optimization platform for enhancing the task scheduling in the cloud. This includes analyzing their functional role in a real-time experimental setup using Virtual Machine (VM) configurations ranging from six to sixty across ten scenarios. Real-time tasks were scheduled using these QC-gates, their performance was recorded and compared using the time performance parameter. The experimental results show that the scheduling performed using the C-NOT QC-gate achieved the computational latency of 26.40ms, the least among the other considered QC-gates. On the other hand, the Hadamard and Phase-T QC-gates recorded 27.78ms and 29.25ms of latency, respectively. This conveyed that the C-NOT QC-gate demonstrated an enhanced task scheduling with an improvement of 4.97% and 9.74% compared to the Hadamard and Phase-T gate. This research supports utilizing entanglement-based scheduling achieved using the C-NOT gate for improved and optimized latency, thus making it the most effective gate set up to schedule cloud tasks.

title: Reverse-Engineering Commercial Microwave Link Data for Extracting Environmental Information
authors: Tal Weissblat, Noam David
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Commercial Microwave Link (CML) networks, originally designed for mobile communication, experience signal attenuation due to weather and atmospheric conditions. While the potential of CMLs as opportunistic rainfall sensors is recognized, practical reuse remains limited by proprietary and closed system architectures. This paper presents a lightweight reverse engineering approach to process noisy Received Signal Level (RSL) data from CMLs for rainfall estimation. Using medianbased baselines, noise thresholding, wet-antenna correction, and a power-law rain-rate model, we analyze data from 17 rainy days in central-eastern Israel. The results show that CML-derived rainfall estimates closely match observations from a nearby rain gauge (mean correlation =0.77; mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MSE} =1.04(\text{mm} / \mathrm{h})^{2}$</tex>; average mean bias =0.084 mm/h), demonstrating how reverse engineering can repurpose existing communication infrastructure into a scalable, low-cost environmental monitoring tool.

title: Multimodal Transformer Model for Depression Prediction from Psychosocial and Lifestyle Indicators
authors: V Prathipa, Pramod Pandey, D Antony Joseph Rajan, G Suresh, Nilamadhab Mishra, T Supriya
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Depression constitutes a significant global mental health issue, and prompt identification is essential for effective treatments. Conventional diagnostic methods, including selfreported questionnaires and clinical assessments, can exhibit subjectivity and require considerable effort. This research introduces a Multimodal Transformer Model for forecasting depression by utilizing psychological and lifestyle data obtained from a structured survey of Bangladeshi individuals. The model assimilates diverse inputs using a transformer-based architecture, identifying complex patterns and relationships that traditional machine learning (ML) techniques might neglect. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method attains a validation accuracy of 94.3 %, illustrating its enhanced efficacy compared to conventional prediction methodologies. The findings demonstrate that multimodal transformers can proficiently assimilate many behavioral and psychosocial cues, providing a solid framework for the early identification of depression. The research underscores the efficacy of transformer-based frameworks in behavioral analytics, offering a scalable and dependable instrument for tailored interventions. This method enhances predictive accuracy and facilitates the integration of many data sources in psychological research, hence aiding better informed and prompt therapeutic decisionmaking.

title: Seasonal Tourism Demand Forecasting using Box-Jenkins ARIMA Time-Series Modeling
authors: Malasriganga C, C. Shalini
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Forecasting seasonal tourist demand is essential for efficient planning, capacity management, and policy development in the tourism industry. This work introduces a Seasonal Tourism Demand Forecasting method using BoxJenkins ARIMA time-series modelling to effectively identify trends and seasonal patterns in visitor arrivals. The research used a practical tourism dataset sourced from GitHub, with essential variables such area, nationality, year, month, and monthly visitor arrivals. In accordance with the Box-Jenkins approach, stationarity testing, seasonal differencing, model identification via ACF and PACF, parameter estimation, and residual diagnostics are conducted systematically. A Seasonal ARIMA model with yearly periodicity is used to represent repetitive seasonal patterns. The assessment of forecasting performance employs quantitative error measures. The experimental findings indicate that the suggested model attains a Mean Absolute Error of 2,950, a Root Mean Square Error of 3,780, and a Mean Absolute Percentage Error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 1 0 \%}$</tex> over a prediction horizon of three months. For a twelve-month prediction, the MAE and RMSE rise considerably to 3,650 and 4,580, respectively, but the MAPE stays below 6.5 %, indicating consistent accuracy. The numerical findings validate that BoxJenkins ARIMA modelling offers an accurate, interpretable, and dependable approach for predicting seasonal tourist demand.

title: Hybrid CNN–LSTM-based Vibroarthrography Signal Analysis for Automated Osteoarthritis Detection and Stage Classification
authors: Pradeep K, Paramesh G, Kavitha G
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Knee osteoarthritis is yet to be accurately detected and assessed based on its severity because of the low-level differences in radiographs and subjective interpretation of imaging results. Most of the current deep learning approaches are based only on convolutional architectures, and this implies that they are incapable of differentiating similar stages of diseases. In order to overcome this drawback, the hybrid deep learning system is proposed to detect and classify the stage of osteoarthritis. The suggested implementation combines convolutional neural networks in discriminatory spatial feature extraction with long short-term memory networks in capturing structured dependencies in learned representations. A publicly available knee osteoarthritis dataset has been experimented with showing a multiclass classification accuracy of 94.2% with binary osteoarthritis detection showing an AUC of 0.94. Ablation analysis also proves the fact that the hybrid architecture has the best performance in comparison with the baseline convolutional models. These findings suggest that the suggested framework is a valid and objective tool of computer-aided osteoarthritis measurement, which can enhance better clinical decision-making.

title: HiFi-Attention Generative Adversarial Network-Based Speech Enhancement
authors: Minipriya T M, Rajavel R
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The quality and clarity of the sound signal are enhanced using the speech enhancement process. Background noises and distortions are common in audio records, and these artifacts are removed with the help of a denoising process. It enhances the fidelity and audibility of the signals, which ensure high accuracy for practical uses. Specifically, in a real-world environment, restoring the speech signal is challenging due to noises and it affects the overall quality of the signal. Researchers give more focus on telecommunication and speech recognition systems, which help to identify clear speech even in a noisy environment. Thus, a new HiFi-Attention Generative Adversarial Network (HiFi-AGAN)-based signal denoising system is developed for diminishing the background noises and supporting the speech enhancement process to retain the original clarity of the signal. The generator of HiFi-AGAN use stacked residual layer to selectively identify the essential speech information. Finally, the generator is trained to identify the appropriate speech using a multi-scale discriminator. The experimental analysis shows that the designed approach acquired higher perceptual quality and speech intelligibility on both TIMIT-corpus and LibriSpeech ASR corpus datasets. While considering the short-time objective intelligibility (STOI) at 20 db for dataset 1, the performance of the HiFi-AGAN is 26%, 18.9%, 11.9%, and 5.2% improved by existing baseline models. Similarly, for dataset 2, the STOI of the HiFi-AGAN is 27.9%, 20.6%, 15% and 5.8% better than others. Experimental outcomes proved that the designed HiFi-AGAN module performs better than traditional denoising methods using two different databases.

title: Eco-Friendly Carbon Dots Synthesized From Ipomoea Aquatica and Typha Angustifolia for Bioimaging and Sensing Applications
authors: Ashwini V, Ramadevi R, Jenila Rani D
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) have drawn much interest for their impressive optical, chemical, and structural diversity. In this paper, we demonstrate the green synthesis of carbon dots from a mixed plant extract of Ipomoea aquatica and Typha angustifolia through a hydrothermal approach. The optical characteristics, functional groups, and crystallinity of CDs produced were investigated by UV-Vis spectroscopy, Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), and X-ray diffraction (XRD), respectively. UV-Vis spectra exhibited a intense absorption band at 251 nm, typical of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi-\pi^{\ast}$</tex> transitions of C=C bonds, with a tail extended towards the visible region, suggesting potential <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{n}-\pi^{\ast}$</tex> transitions from surface carbonyl groups. FTIR spectra showed prominent peaks at 3321 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> (stretching of O-H), 2318-2106 cm-1 (stretching of C≡C or C≡N), 1637 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> (stretching of C=O), and 486 cm−1 (bending of C–H), indicating the abundance of oxygen functional groups from the plant metabolites. XRD patterns revealed broad diffraction peaks with prominent reflections at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \theta=9.11 \mathrm{o}$</tex>, 18.52o, 26.94o, indicating the formation of predominantly amorphous to semi-crystalline carbon structures at the nanoscale. These findings suggest that the synthesized CDs can be considered as promising candidates for potential applications in sensing, bioimaging, and environmental remediation.

title: Low-Latency FPGA Design of Multi-Channel Pipelined FFT Architecture
authors: Shaik Bilal Hafci, G Prasanna Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: In modern digital signal processing Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) architectures play an important role especially in communication systems and real-time signal analysis. There are many traditional FFT architectures which are foundation for developing nee pipelined designs that enhances the efficiency of the design. This paper presents a low latency multichannel parallel pipelined FFT architecture to achieve higher processing speed while reducing FPGA resource utilization. The proposed design is based on the deep pipelining with parallel butterfly processing units and efficient scheduling of Block RAM (BRAM) through a dedicated controller. The FPGA implementation of proposed design optimizes the resources. This architecture is the best fit for real-time signal processing applications, including Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) systems.

title: Edge–IoT Fabric for Zero-Downtime Predictive Fault Analytics in Sustainable and Resilient Manufacturing Systems
authors: C R Bharathi, Kolanchinathan V P, E. Balaji, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The modern manufacturing facilities require continuity of equipment functioning to ensure the sustainability, productivity, and operational reliability. Traditional cloud-based analytics also bring about latency, bandwidth consumption, and slow fault mitigation making them inapplicable to real-time industrial monitoring. The given paper suggests an Edge-IoT Fabric of Zero-Downtime Predictive Fault Analytics, a combination of multi-sensor IoT industrial nodes and an edge-intelligent processing layer to provide early detection of anomalies and autonomous correction of faults. An edge-deployed hybrid lightweight learning model is a combination of Temporal Convolutional Networks and an Edge- Optimized Gradient Boosting classifier, which is used to assist the low-latency inference. The realistic assessments with 420 streams of industrial sensors show that it can predict faults with 97.6 correct rates, reduce planned stoppage by 82 percent, and save 28 percent of energy, as opposed to cloud-only baselines. The system has a high scaling rate, low communication rate, and strong prediction ability, which is appropriate in the next generation manufacturing that is sustainable and resilient.

title: Comparison of Backpropagation Neural Networks and Gradient Boosting Algorithm in Accuracy Using Heart Disease Classification
authors: Kamil Dimililer, Elif Buse Duman, Omid Mirzaei, Priscilla Olawale, Dicle Sayın, Suleyman Asir
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Cardiovascular Diseases (CVDs) have become the leading cause of death worldwide. The early and accurate detection capability of machine learning algorithms significantly improves patient outcomes. This article presents a comparative analysis between two leading machine learning algorithms: Backpropagation Neural Networks (BPNN) and Gradient Boosting Algorithm (GBA). Using the widely benchmarked UCI Cleveland Heart Disease dataset, both algorithms were evaluated in terms of prediction accuracy and suitability for tabular clinical data. For BPNN and GBA, the obtained accuracy values were 43.2% and 98.1%, respectively. The two models differed by 54.9%, which was a significant difference. The outcomes demonstrate a distinct performance difference between the GBA and BPNN models. The findings also show that BPNN provides strong nonlinear modeling capabilities, while Gradient Boosting algorithms mostly demonstrate higher accuracy, computational efficiency, and resistance to overfitting in structured tabular datasets.

title: CognitiveShield-IoT: A Self-Evolving AI-Agent Driven Security Architecture for Adaptive and Resilient IoT Ecosystems
authors: Vishwas D B, Bharath M B, Trisha.V, Sahana.S, K Dinesh, Malepati Sampradha Reddy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) networks has introduced significant security challenges due to device heterogeneity, resource constraints, and evolving cyber threats. Existing security mechanisms largely rely on centralized architectures or static detection models, which suffer from high latency, limited scalability, privacy risks, and poor adaptability to zero-day attacks. To address these limitations, this paper pro-poses CognitiveShield-IoT, a distributed AI-agent driven security framework that integrates federated learning with reinforcement-based adaptive mitigation. The proposed system enables edge-level anomaly detection, privacy-preserving global model aggregation, and autonomous threat response through continuous policy optimization. Experimental evaluation using benchmark IoT intrusion datasets demonstrates superior performance, achieving 99.12% accuracy, reduced detection latency of 12.4 ms, and significantly lower communication overhead compared to baseline approaches. Reinforcement learning convergence analysis further confirms stable and adaptive mitigation behavior in dynamic attack scenarios. Future work will incorporate explainable AI modules and blockchain-assisted secure aggregation to enhance transparency, trust, and resilience in large-scale IoT security deployments.

title: Wildlife Detection and Classification using Spiking Neural Networks (SNN)
authors: K. L. Sailaja, Mandarapu Deepak Prem Kumar, Shaik Nagurbasha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Wildlife monitoring in remote and resource-constrained environments demands energy-efficient intelligent systems capable of sustained operation on battery-powered edge devices. Conventional deep learning models achieve high classification accuracy but incur substantial computational and power overhead, limiting their practical deployment in long-term field applications. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an energy-aware wildlife classification framework based on Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) implemented using snnTorch. The proposed architecture integrates Poisson rate encoding and Leaky Integrate-and-Fire neurons to enable sparse, event-driven temporal computation, significantly reducing redundant processing. A CNN baseline is developed under identical conditions to evaluate the accuracy–energy tradeoff. Experimental results on a four-class African wildlife dataset (Buffalo, Elephant, Rhino, Zebra) demonstrate that the SNN achieves 82.69% accuracy while consuming 4.21 watts per image with an inference time of 0.019 seconds, compared to 95.59% accuracy, 8.93 watts, and 0.012 seconds for the CNN—yielding a 52.85% reduction in power consumption. Although evaluation is conducted in a multi-class setting, the substantial energy savings establish the proposed SNN framework as a strong candidate for deployment in simplified binary(presence/absence) or risk-based wildlife detection systems requiring sustainable, long-term autonomous monitoring.

title: A Circular Canvas for Manufacturing MSMEs to Support the Transition Toward the Circular Economy
authors: Sandra Gonzales-Valencia, Wendi Chipana-Garcia, Trixy Rojas-Tunque, Elizabeth Vidal-Duarte
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises (MSMEs) in the manufacturing sector play a central role in developing countries economies but face significant barriers to adopting circular economy (CE) practices due to technological, financial, and operational constraints. While prior studies provide conceptual frameworks, readiness models, or barrier assessments, few offer operational artifacts that integrate lifecycle thinking with business model redesign tailored to MSMEs. This study proposes a Circular Canvas for Manufacturing MSMEs developed through a structured Design Science Research approach. The framework was constructed through systematic literature synthesis, gap identification, requirement definition, and modular architecture design. The resulting artifact integrates five interdependent modules: Environmental Assessment, Sustainable Design, Circular Market, Operations, and Economic Viability, explicitly linking Life Cycle Assessment diagnostics to business model reconfiguration and incremental transition pathways. Unlike existing circular business model frameworks, the proposed Canvas embeds environmental monitoring logic within managerial decision blocks and structures adoption into progressive maturity levels. By operationalizing circular transformation as a coherent, scalable management pathway rather than a conceptual abstraction, the framework contributes a practical tool to support technologyenabled sustainability transitions in resource-constrained manufacturing MSMEs.

title: Deep Learning-Based ECG Retrieval for Scalable Clinical Decision Support
authors: Varshika Rajesh, R. Prathanyaasree, Vishal. U, Snekhalatha. Umapathy
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Electrocardiography (ECG) is widely used for the diagnosis and monitoring of cardiovascular conditions. While deep learning has shown strong performance in ECG classification, most existing methods focus on label prediction and provide limited support for case-based clinical reasoning. This paper presents a deep learning-based framework for ECG signal retrieval designed for scalable clinical decision support systems. The proposed approach learns discriminative ECG representations using metric learning with a triplet loss-based neural network and retrieves clinically similar ECG signals based on distances in a learned embedding space. Experiments conducted on the PTB-XL dataset show that the proposed system achieves a Recall@10 of 0.955, Precision@10 of 0.681, and a Mean Reciprocal Rank of 0.789, hence retrieving ECG signals that are clinically relevant.

title: Credit Card Fraud Detection Using Lightgbm and Smote: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Balasubramanian V, Sathish V, Sriram R, Esakkiammal U, Parthiban K. G, Chitra S
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Frauds made by credit cards are a massive threat to credit card companies and customers, and billions of dollars are lost per year. In this paper, a machine learningbased solution to detect the presence of fraudulent credit card transactions will be identified based on the Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) algorithm together with the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) to deal with the issue of class imbalance in fraud detection data. Our approach involves careful data preprocessing, feature importance analysis, and hyperparameter optimization to attain optimal model performance. The experimental outcomes of a real-life credit card transaction data set indicate that our proposed approach is very accurate (99.95%), precise (98.51%), recall (82.00%), and F1-score (89.47%), and the Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC-ROC) is 0.9850. These findings are further confirmed by the use of stratified 5-fold cross-validation that results in a mean AUC-ROC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9823(\pm 0.0031)$</tex> of the data. In analysis of feature importance in terms of SHAP values, the analysis of PCA components shows the most relevant predictors of fraudulent activity are V14, V4, V12 and V10. It has been implemented as a web-based application coded in Flask and provides the option of real-time fraud detection. Our results demonstrate that LightGBM combined with SMOTE provides an effective and practical solution for credit card fraud detection.

title: IoT based Smart Water Distribution System for Equitable Supply in Rural Areas
authors: Hariharan R, Valarmathi K, Vijay Prakash.R, Dharshini S, Genga Parameswari H, Krishnaveni M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The IoT-based Smart Water Distribution System for Equitable Supply in Rural Area is developed to provide efficient and smart water management by monitoring water flow and detecting leaks in real time. The system uses three water flow sensors to calculate individual and total water consumption, which helps to detect the leakage point precisely. An ESP32 microcontroller is used as the main component, which connects to an I2C LCD to display real-time values of flow rates and leakages. The values are updated continuously on the ThingSpeak IoT platform, which helps to monitor and analyze water usage patterns remotely. When a leak is detected or a user consumes water beyond the set threshold limit, the system automatically sends an alert message to the user through the GSM module and turns off the solenoid valve to avoid water wastage. This smart system helps to conserve water, improve leak detection accuracy, and provide automated control for effective water management in residential and industrial applications.

title: A Real-Time Explainable Cybersecurity Framework for Detecting Known and Unknown Attacks in Wireless Sensor Network
authors: Modumpally Bharath, Manchikatla Pavithra, Ch. Sri Lakshmi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: WSNs are an essential component of the Industry 4.0 ecosystems that create an opportunity to sense in real-time, automate, and make intelligent decisions. Nonetheless, their spread, resource constrained and dynamic characteristics endow them with high susceptibility to advanced cyber-attacks, such as blackhole, grey hole, flooding, and time scheduling attacks. To overcome these issues, this paper is able to suggest a real-time, explainable, and adaptive cybersecurity framework that is capable of identifying known and unknown attacks within the industrial WSN contexts. The framework includes the use of Cooja-based network simulation to create realistic traffic with a simulated applied to simulated traffic and a hybrid intrusion detection pipeline utilizing supervised machine learning classifiers, unsupervised autoencoder-based anomaly detection, and deep learning-based CNN-LSTM temporal modeling. Moreover, a reinforcement learning module learns dynamically the best mitigation measures to reduce energy use, network outages and packet losses. Federated learning: Federated learning is a privacy preserving and scalable model update framework that allows distributed industrial deployments and Explainable AI elements that guarantee transparency and trust by offering interpretable insights of detection. The model was implemented using Python and TensorFlow in a GPU-enabled environment, achieving low inference latency suitable for real-time deployment. Statistical validation using 10-fold cross-validation confirms the robustness and reliability of the proposed framework.

title: A Real-Time Product Usage and Smart Reporting Platform Based on XGBoost and Transformer Models
authors: Erinç Samim Merdivenci, Besna Ruken Tarhan, Damla Doğru Babacan, Tuna Çakar
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This study introduces a smart reporting platform that helps e-commerce and digital marketing teams monitor campaign performance in real time and make data-driven decisions. In the platform, campaign logs, CRM data, and seasonal information were combined into a single analytics structure using Dremio and PySpark, and visual dashboards were built with Power BI. By extracting customer- and campaign-level features, we first used Random Forest and then XGBoost models to predict campaign response and short-term shopping tendency. The results show that XGBoost provides about 7 % higher accuracy and can predict future shopping periods with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy. During hightraffic periods such as Black Friday, the platform identified the best sending days and hours by sector. In A/B tests, interaction rates increased by 15 % on average. In addition, a transformerbased model (used via Hugging Face) grouped customers into meaning-based interest segments. With segment-matched campaigns, an extra <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5} \boldsymbol{\%} \mathbf{- 1 0 \%}$</tex> improvement was observed. Overall, this work presents a practical and scalable decisionsupport framework that combines modern data infrastructure with machine learning and natural language processing.

title: A Refined Ensemble–Neural Network Architecture for Robust QRS Detection in Noisy ECG Signals: A Hybrid Signal Processing and Deep Learning Approach
authors: Swetha S Kulkarni, Jijesh J J
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The QRS segment analysis contributes a major role in the analysis of electrocardiogram (ECG), variation in the heart rate analysis, and accurate arrhythmia diagnosis in the biomedical sector. The traditional method, such as Pan– Tompkins, shows interpretable results and reduced latency, but on the other hand, it affects extreme noise with non-standard morphologies. Deep Learning (DL) is a solution to overcome the setbacks of the traditional approach, which showcases high accuracy. However, it has the following disadvantages, such as high cost of computation, requirement of high-quality data sets and less explainability. The proposed method is a hybrid approach that involves a refined ensemble neural network (NN) architecture, which adds three traditional QRS detector methods and a one-dimensional traditional convolutional neural network for the purpose of the candidate refinement process. The working of the proposed system is such that Pan– Tompkins, Hamilton–Tompkins, and a first-derivative detector generate candidate R-peaks with the support of voting. In the next stage, a Convolutional Neural Network starts recovering missed beats and removes residual false positives using 250 ms of signal windows. The proposed model is implemented using the MIT–BIH Arrhythmia Database, and by adopting a refined ensemble NN, we obtain the value of positive predictive value (PPV) of 100% and an F1 score of 0.92, which shows superior performance of the model. A comparative analysis with traditional approaches, such as the Pan–Tompkins algorithm and strict ensemble methods, with the proposed model is carried out, and it is found that the F1 score of the Pan–Tompkins algorithm is 0.815, and the strict ensemble has a reduced F1 score of 0.60, which clearly depicts the high sensitivity and precision of the proposed model. Thus, the model is suggested to be used for ECG, heartbeat analysis, and arrhythmia diagnosis in the biomedical sector.

title: SenseAI: A Hybrid, Age-Stratified Cognitive Flexibility and Auditory Response Screening System for Early Autism Risk Detection
authors: Samindi Sankalpani, Sandeepa Karunathilaka, Hasini Ilanganthilake, Geethika Senawirathna, Samanthi Siriwardana, Mihiri Samaraweera
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) screening in early childhood remains limited by reliance on subjective caregiver reports and limited access to specialist assessment in low resource settings. This work introduces SenseAI, a develop mentally stratified, multimodal, and interpretable artificial in telligence framework for ASD risk screening in children aged 2-6.9 years. The system integrates tablet-based executive func tion paradigms-Go/No-Go inhibitory control and Dimensional Change Card Sort (DCCS) rule-switching-with automated Au ditory Response to Name (RTN) analysis derived from parent recorded videos. Using a clinically collected dataset <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(n=80)$</tex>, age-normalized behavioral features were extracted and modeled via calibrated Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers under child-level cross-validation. The cognitive module achieved AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.89$</tex> and sensitivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=90.0$</tex> demonstrated 77.78framework enhanced interpretability and clinical alignment. The offline-first architecture ensures feasibility in connectivity-limited environments. Findings support the potential of combining executivefunction biomarkers and auditory orienting behavior within an explainable AI framework for scalable and culturally adaptable ASD risk screening.

title: Enhanced Power Conversion using a Reduced Switch Multilevel Inverter for Renewable Energy Integration
authors: E. Parimalasundar, K. Suresh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved model of reduced-switch multilevel inverter configuration for addressing the need for reliable and small-sized high-efficiency converters in grid-tied and renewable applications. Recent developments in power electronics have focused on reducing the number of components while maintaining high power quality and efficiency. This study introduces a five-level reduced-switch multilevel inverter integrated with a DC–DC conversion stage. In comparison to a typical cascaded conventional H-bridge inverter, the introduced topology considerably decreases the number of active switches, leading to low conduction losses, switching stress, and gate driver effort. The sinusoidal pulse width modulation is used to obtain a smooth multilevel voltage synthesis in the inverter with lower harmonic distortion. The system is designed in MATLAB/SIMULINK and then simulated, and its performance is evaluated under resistive load and resistive–inductive load conditions. Quantitatively, the converter offers a power output around 3000 W at full load with an efficiency approaching 76% and a power loss of approximately 126 W, and it attains nearly 1150 W while operating at half-load with an efficiency of about 76.67%. The topology shows better efficiency and power quality than traditional structures. The developed converter is appropriate for renewable energy systems integration, on-grid power quality control, and industry application, demonstrating the feasibility and practicality.

title: Fusion-Based Deep Learning Framework for Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Yamini Aruna M, M. Mohamed Iqbal
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Accurate and early diagnosis of brain tumors is essential for effective treatment strategies, but manual analysis of medical images is a time-consuming process and also susceptible to human errors. In this paper, a fusionbased deep learning framework for automatic brain tumor classification using multimodal MRI and CT images is proposed. MRI images have high-resolution soft tissue contrast, and CT images have complementary structural density information. The proposed method combines a customized convolutional neural network (CNN) with a pretrained VGG16 model to extract complementary low-level and high-level features. Tumor-centered preprocessing and segmentation are used to improve region-of-interest learning. The fused features are classified into glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and normal classes using a fully connected classifier. The experimental results show that multimodal fusion outperforms single-model classification in terms of classification accuracy and robustness, thus proving the efficacy of the proposed framework for automated brain tumor diagnosis.

title: A Power-Optimized 9T SRAM Design Using CNTFET 32nm Scale Technology
authors: G. Anumitha, Jayanthi Srinivasan, Raja P, Agalya Jagateesan, Jayasri K
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: In a rapidly changing world, internet of things has become one of the top technologies that effortlessly connect humans and devices from anywhere and at any time. These IoT devices are small and are efficiently designed to operate on low power while processing the data within a few seconds. To store this data, SRAM plays a vital role as the main memory component in these systems. However, CMOS, based SRAM has issues with stability and power efficiency when it is operating in a small, scale regime. This system therefore, concentrates on the development of a new SRAM Design with the help of Carbon Field Effect Transistor to overcome these problems. This technology enhances both read and write power operations and still requires very little power. The findings indicate that the CNTFET, based SRAM is more efficient than the traditional design thereby making it an ideal solution for future low power IOT devices

title: An Advanced Data-Driven Framework for Reliable Wind Energy Prediction
authors: E. Narasimhulu, P. Sai Sampath Kumar, P. Sesi Kiran, B. Nidhinath, Y. Venkatesh, T Uday Kiran
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The main objective of this research proposal is the prediction of the generation of electrical power from wind energy through an intelligent machine learning approach. Wind energy is a clean and sustainable form of power generation. However, the generation of wind energy is extremely weather-dependent, with variations in wind speed, wind direction, and wind temperature. As a result, predicting wind power is a challenging task. However, through the application of machine learning approaches, it is possible to analyze past wind power data and predict future wind power. There are a total of four machine learning approaches employed in this research proposal. They are Linear Regression, Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting. Real-world wind data is employed in the research proposal. Preprocessing is done in the data in order to improve the accuracy of the predictions. According to the research proposal, ensemble models like Random Forest and Gradient Boosting perform well in terms of accuracy and mean absolute error in comparison with Linear Regression and SVR.

title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition using Facial Expression and Hand Gesture Analysis for Safety Applications
authors: Anand Thota, N. Devi Sri, K. Dinesh, A. Prasannatha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Emotion recognition plays a crucial role in intelligent safety monitoring systems, mental health assessment, and human–computer interaction. This paper presents a real-time multimodal emotion recognition system that integrates facial expression analysis and hand gesture recognition to detect distress conditions and trigger emergency alerts. Facial emotions are recognized using a pre-trained convolutional neural network (CNN) model trained on the FER2013 dataset and deployed in ONNX format for efficient inference. Hand gestures are analyzed using contour detection and the Convex Hull algorithm to identify distress-related gestures such as a clenched fist. A decision-level fusion strategy ensures that alerts are triggered only when both facial and gesture cues indicate distress, thereby reducing false positives. Experimental validation was conducted on five participants under real-time conditions using a standard laptop webcam. The system achieved reliable real-time performance with an average detection latency of approximately 180–220 ms per frame. The proposed framework demonstrates effectiveness for safety surveillance, assistive systems, and emergency response applications.

title: Towards Intelligent Network Security: An AI-Based Intrusion Detection System
authors: Praveen Katha, Santhoshkumar S. P, Manohar G, Kamalakannan Machap, Nalini T, Vikram D
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The fast pace of interconnected systems and internet-based services has amplified the level of complexity of cyber-attacks, thereby making the conventional signature-based intrusion detection systems ineffective in dealing with the changing threats. In order to overcome such shortcomings, this paper presents a smart AI-based Intrusion Detection System (IDS) that uses Machine Learning (ML) to increase security. With the NSL-KDD data, the framework trains both normal and malicious network behavior on the basis of extensive data preprocessing such as feature transformation, noise elimination, normalization and feature selection to minimize computational cost. Several Machine Learning (ML) classifiers also trained and tested based on their standard performance measures. The experimental evidence indicates that the proposed IDS has a strong attack recognition capability with limited false alarms, and the use of Random Forest is more effective than other models. The given solution is a scalable, adaptive, and reliable one that provides a solution to securing the modern networks against complex cyber threats.

title: Next-Generation MIMO Transceivers for Integrated Sensing and Communications: Unique Security Vulnerabilities and Solutions
authors: Kawon Han, Christos Masouros, Taneli Riihonen, Moeness G. Amin
publish: Proceedings of the IEEE
abstract: Integrated sensing and communications (ISAC), which are recognized as a key enabler for sixth generation (6G), have brought new opportunities for intelligent, sustainable, and connected wireless networks. Multiple-input–multiple-output (MIMO) transceiver technology lies at the core of this paradigm, providing the degrees of freedom required for simultaneous data transmission and accurate radar sensing. The tight integration of sensing and communication (S&C) introduces unique security vulnerabilities that extend beyond conventional physical-layer security (PLS). In particular, high-power transmissions directed at sensing targets may empower adversarial eavesdroppers, whereas passive interception of ISAC echoes can reveal sensitive information such as target locations and mobility patterns. This article presents an overview of recent advances in MIMO ISAC transceiver design, considering transmitter perspectives, receiver architectures, and full-duplex implementations. We examine MIMO transceiver designs under unique security threats specific to ISAC and highlight emerging countermeasures, including secure signaling design, interference exploitation, and transceiver optimization under adversarial conditions. Finally, we discuss challenges and research opportunities for developing secure ISAC systems in next-generation wireless networks.

title: Electrokinetic and Antibiofilm Effects of Silver Nanoparticles Combined with Imipenem Against multidrug-resistant of Klebsiella pneumoniae
authors: Inglid Fontoura, Maiara Lima Castilho
publish: IEEE Transactions on NanoBioscience
abstract: Antimicrobial resistance has been recognized as a global public health problem, contributing to high rates of morbidity, mortality and costs associated with infectious diseases. In 2024, the World Health Organization identified carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae (K. pneumoniae) as the pathogen of greatest importance for research and development of new drugs. In this context, the work analyzes the association of silver nanoparticles with the carbapenem antibiotic Imipenem (IPM) in a multidrug-resistant (MDR) clinical isolate of K. pneumoniae, in comparison to susceptible isolate. The silver nanoparticles (AgNPs) were synthesized using the bottom-up method and characterized through UV-Vis spectroscopy, dynamic light scattering, infrared spectroscopy and X-ray diffraction. Antimicrobial susceptibility tests were carried out on planktonic cells and biofilms. The pH, zeta potential and electrophoretic mobility analysis of the bacteria and antimicrobials was carried out at concentrations identical to those used in the antimicrobial susceptibility assay, aiming to understand the mechanism of internalization and synergistic potentiation. The combination of imipenem (IPM) and AgNPs exhibited an inhibitory effect on planktonic cells and biofilms of the MDR clinical isolate of K. pneumoniae. The surfaces of the bacterial isolates and antimicrobials displayed a negative surface charge. From these results and insights into bacterial electrokinetics, an anionic influx of the antimicrobials through the porin channels can be proposed, leading to increased permeability. The association of silver nanoparticles with IPM was shown to restore the efficacy of the carbapenem antibiotic against MDR K. pneumoniae.

title: High-Frequency Harmonic Suppression of Low-Inductance PMSM Based on Adaptive Bus Voltage Modulation Using Extremum Seeking and Predictive Control
authors: Lirong Deng, Haifeng Zhang, Peiru Sun, Shiqiang Zheng, Baodong Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Low-inductance permanent magnet sync hronous motors (PMSMs) are prone to significant high-frequency current harmonics when driven by pulse-width modulation (PWM) inverters. In magnetically levitated turbomolecular pump (MLTMP) applications, these harmonics lead to increased losses, temperature rise, torque ripple, and may even deteriorate magnetic bearing stability. To address this issue, an adaptive DC bus voltage modulation strategy based on extremum seeking control and cascaded deadbeat predictive control (ESC-CDPC) is proposed. A front-end buck converter is employed to enable variable bus voltage, and a model-free ESC algorithm continuously generates the optimal voltage reference by minimizing a composite cost function balancing efficiency and modulation constraints. The CDPC algorithm ensures fast and low-complexity voltage tracking, while time-scale separation guarantees stable coordinated operation. Compared with conventional fixed and dynamic voltage schemes, the proposed strategy optimizes voltage utilization and achieves source-level suppression of high-frequency harmonics. Experimental results show that the current total harmonic distortion decreases from 49.55% and 28.59% to 17.92%, the winding and housing temperatures drop by 4.4 and 3.5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^\boldsymbol{\circ}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, and the input power is reduced, demonstrating improved energy efficiency and performance of MLTMP systems.

title: Low Scan Loss Metalens Antenna by On- and Off-Axis Phase Distribution Correction
authors: Junjie Zhang, Shanggang Lin, Suiyan Tan, Qun Lou
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Beam scanning of metalens antennas often suffers from large scan loss. This paper presents a low scan loss metalens antenna which is designed by an optimization method. The optimization method includes the quantization of phase distribution in the optimization loop, where the actual phase distribution is determined based on the phase-shift database of the unit cells. The phase distribution is parameterized as an ideal thin-lens plus a low-dimensional aberration correction. The correction terms are tuned using a multi-start derivative-free Nelder–Mead search. Two cases are considered, that is, an on-axis beam and a 30° off-axis beam. To obtain low scan-loss, this optimization maximizes the gains in these two cases with smoothness regularization to suppress abrupt state transitions. Four-layer Jerusalem-cross unit cells were simulated and extracted to build the phase-shift database. Through the optimization, a metalens antenna operating at 10 GHz with the size of 225 mm × 225 mm was designed. Measurement indicates a gain of 21.0 dBi for on-axis beam and 20.3 dBi for 30° beam, corresponding to 0.7 dB scan loss. This metalens antenna along with the optimization method may be potential for applications that require low scan loss and simple architecture.

title: PVCA: Privacy-Preserving and Verifiable Cross-System Authorization for Platoon Communications in VANETs_supp1-3697662.pdf
authors: Yang Ming
publish: N/A
abstract: Vehicle platoon, an increasingly significant technology in vehicular ad hoc networks (VANETs), effectively reduces energy consumption and environmental pollution, mitigates traffic congestion, and enhances road capacity and traffic safety. Collaborative communication between platoons is available to promote the reliability of data dissemination, ameliorate driving strategies, and further strengthen traffic efficiency. Nevertheless, existing efforts for secure multi-platoon data dissemination still face the following issues: (i) lack of a direct authorization strategy for platoons working on different systems; (ii) no effective mechanism to protect vehicle privacy during platoon communications; (iii) absence of a practical verification approach for cross-system ciphertext transformation. This paper builds a privacy-preserving and verifiable cross-system authorization (PVCA) scheme to mitigate the above issues. Specifically, we first put forth the optimized anonymous identity-based broadcast encryption (IBBE) and policy-hiding attribute-based encryption (ABE) protocols to adapt platoon communications while protecting identity and attribute privacy. After that, the authorized token bridging the proposed protocols is designed to enable ciphertexts of IBBE format to be transformed into new ciphertexts of ABE format, enabling flexible authorization. Furthermore, inspired by the Fujisaki-Okamoto transformation, we devise an efficient zero knowledge proof of knowledge protocol, making our PVCA achieve verifiability and fairness. Rigorous security proof and analysis demonstrate that our PVCA is not only secure against chosen plaintext attacks and collusion attacks, but also satisfies the necessary security requirements. We implement a prototype of PVCA (on a desktop computer and Raspberry Pi) and provide a simulation utilizing NS-2 to validate its feasibility for platoon communications in VANETs. The results indicate that, compared to the state-of-the-art ciphertext transformation solutions, PVCA reduces the running time for original ciphertext generation, transformation, and decryption by at least 91.57%, 49.87%, and 50%, respectively, while compressing the original ciphertext and authorization token sizes by over 78.07% and 15.20%.
