title: Active Momentum Control for Spinal In-Pipe Robots in Microgravity: On-Orbit Validation
authors: Shenyao Feng, Yu He, Hui Li, Xiang Wang, Qiang Huang, Xiao Huang, Zhihong Jiang, Ye Tian, Pei Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Locomotion in confined microgravity environments, such as spacecraft piping, presents unique challenges due to the lack of gravitational normal forces and the strict requirement for environmental safety. Traditional rigid-body controllers often struggle to generate sufficient traction for agile maneuvering without violating the structural limits of fragile surroundings. This article introduces a novel control framework for a 6-link spinal wheeled-legged robot that actively exploits internal spinal dynamics to steer the system. We propose a lie-algebraic error-state model predictive control that plans optimal momentum fluxes and spinal configurations, ensuring singularity-free tracking even during omnidirectional tumbling. To guarantee safety, a hierarchical whole-body controller enforces strict environment-admissible force constraints and integrates an event-triggered asymmetric friction adaptation strategy to handle uncertain surface properties. Uniquely, the proposed framework is validated through real-world on-orbit experiments using a flight-equivalent pipe testbed aboard the China Space Station. Results demonstrate that our method successfully achieves agile heading recovery where rigid-body baselines fail to converge due to traction loss, while strictly maintaining contact forces within the 6.6 N safety limit despite friction uncertainties.

title: Improved Remote Error Estimation Method for Smart Meters with Advances in Loss Calculation, Measurement Synchronization, and Solution Stability
authors: Zemin Qu, Yang Jiao, Hongbin Li, Qing Chen, Xin Liu, YuXuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Accurate remote estimation of smart meter errors is crucial for ensuring the reliability of metering data, thereby enabling fair electricity trading and supporting advanced applications such as load forecasting. Existing remote smart meter error estimation methods face key issues, including loss calculation, measurement synchronization, and solution stability, which lead to low estimation reliability. To address these issues, an improved remote error estimation method for smart meters is proposed in this paper. A functionally equivalent calculation of line losses is achieved by describing the two-stage power profile within each metering period. To mitigate estimation deviations caused by measurements asynchrony, the metering period is expanded, and the optimal expansion length is derived from the analysis of energy measurement imbalances within the metering periods. Furthermore, the precision-driven adaptive regulations strategy for the coefficient matrix is employed to suppress model ill-conditioning, enabling accurate and stable remote estimation of metering errors. The effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method are validated through verification experiments.

title: A Single-pass Multistatic SAR Satellites Formation Design Method for TomoSAR Data Acquisition
authors: Sisi Dong, Weidong Yu, Jili Wang, Yulun Wu, Qiang Lin, Chunle Wang, Xiaoxue Jia
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar tomography (TomoSAR) enables three-dimensional SAR imaging. However, most spaceborne TomoSAR data is currently acquired through repeated passes of a single satellite, during which the atmospheric and deformation phases complicate processing and degrade height accuracy. Satellite formations provide a potential solution by deploying multiple satellites flying in close proximity to simultaneously capture SAR images from different perspectives, forming multiple baselines. This paper proposes a multistatic SAR satellite formation design method for single-pass TomoSAR data acquisition. This method employs the equivalent phase center (EPC) approximation to explore the relationship between the number of satellites and EPCs, determining the optimal satellite number and transceiver configurations. Specifically, only two satellites are designated as T&R satellites equipped with both transmitters and receivers, while the rest are R satellites carrying receivers only. Both odd- and even-numbered satellite formations are considered, with the two T&R satellites placed at the outermost positions to enhance tomographic resolution. To maximize the unambiguous tomographic range, a parameter design method is developed to ensure uniform EPC spacing, resulting in uniformly distributed baselines. In addition, two formation schemes, T2R3-Helix and T2R4-Helix, are provided as examples. The method provides a viable approach for future TomoSAR data acquisition and broadens the application prospects of low-mass, low-cost small satellites.

title: Design and Fabrication of a Compact Waterjet Thruster for Deep-Sea Applications
authors: Ning Dayong, Liangjia Wang, Liang Gangda, Zhilei Liu, Wang Jinhui, Jiaoyi Hou
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: In the context of the growing demand for compact equipment in deep-sea exploration missions, the development of lightweight propulsion systems is confronted with considerable challenges. In view of the necessity to resist high pressure, the design of deep-sea thrusters generally incorporates a bulky configuration. To address this technical bottleneck, this study proposes a novel compact deep-sea thruster based on electromagnetic soft-coupled actuation. The thruster comprises a body support structure, an electromagnetic drive mechanism, and a soft-bodied water bladder. The mechanism in question generates periodic propulsion forces by altering the volume of the bladder in response to pulsed electrical signals. This study investigates the propulsion force and operational cycles of the thruster under varying structural parameters and control currents. A pressure tank is employed to validate the thruster’s pressure resistance, and two typical operational configurations are designed to test its propulsion effectiveness. The findings indicate that the thruster functions reliably at depths in excess of 11 000 m. It has been demonstrated that the two typical configurations are capable of attaining swimming speeds of 92.7 mm/s (1.55 BL/s) and 68.2 mm/s (1.17 BL/s), respectively, thereby demonstrating exceptional propulsion capabilities.

title: AG-STDF: Anatomy-Guided ECG Spatiotemporal Dynamical Fusion for Screening Coronary Microvascular Dysfunction_supp1-3698945.docx
authors: Qinghua Sun
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: Coronary slow flow (CSF), present in 1-7% of coronary angiograms, occurs in patients with angiographically normal epicardial arteries and is associated with acute coronary syndrome, representing an unmet need in coronary microvascular dysfunction (CMD) management. This study proposes an anatomy-guided spatiotemporal dynamical fusion (AG-STDF) framework for CSF screening. Methods: AG-STDF integrates anatomical electrophysiological territory mapping with 12-lead electrocardiogram (ECG) dynamics. ECG signals are decomposed into coronary territories-left anterior descending (LAD), left circumflex (LCX), and right coronary artery (RCA)—and static morphology is fused with intrinsic dynamics (derived from dynamic modeling) using joint recurrence quantification and multi-scale decomposition. Results: AG-STDF achieves an AUROC of 0.9467 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.0272 for CSF detection. At the vessel level, where therapeutic decisions are made per 2024 European Society of Cardiology guidelines, AG-STDF predicts corrected thrombolysis in myocardial infarction frame counts (CTFC) with an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{\text{2}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> = 0.5951 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.0666 (AUROC: 0.8428). Notably, AG-STDF performs steadily in right-dominant coronary patients, with favorable results in LAD (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{\text{2}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> = 0.6753) and mild fluctuations in LCX (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{\text{2}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> = 0.5673) and RCA (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{\text{2}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> = 0.5296) due to anatomical variations and competitive perfusion. Anatomical constraints and spatiotemporal fusion improve AUROC by 3.5% (p <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$< $</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.01) and 9.47% (p <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$< $</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.05). When equipped with an Intel Core i5-10500 and NVIDIA GTX 1660S, AG-STDF processes a 10-second ECG segment in 19.37 s. Conclusion: AG-STDF allows accurate, non-invasive CSF detection and vessel-level assessment in right-dominant populations. Significance: It serves as a cost-effective CMD screening tool that may reduce invasive testing and support early diagnosis.

title: Phoneme-Based Accent-Adaptive Speech Recognition for Accurate and Mispronunciation Feedback
authors: G. W. C. K. Fernando, A. I. Meegoda, L. A. D. Sanjana, D. G. S. Dilshan, M. P. Gunathilake, D. Kasthurirathna
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The increasing use of online meeting platforms has intensified the need for clear spoken communication, particularly among non-native English speakers. While modern Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems provide accurate transcription, they offer limited support for pronunciation assessment in spontaneous multi-speaker environments. This paper presents a hybrid accent-adaptive pronunciation detection framework for post-meeting analysis of South Asian English speech. The proposed system integrates commercial ASR transcription with a fine-tuned Whisper model to improve accent robustness, and combines word-level confidence scoring with phoneme-level alignment using the Montreal Forced Aligner and the CMU Pronouncing Dictionary. A hybrid decision mechanism incorporating confidence thresholds and phoneme deviation analysis is further enhanced with an accent-aware filtering strategy to reduce false positives. Experimental evaluation on multi-speaker meeting recordings demonstrates a significant reduction in false positive rate and improved pronunciation detection accuracy. The framework maintains computational efficiency suitable for post-meeting deployment while providing interpretable, sound-level diagnostic feedback. The results confirm that hybrid confidence-phoneme integration offers a practical and scalable solution for automated post-meeting pronunciation feedback in multi-speaker online meeting environments.

title: Dual-Channel LLM-Guided Semantic Feature Extraction with Zero-Shot Classification for Zero-Day Malware Detection
authors: Ahmed Essaa Al-Owaidi, Galip Cansever
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Zero-day malware remains a significant challenge for conventional detection systems due to its ability to evade signature-based models and exhibit previously unseen behavioral patterns. This paper proposes a dual-channel semantic detection framework guided by a large language model to address this challenge. The framework extracts complementary representations from static code artifacts and dynamic behavioral traces, where both channels are encoded using CodeBERT, a transformer-based model pre-trained on source code and programming-related text. The resulting semantic embeddings are fused into a unified representation that enables zero-shot classification of malware families without requiring prior exposure to the target class. The proposed method is evaluated on five publicly available datasets-Malware Dataset, Windows Malware Dataset, Network Malware Detection Dataset, CTU IoT Traffic, and IoT Botnet Traffic-under a strict family-exclusion protocol to simulate realistic zero-day conditions. Experimental results show that the framework achieves known-class detection accuracy ranging from 98.88% to 99.34%, while maintaining zero-day detection accuracy between 96.38% and 98.03%. Ablation studies demonstrate that the dual-channel design consistently outperforms single-channel variants, and comparisons with recent state-of-the-art approaches confirm improved robustness and generalization. These findings indicate that integrating code-level and behavior-level semantics through CodeBERTdriven fusion provides an effective and scalable strategy for detecting previously unseen malware families.

title: Design of an Asymmetric Converter for 6/4 Switched Reluctance Motor
authors: S. Allirani, B.S.Vaishnavi, S.M.Sawmiya, A.K.Sai Vasanth
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Switched Reluctance Motors are commonly employed in electric vehicles and industrial drives because of their simplicity and fault tolerance. But the high torque ripple due to the non-linear magnetic core characteristics is a drawback that restricts the performance. This paper proposes the design and simulation study of an asymmetric bridge converter for a 6/4 Switched Reluctance Motor with the objective of torque ripple reduction. A closed-loop control system incorporating PI and PID controllers is developed and simulated using MATLAB/Simulink. The torque performance is studied before and after employing the proposed control method. The simulation study reveals that the application of PID control has a substantial effect on torque ripple reduction and torque smoothness compared to the PI control method.

title: Automated Brain Tumor Segmentation in Multi-Parametric MRI using nnU-Net
authors: D.Ganesh, P.Venkateswarlu Reddy, Neeruganti Vidya Sree, Thotla Chakra Ganesh, Pudi Nikhil Surya Teja, Galam Dhanunjaneya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Accurate brain tumor segmentation from MRI forms an integral part of diagnosis, treatment planning, and disease monitoring. Manual segmentation is really time-consuming and prone to inter-observer variability, hence motivating the need for automated solutions. This paper reports a fully automated deep learning-based framework for brain tumor segmentation using nnU-Net for multi-parametric MRI data: T1, T1-contrast enhanced (T1-CE), T2, and FLAIR modalities. The idea borrowed the self-configuring capability of nnU-Net, meaning it automatically adjusts network architecture, preprocessing, and strategies for training based on dataset characteristics, without requiring manual tuning of hyperparameters. The model was evaluated on the BraTS dataset and achieved Dice similarity coefficients of 0.92 for whole tumor (WT), 0.89 for tumor core (TC), and 0.87 for enhancing tumor (ET). Experimental results on accurate boundary delineation and strong generalization capability have shown improved performances compared to conventionally used U-Net-based approaches. The proposed approach provides a robust, reproducible solution for automated brain tumor segmentation and is thus prospective in computer-aided diagnosis integrations into clinical environments.

title: When to Fly: A Passenger Decision-Support System Based on Climate-Sensitive Flight Disruption Likelihood
authors: Nasim Ahmadzadeh Nobari Azar, Mohammad Karami
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Flight status is significantly impacted by adverse weather and poor air quality conditions, which in turn increase the likelihood of disruptions and cancellations. Despite this, most airline booking systems do not explicitly estimate the risk associated with weather or air-quality forecasts and do not inform travelers. This study introduces a climate-sensitive scheduling framework designed to recommend safer departure times, alternative routing options, or short delays to help reduce disruption risk in the airline industry for travellers. To develop and test the framework, the flight-level data were collected from the U.S. Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) for the JFK-LAX route in January 2024, combined with hourly meteorological and air-quality data from Open-Meteo. A gradient-boosted model was then trained to estimate the probability of flight disruptions. The results point to daily “no-fly window” that reflects how weather and air-quality conditions increase the likelihood of flight cancellations. This tool is to support travelers at the booking stage, guiding them to choose flights with lower risk and avoid missed connections. It is especially useful for patients traveling internationally, where disruptions can delay urgent medical care. This study provides insights from different managerial perspectives.

title: Low-Altitude UAV Tracking via Sensing-Assisted Predictive Beamforming
authors: Yifan Jiang, Qingqing Wu, Hongxun Hui, Wen Chen, Derrick Wing Kwan Ng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Sensing-assisted predictive beamforming shows significant promise for enhancing various future unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) applications in integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems. However, the impact of such beamforming technique on the communication reliability was largely unexplored and challenging to characterize. To fill this research gap and tackle this issue, this paper proposes a cellular-connected UAV tracking scheme leveraging extended Kalman filtering (EKF), where the predicted UAV trajectory, sensing duration ratio, and target constant received signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) are jointly optimized to maximize the outage capacity at each time slot. To address the implicit nature of the objective function, analytical outage probability (OP) approximations are proposed based on second-order Taylor expansions, providing an efficient and full characterization of outage capacity. Subsequently, an efficient algorithm is proposed based on a combination of bisection search and successive convex approximation (SCA) to address the non-convex optimization problem with guaranteed convergence. To further reduce computational complexity, a second efficient algorithm is developed based on alternating optimization (AO). Simulation results validate the accuracy of the derived OP approximations, the effectiveness of the proposed algorithms, and the significant outage capacity enhancement over various benchmarks. Furthermore, we show that the optimized predicted UAV trajectory tends to be parallel to the base station’s uniform linear array antennas with a nonzero minimum distance, indicating a trade-off between decreasing path loss and enjoying wide beam coverage for outage capacity maximization.

title: IEEE Standard for the Qualification of Switchgear Assemblies for Class 1E Applications in Nuclear Power Generating Stations
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: The methods and requirements for qualifying switchgear assemblies for indoor areas outside of the containment in nuclear power generating stations are described in this document. These assemblies include: metal-enclosed low-voltage power circuit breaker switchgear assemblies, as defined in IEEE Std C37.20.1; metal-clad switchgear assemblies, as defined in IEEE Std C37.20.2; metal-enclosed bus, as defined in IEEE Std C37.23; and metal-enclosed interrupter switchgear assemblies, as defined in IEEE Std C37.20.3.

title: Performance Evaluation of Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Ensemble Approaches in Diabetes Prediction
authors: Swarnali Daw, Bingshati Mondal, Devadeepa Chakraborty, Suparna Paul, Ishika Halder, Bidyajani Maji
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus has emerged as one of the most alarming health concerns all over the world, indicating the importance of error-free and early detection systems. Here, we present a relative study of multiple Machine Learning and Deep learning algorithms applied to the Pima Indian Diabetes dataset. Traditional classifiers, including K-Nearest Neighbors, Support Vector Classifier, Random Forest, and AdaBoost, were evaluated with SMOTE and cross-validation. The Deep Neural Network (DNN) is combined with Explainable AI (XAI) methods. The inclusion of ensemble learning approaches can elevate predictive performance by integrating the strengths of different models. In this paper, the above three models are implemented, and their ability to predict diabetic patients is relatively studied based on accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. A comparison table is developed to show these results .Through this comparative study we have observed that Random Forest demonstrates the topmost level of accuracy. These findings would guide future research efforts in Diabetes diagnosis.

title: Speech-to-Sign Language Translator
authors: Dinky Khurana, Jasmine Kaur, Baishnavi Patsani, Amit Vajpayee
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Polysemy between the hearing-impaired and the hearing-impaired people has been another great challenge considering that there is very little real-time interpretation of the sign language. Current speech-to-sign translation systems are characterized by a very long latency, limited vocabulary and poor contextual awareness. The current paper suggests a speech to sign language translator that works in real-time and gains its effectiveness results on the basis of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and deep learning methods to increase its accuracy and responsiveness. The suggested system takes the form of initially translating verbal information into text through an automatic speech recognition component and then analyzes the meaning of the utterances with NLP to avoid a loss of context. The model is then a deep learning program to map the processed text into the sign language representation. The system will also aid in the scalability of the vocabulary and effective generation of gestures to be able to provide a more fluent and natural generation of signs. Experimental analysis shows that it is more accurate in translation and the time of response is less than the traditional approaches. The suggested system is a workable and scalable model of inclusive communication and may be used effectively in an educational, healthcare, and public service setting.

title: A Predictive Analytics Framework for Healthcare Resource Optimization using Hospital Operational Data
authors: R. Varaprasad, A. Sreepradha, Gante Jahnavi, Alina Zoya, Gangula Kavya Sri, Bogathi Akhila
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The efficient prediction and allocation of healthcare resources play an essential role in the efficient management of the hospital capacity especially in the intensive care unit (ICU) where uncertainty in the demands may result in shortage or underutilization of resources. The paper proposes a predictive analytics model with respect to healthcare resources optimization based on aggregated operational data of a hospital. The model integrates time series forecasting based on ensembles, optimization-based resource allocation and explainable artificial intelligence that may be applied to the end-to-end support of hospital operations. XGBoost-based predictive model offers the option to predict the bed demand in the ICU with respect to the non-linear demand and time dynamics. Experimental evaluation is performed on the availability of the publicly released dataset of the COVID-19 Reported Patient Impact and Hospital Capacity published by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), over the period between August 2020 and June 2022 and containing 70,128 daily state-level observation across 50 states in the US. The proposed model achieves about 19% and 24% reduction in mean absolute error (MAE) and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE), respectively, with the best baseline. The demand forecasts are then fed to a constrained optimization module to reduce the average bed shortage by 66% and increase the utilization efficiency. SHAP-based explainability is used to give global and local attribution of the most important operational drivers that influence demand predictions, resulting in increased transparency and trust. The proposed framework provides an interpretable, reproducible and scalable solution for proactive healthcare resources planning.

title: Visualization Beyond Summarization: An AI-Infused Context-Aware Method to Make Sustainability Content Digestible
authors: Mahisha Gunasekaran, I-Han Hsiao
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Despite growing urgency around climate change and environmental action, sustainability-related content remains difficult for the general public to access and understand. Complex research articles, jargon-heavy reports, and intricate visualizations often act as barriers to engagement. This work introduces an AI-infused approach to simplify dense sustainability content by automatically extracting key environmental insights and presenting them through clear, digestible data visualizations. Our method leverages contextual cues to interpret the content semantics and guide the selection of appropriate chart types, color schemes, and design elements that reflect the semantic structure of the content. We evaluated our system using the LLM-as-a-judge framework across multiple criteria, including factual accuracy, clarity, visual appropriateness, and design rationale. Quantitative metrics such as coverage, precision, and recall were also adopted to evaluate the outcome. Our findings show that injecting contextual information into the AI model significantly improves both the clarity and interpretability of the generated visualizations, thereby advancing accessibility in sustainability content interpretation.

title: A Learning-Based Fault Detection Approach for Unmanned Aerial Systems Using Inertial Sensor Data
authors: Furkan Yuksel, Suleyman Tosun
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Current fault detection methods often exceed the computational limits of small-scale unmanned systems, creating a need for lightweight, sensor-efficient diagnostic solutions. In this paper, we introduce a low-cost, embedded fault detection approach for unmanned systems based on inertial measurement unit (IMU) data. We focus on a quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) using a micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS) IMU. Our method employs an unsupervised, anomaly-based learning algorithm to model healthy operational behavior from sensor outputs. The system identifies deviations from this behavior as potential faults. Designed for resource-limited platforms, the algorithm operates in real-time on an onboard microcontroller. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves reliable diagnostics using only IMU data, eliminating the need for labeled fault samples or complex sensor fusion.

title: Design of an Ultra-Wide Stopband CSRR Microstrip Bandpass Filter for L-Band Applications
authors: Emre Salim, Yiğit Parlak, Sultan Can
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: In this study, a microstrip filter design was implemented. To improve the performance parameters of the design, a Complementary Split Ring Resonator (CSRR) structure is integrated into the ground plane of the design. The aim was to improve bandwidth, selectivity and bandstop suppression results. It was demonstrated that filter exhibits bandpass characteristics with a center frequency of 1.71 GHz, a bandwidth of 24.56 %, an insertion loss of 1.15 dB, and a return loss of 24.7 dB, and shows bandstop characteristics between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.24-6.4 \text{GHz}$</tex> frequencies according to a -10 dB value. Current density analyses of the design were performed. CSRR structure improved parameter values in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.57-1.96 \text{GHz}$</tex> band. By comparing the proposed topology to recent filter studies in the literature in terms of compactness, bandwidth, loss, performance, harmonic suppression, the limited or enhanced features were clearly highlighted. Proposed CSRRloaded structure provides an effective solution for suppressing higher-order harmonics and achieving a ultra-wide stopband performance, all while maintaining high transmission efficiency and low insertion loss in the bandpass.

title: YOLOv11-FD: A Hybrid Approach Integrating Mamba and Frequency Analysis for Wind Turbine Blade Damage Detection
authors: Ye Liu, Chenggang Yin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: Wind turbine blade damage detection is critical for ensuring stable operation of wind farms. To address the issues of limited global perception and low accuracy in detecting small objects, a YOLOv11-based method improved from a frequency-domain perspective, named YOLOv11-FD, is proposed. First, a frequency decoupling Mamba feature extraction module (TViM) is designed to enhance the model’s global perception capability. Second, the C2PSA module is augmented with efficient discriminative frequency domain-based feedforward network (EDFFN) to form the C2PE module, which improves the model’s ability to detect small targets. Third, the Converse2D operator is used to refine the upsampling module, which enables the module to better recover information lost during downsampling. Fourth, a novel FPIoU loss function is proposed, which combines Focaler-IoU and PIoU v2 methods to improve the overall performance of the model. The proposed method is evaluated on a dataset of wind turbine blade damage images. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLOv11-FD outperforms YOLOv11s, not only achieving an mAP@50 of 96.76% and an mAP@50-95 of 78.44%, but also exhibiting reduced localization and classification uncertainty under repeated measurements. The novel YOLOv11-FD model proposed in this research is beneficial for automated damage detection of wind turbine blades and similar inspection scenarios.

title: Framework for Privacy-Preserving Synthetic Medical Image and Report Generation
authors: Sriramkumar R, Nilesh Ashtankar, M.Lakshmanan, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, R.Tamilamuthan, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a new promising method that allows the training of privacy-preserving medical AI systems, as it collaborates on the model without exchanging raw patient data. However, it is still true that the majority of the federated learning frameworks are monomodal and do not take advantage of the complementary data of medical images and radiology text. Also, the data-sharing limitations together with the small number of annotated datasets available for training deep learning models, severely limit their application in clinical settings and their generalization capabilities. This paper reports a Federated Vision–Language and Diffusion Framework (FVL-Diffusion) project that collaborates and integrates different modalities in representation learning as well as in the generation of privacy-preserving synthetic data across the distributed medical centers. The proposed architecture consists of the federated vision–language encoder and the diffusion-based generative model, such that each client hospital is allowed to train locally on the paired MRI/CT images and the corresponding radiology reports. The only thing that is being sent over the wire are the model updates— not the sensitive data—using the secure aggregation and differential privacy techniques. Global diffusion model generates high-fidelity medical images and text that are privacy-compliant alternatives to real patient data and that enhance the downstream classification and segmentation performance. Experimental results using data from different institutions have demonstrated that FVL-Diffusion diagnostics accuracy increases, the influence of data heterogeneity is narrowed down, and the system is made privacy leak-proof to a great extent. The framework showcases a scalable, secure, and clinically relevant route for federated multimodal medical AI systems' development.

title: A Hybrid Residual-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention Model for Lake Water Level Forecasting
authors: Meliha Mutlu, Mete Celik, Meltem Kacikoc, Filiz Dadaser-Celik
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Lake water level forecasting is crucial for water resource planning and management. However, hydrological time series, including lake levels, often exhibit nonlinear behavior and can have complex temporal dependencies, making reliable prediction challenging. In recent years, deep learning approaches have been increasingly applied to model such complex dynamics in hydrological systems. In this study, the lake water level forecasting problem for Eğirdir Lake in Türkiye is studied by proposing a Residual-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model that combines convolutional feature extraction, residual learning, bidirectional temporal modeling, and attention mechanisms. The performance of the proposed model was compared with other deep learning models. The experimental results show that lake water levels exhibit strong temporal dependencies and that lagged water-level variables significantly improve forecasting accuracy. The proposed hybrid architecture achieved the best prediction performance among the evaluated models.

title: Temperature Monitoring and Adaptive DVFS for System-on-Chip Architecture
authors: Ashwini Barbadekar, Aditya Pradip Wani, Harsh Satyavan Wadekar, Sahil Laxmikant Mutekar, Varun Deshmane, Pratik Kaware, Sonal Kukade
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: High-performance system-on-chip (SoC) designs are increasingly faced with stringent power and thermal budgets. Unchecked power dissipation generally results in thermal runaway, reduced reliability, and coarse-grained performance throttling. The proposed research presents the architecture and Verilog implementation of an adaptive dynamic voltage and frequency scaling controller integrated with a behavioral thermal model, which allows real-time and fine-grained power management. The proposed research proposes a modular solution designed in Verilog that uses a performance-tracking algorithm combined with preemptive thermal throttling for maintaining a certain temperature limit while maximizing energy efficiency. The design includes a safe V/F switching Finite State Machine to handle dynamic voltage transitions and is further validated through a comprehensive testbench. The proposed research will establish a systematic approach toward thermal-aware DVFS, essential for energy-constrained applications such as Wireless Sensor Networks and edge computing.

title: IoT-Enabled Soil Condition Analysis for Efficient Farm Management
authors: Rubinabegam M, Jaiganesh M, Soundharya K, Muthulekshmi M, Sandhiya R, Vedasundaravinayagam P
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Sustainable agricultural methods and the everincreasing need for food throughout the world depend on wellmanaged farms. Irrigation, fertilization, and crop management practices might be hindered by the limited real-time data provided by labor-intensive and time-consuming traditional soil monitoring systems. One novel approach is the use of the Internet of Things (IoT) in farming, which allows for the constant monitoring of soil characteristics such <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, moisture, temperature, and nutrient levels. With an interconnected sensor network that gathers and transmits data in real time, farmers can conduct soil condition analyses enabled by the Internet of Things. This data-driven approach optimizes resource utilization, increases crop production, and decreases environmental impact. Improved agricultural output is a byproduct of this method's ability to make use of predictive analytics in the early warning signs of soil deterioration and water stress. Farmers may practice sustainable land management, cut down on waste, and use precision agricultural methods by using IoT technologies. To promote sustainable agricultural development and economic progress, the proposed IoT-based system shows how conventional farming may be upgraded to be more intelligent, efficient, and ecologically conscious.

title: On the Stability and Limitations of Causal Discovery in Global Crop Yield Modeling: A Multicountry Bootstrap Analysis
authors: KishoreVerma S, Lokeshwaran K, Adeline Johnsana J S, Mohana Sundaram K, N. Ramshankar, Selvin Prem Kumar S
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: To create resilient food systems, it is necessary to comprehend the causal factors of agricultural productivity, but the global crop yield data are challenging because of the confounding factor, temporal errors, and aggregation bias. The paper is an evaluation of the credibility of data-driven causal structure learning against a multi-country dataset comprising of yield, climate variables and pesticide intensity over the years 1990-2013. DirectLiNGAM is used to estimate causal directions, and then 100 rounds of bootstrapping are to determine structural stability. Results show that there are a number of statistically stable but agronomically implausible edges like yield to temperature and yield to pesticides indicating that there are strong latent confounding and cotrending effects. Climatic relations show moderate stability but rainfall to yield directions are very unstable. Backdooradjusted causal effects estimate as well as partial-dependence analyses of complementary data indicate that there is a high level of heterogeneity and nonlinearity in different regions. On the whole, the results indicate that a careful interpretation of the causal discovery of agricultural data, on a global scale, should be complemented by domain-specific modeling and resilience tests.

title: A Soft Voting Ensemble Framework for Crisis Type and Urgency Classification in Tweets
authors: Sunil Sovan Das, Basudev Nath, Jogeswar Tripathy, Swati Sucharita Roy, Sampa Chau Pattnaik, Nilima Rani Das
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: In the time of disaster, social media platforms produce enormous amounts of textual information in real-time, and automated classification of tweets is needed to efficiently respond to crises. This paper presents a lightweight and efficient machine-learning system to classify Crisis Type (seven classes) and Urgency Level (three classes) with the help of TF-IDF features along with standard classifiers. Upon preprocess of tweets and TF-IDF representation, three base models, including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression (LR), and Random Forest (RF), are trained individually. Their prediction probabilities are then combined with an ensemble of Soft Voting, which allows stronger and more consistent classification results. The experiments performed based on the crisis-related tweet dataset demonstrate that the ensemble model is constantly better, with Crisis Type and Urgency Level achieving the accuracy of 88.11% and 93.46%, respectively. The findings show that when made in an ensemble learning system, classical machine-learning models can deliver powerful and computationally effective disaster tweet classification. The framework provides a real-time practical solution that can be used to monitor emergency.

title: AI-Powered SQL Generation Using Natural Language Processing for Intelligent Database Interaction
authors: Shoba LK, Geethika Biradavolu, Himasree Pandi
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Natural language interfaces for databases have gained significant attention as organizations seek to democratize data access for non-technical users while maintaining query correctness and security. Recent advances in large language models have enabled more accurate translation of natural language queries into structured query language, yet challenges remain in schema awareness, safety enforcement, and crossdatabase compatibility. This work presents an AI-powered, schema-aware natural language-to-SQL generation framework that enables intelligent and secure database interaction through a web-based interface. The system automatically discovers database schemas, including tables, attributes, and relational constraints, and incorporates this contextual knowledge into a prompt-engineered language model pipeline for accurate SQL synthesis. Multi-layer validation mechanisms enforce read-only query execution, prevent malicious operations, and ensure syntactic correctness using dialect-aware parsing. The generated queries are dynamically adapted to multiple database engines, enabling seamless interoperability across heterogeneous environments. Additionally, the framework supports file-driven table creation, real-time query execution, and interactive result visualization to enhance analytical usability. Experimental evaluation using diverse query categories demonstrates reliable SQL generation, robust safety compliance, and efficient execution performance. The proposed approach highlights the feasibility of integrating large language models with schema intelligence and execution safeguards to enable trustworthy natural language database interaction in real-world data-driven applications.

title: Engaging Immersive Virtual Reality Learning Environment for Students Using Haptic Technology
authors: Nushkan Nismi, Kalpani Manathunga
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR) has been widely recognized for its ability to provide immersive and interactive experiences that enhance student engagement in educational contexts. Haptic-enhanced VR environments can further improve learning experiences by providing tactile feedback that increases realism and encourages active participation. In this study, the extent to which the integration of haptic feedback influences perceived system usability, user engagement, and realism in a VR learning environment is examined. To support this investigation, a haptic-enhanced VR learning environment integrating vibrotactile feedback and physical weight simulation was developed to improve realism during virtual object manipulation tasks. Users were able to interact with virtual objects while receiving synchronized tactile and kinesthetic feedback, enabling more natural interactions. A functional prototype was evaluated through a user study involving 40 secondary school students. The evaluation focused on usability, engagement, perceived realism, and overall user experience using a post-study questionnaire and qualitative feedback. The results indicate consistently high ratings across all measured dimensions, with increased immersion and user satisfaction reported following the integration of haptic feedback. These findings suggest that the incorporation of tactile and weight-based feedback can significantly enhance interaction quality in educational VR environments, highlighting the potential of haptic technologies for future immersive learning applications.

title: Satellite Based Hydrological Feature Engineering and Clustered Time Series Modeling for Sustainable Reservoir Water Management
authors: Andrii Biloshchytskyi, Alexandr Neftissov, Ilyas Kazambayev, Lalita Kirichenko, Aliya Aubakirova, Ainur Medetkhan, Oleksandr Kuchanskyi, Svitlana Biloshchytska, Mukhtar Orazbay
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of reservoir storage is critical for sustainable freshwater management, particularly in regions facing climate stress and increasing water demand. This study develops an integrated forecasting approach that couples satellite observations with machine learning to support informed water allocation decisions. Sentinel-2 and MODIS data were processed to extract time series of Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) and Land Surface Temperature (LST) as hydrologically meaningful predictors. Physically based seasonal criteria were established to filter winter periods, since snow and ice alter spectral and thermal properties and reduce model fidelity. An XGBoost regressor was trained for the Tasotkel reservoir (2020-2024) using features generated in Google Earth Engine. Seasonal filtering improved performance across all metrics, achieving MAE =52.793, RMSE =60.276, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}=0.673$</tex>. Results demonstrate that targeted seasonal data clustering enhances predictive accuracy and supports proactive, data-driven water resource governance. The seasonal filtering procedure effectively clusters the time series into hydrologically consistent segments, enabling more stable feature behavior and improving model interpretability. The proposed framework illustrates the practical value of remote sensing combined with machine intelligence for advancing reservoir resilience and long-term water sustainability.

title: A Review of Gate-Structured Recurrent Neural Network Strategies for State-of-Charge Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries
authors: Fatih Eroglu, Bilal Catkin
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The rapid enhancements and widespread adoption of deep learning have significantly supported the emergence of innovative approaches for state-of-charge (SOC) estimation in lithium-ion batteries. Recurrent neural networks (RNNs), traditionally employed in areas like speech processing and machine translation, are now being increasingly explored by researchers for their potential in SOC estimation applications. This paper provides a review of gate-structured RNNs used for SOC estimation in lithium-ion batteries with a focus on long and short-term memory (LSTM) and gated recurrent unit (GRU) networks. Various gate-structured RNN-based SOC estimation approaches are examined considering their advantages and limitations in comparison to other existing methods. With ongoing improvements in battery storage technology, RNNs are expected to maintain a prominent role in SOC estimation in future electric vehicle applications.

title: A Cryptography-Driven Dual-Layer Quantum-Resilient Detection Architecture for Secure Cloud Data Access
authors: Mahesh Muthulakshmi R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Data security has become a critical challenge in cloud computing as organizations increasingly depend on cloud-based digital infrastructure for data storage, processing, and intelligent services. The expansion of cloud adoption has simultaneously intensified security threats, including unauthorized access, data manipulation, and cryptographic attacks This paper introduces a novel cryptography-driven detection framework that integrates symmetric encryption with quantum-resilient code-based cryptography to safeguard cloud data. The proposed Code-Driven Dual-Layer Quantum-Resilient Detection System (CDD-QDS) architecture employs the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) to ensure fast and reliable data confidentiality, while a McEliece-inspired code-based cryptographic layer is utilized to secure key exchange and resist post-quantum attacks. Unlike conventional security models that treat encryption and intrusion detection independently, the proposed approach embeds detection capabilities within cryptographic operations by monitoring decoding behavior, error patterns, and key entropy. This integrated strategy enables early identification of malicious activities targeting cloud infrastructure before data decryption occurs. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through comparative performance analysis, focusing on data integrity preservation, confidentiality assurance, detection accuracy, and computational overhead. Experimental results demonstrate that the integrated cryptographic detection mechanism enhances cloud security by 98.7% accuracy and 0.8% FPR while maintaining efficiency in machine-learning-based cloud environments.

title: Smart Civic Issue Tracker: Proof-Based Digital System For Effective Municipal Issue Resolution
authors: P. Govindaraju, T. Marthandan, C. Gowri Shankar, Bharathraj P, Inbaselvan N, Kavya S
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The project aims to create an AI-based municipal grievance management system where citizens can file complaints with pictures and get an automatic verification of the complaints based on deep learning, eliminate duplicates, monitor the state of the complaint in real time, and enhance the level of transparency and efficiency of municipal service. Manual and semi-digital grievance handling systems are part of the existing systems. The suggested system is a Municipal Hybrid CNN-based Web platform of a municipality that has a deep learning-based image verification system to identify duplicated or already reported complaints. Citizens send complaints along with pictures and this is verified and placed in a central database, and automatically given to the authorized officers. The parameters applied to determine system performance include the complaint resolution time, the rate of duplicate detection, system efficiency and transparency. The proposed system was found to have improved efficiency than the traditional manual systems with image verification accuracy of 96.5% with the chosen system, with a reduced duplicate complaint of 42% and an increased speed in responding to complaints at 38%. The suggested municipal grievance system has an intelligent, transparent, and scalable solution, as opposed to the traditional ones. It promotes responsiveness, civic contentment and efficiency of the urban municipal grievance system through the incorporation of deep learning and digitalisation.

title: Explainable Deep Neural Networks for Heart Disease Prediction in Clinical Decision Support
authors: Chahyaandida Ishaya, Pinar Yildirim, Yusufu Gambo
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has demonstrated considerable potential in enhancing heart disease prediction by utilizing deep learning models based on electronic health record (EHR) data. Many deep neural networks function as “black-box” systems, which constrains their transparency and diminishes therapeutic trust. This work examines the incorporation of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methodologies to enhance the interpretability of deep learning models for predicting cardiac disease. The study employed the Cardiovascular disease dataset obtained from Kaggle for this analysis. The dataset comprises 70,000 patient records, featuring 11 input variables and a binary target variable indicating the presence or absence of cardiovascular disease. A preprocessing pipeline comprising missing value treatment, feature encoding, Z-score normalization, and class balancing via the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) was executed prior to an 80/20 train-test division. Three deep learning architectures-Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), One-Dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-were executed utilizing TensorFlow and Keras. Experimental results indicate that the MLP model attained the highest overall performance, with an accuracy of 73.05% and an F1-score of 0.726, although the CNN achieved the superior ROC-AUC of 0.792. SHAP and LIME were utilized to deliver both worldwide and local elucidations of model predictions, enhancing transparency and facilitating the advancement of reliable AI-driven clinical decision support systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of Structural Features in Genuine and Fraudulent Listings on C2C Platforms
authors: Farid Huseynov, Busra Ozdenizci Kose, Serhat Erat, Mustafa Kemal Mayuk
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The rapid expansion of global e-commerce has significantly increased the scale of customer-to-customer (C2C) transactions, while simultaneously amplifying the risk of fraudulent activity within online marketplaces. As fraud strategies become more adaptive and structurally embedded in platform dynamics, identifying interpretable signals that distinguish fraudulent from genuine listings remains a critical challenge. This study conducts a comparative analysis of structural features in C2C listings manually labeled as fraudulent or genuine. Rather than focusing on textual content or advanced predictive modeling, the research examines platform-native listing attributes, including pricing configurations, seller activity indicators, engagement metrics, visibility signals, and off-platform communication attempts. Independent samples t-tests and effect size measurements were employed to determine statistically significant differences between the two groups. The findings reveal that fraudulent listings exhibit distinct structural patterns, particularly in communication-related indicators, visibility manipulation strategies, and limited historical seller activity. Several features demonstrate substantial effect sizes, indicating strong discriminatory power at the structural level. By emphasizing interpretable and platform-generated attributes, this study contributes to the e-commerce fraud literature by highlighting the diagnostic value of structural listing features and offering insights for more transparent and sustainable platform governance mechanisms.

title: Influence of Biohumus on the Agrochemical Characteristics, Soil Fertility, and Yield Performance in Open-Field Cultivation
authors: Zhangyl Abilbek, Assel Bizhanova, Dilnaz Rakhmet, Umar Adam Ibrahim, Elmira Ospankulova
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This research investigates the effect of biohumus on soil agrochemical properties, fertility, and the productivity of eggplant (Solanum melongena L.) and pepper (Capsicum annuum L.) under open-field conditions. The study was conducted at the SDU University experimental site in Kaskelen, Almaty region, Kazakhstan, during the 2025 growing season (AprilOctober). The experiment aimed to determine the role of biohumus as an organic amendment in improving soil nutrient balance and sustaining fertility during longterm vegetable cultivation. The experiment was arranged in a randomized complete block design with six treatments: control (no fertilization), mineral fertilizers only, mineral fertilizers combined with biohumus (1.5 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.0 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha})$</tex>, biohumus alone <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1.5 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha})$</tex>, and manure (20 t/ha). Soil samples were collected from the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0 - 3 0 c m}$</tex> layer before planting, at mid-season, and after harvest. Agrochemical analyses included measurements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, electrical conductivity (EC), and the contents of N, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{O}$</tex>. Results indicated that biohumus application significantly improved soil fertility parameters compared to the mineral-only treatment. During mid-season, biohumus-treated plots showed increases of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 8 \%}$</tex> in available nitrogen and phosphorus, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7}-\mathbf{1 2 \%}$</tex> in exchangeable potassium. By the end of the season, soils treated with biohumus maintained higher residual nutrient levels, stable pH (6.6-7.2), and optimal EC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.62-0.78 \text{mS} / \text{cm})$</tex>, confirming the buffering and nutrient-retaining capacity of biohumus under open-field irrigation. The seasonal yield of eggplant increased by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22-27 \%$</tex>, and pepper by 18 23% compared to the mineral-only treatment. The moderate rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex> biohumus was the most effective, ensuring balanced nutrient availability without excessive accumulation of salts or nitrogen. The findings demonstrate that biohumus enhances the agrochemical status of soils, maintains fertility throughout the growing season, and increases the productivity and sustainability of open-field vegetable cultivation in southern Kazakhstan.

title: Vibetalk- AI-Powered Assistive Communication System for Hearing and Speech Impairment
authors: Akshata Undri, Amulya R, Lakshitha S R, Jessica J Maris, Roma Kudale
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The VibeTalk AI-Powered Assistive Communication System for Hearing and Speech Impairment was created to help people with any hearing loss or speech impairments to ensure communication was made easier with people who do not know sign language. Using AI, this system recognizes Indian Sign Language (ISL) gestures in real-time, and then these gestures are converted into speech to make communication much faster and more efficient for people with disabilities. The system is built as a Web App that uses HTML and CSS, and offers a simple and responsive interface that users can easily navigate. The web interface includes three main modules: Alphabet Recognition (A–Z), Number Recognition (0–9), Word Recognition (Common Phrases). When a user happens to select any one of the 3 categories, the camera is activated and gets a cue to capture hand gestures. Once that is done, the system translates ISL gestures into text in real time through a text-to-speech (TTS) engine, which allows a smoother interaction process with non-sign language users. Looking ahead, the project aims to increase the number of gestures in its database to include a wider variety of ISL signs, support multiple languages, optimize it for mobile devices, and improve the user experience. In the long term, this project aims to empower people with disabilities, helping them connect with others and participate more fully in society.

title: An Effective Detection of Financial Statement Fraud Using Parallel Convolutional Neural Network with Crayfish Optimization Algorithm
authors: Naduvathezhath Nessariose Jose, Thirumaraiselvan P, Vivekanandan Govindarajan
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Financial statement fraud involves manipulating financial records to deceive investors, regulators, and stakeholders for illegal profit. Fraudulent practices distort organizational performance and threaten market integrity and economic stability. Automatic financial statement fraud detection uses analytical and intelligent methods to find deceptive accounting data patterns. High class imbalance, noisy financial ratios, and the inability to capture complex nonlinear and inter-entity relationships limit traditional detection methods. This work uses Parallel Convolutional Neural Network with Crayfish Optimization Algorithm to detect Financial Statement Fraud. Initial financial fraud data comes from SEC Accounting and Auditing Enforcement Releases. In pre-processing, Shape-Aware Mesh Normal Filtering (Sh-AMNF) removes inconsistencies, suppresses noise, and normalizes financial ratios to improve data reliability. After refining the dataset, the Apiary Organizational-Based Optimization Algorithm (AOOA) selects the most discriminative financial indicators to reduce redundancy and dimensionality. The Parallel Convolutional Neural Network (PCoNNet) learns hierarchical and fuzzy representations across spectral (financial ratios) and spatial (entity-level) domains from optimized features. Additionally, the Crayfish Optimization Algorithm (COA) optimizes network weight for faster convergence and better classification accuracy. Experimental results show that the proposed framework outperforms current methods in recall 99.56%, f1score 99.71%, precision 99.65%, accuracy 99.86%, and specificity 99.69% in real-world financial fraud analysis.

title: Flow-Level Statistical Fingerprinting for Unsupervised Network Intrusion Detection
authors: Induru Perera, Chathumal Abeysinghe, Prageeth Pahanpriya, Rashminda Iddamalgoda, Anuradha Jayakody, Hanojhan Rajahrajasingh
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper proposes a behavioral network fingerprinting framework for unsupervised network anomaly detection in environments where labeled attack data are unavailable. A behavioral fingerprint is formally defined as a fixed-length vector of flow-level statistics that together characterize the volumetric, protocol, distributional, and temporal properties of a traffic window in a manner that is sensitive to the behavioral differences between normal usage and malicious activity. The proposed fingerprint comprises a compact 10-feature set derived from 14 candidate features using Pearson correlation analysis to remove four redundant ones. Five unsupervised models, namely Isolation Forest (IF), One-Class SVM (OCSVM), Local Outlier Factor (LOF), Deep Autoencoder (AE), and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), are trained on normal-only traffic from the UNSW-NB15 dataset, with decision thresholds selected on a held-out validation split of normal traffic to avoid test-set leakage. The models are then evaluated on a labeled test set of more than 23,000 samples. The IF achieves 96.26 % accuracy and 98.56 % recall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{ROC-AUC}=0.9771)$</tex>, while the AE reaches the highest ROCAUC of 0.9905. All five models outperform published baselines on the same dataset, suggesting that feature representation plays a critical role in unsupervised anomaly detection, independent of the underlying model architecture.

title: Fusion of Excitation-based Features for Speaker Identification with Advanced Deep learning Model
authors: Hema Kumar Pentapati, Sridevi K, Siva Prasad B, RaviChandra Bandi, Rajasekhar K, M V S Roja Ramani
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Now a days, deep learning has become popular for building Speaker Identification systems. One important step in these systems is extracting the most useful features from speech. In our work, we combine two types of features — Log-Mel Spectrum and Excitation Features — to develop a text-independent speaker identification model. An advanced deep learning model called a Dilated Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is proposed, which helps capture more information by covering a wider area of the input. Our proposed system achieves an accuracy of 93.95% when tested on challenging speech data. We evaluated its performance using the LibriSpeech dataset, with 250 voice samples per speaker. Compared to existing methods that reached only 90.8% accuracy, our approach shows a clear improvement.

title: A Secure and Explainable Federated Edge-AI Framework for Climate-Smart Smallholder Farming
authors: Rumitha S, Jenefa A, Ebenezer.V, Santhiya.P, Arul Xavier V.M, Kethsy Prabavathy A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Smallholder farms require trustworthy, low cost decision support that works with limited bandwidth and strict privacy needs. Centralized training risks data leakage and often misses locality specific patterns, while purely edge training fragments knowledge and weakens calibration and interpretability. This paper presents FedEdge X, a secure and explainable federated edge AI framework that keeps raw data on device, applies differentially private local training with secure aggregation, and pairs domain aware augmentation with post training calibration. A compact multimodal model processes RGB images, IoT time series, and tabular weather to support disease recognition, irrigation advisory, soil moisture forecasting, and yield prediction. Evaluation on IFD Fields (18k/2k/2.5k), OPD Plant (12.5k/1.5k/2k), SoilSense (2.6M/0.3M/0.3M points), Irrigo (45k/5k/5k), and YieldTrack (9.6k/1.2k/1.2k) shows that FedEdge X improves classification over strong baselines on IFD (95.3 accuracy, 94.8 F1) and OPD (96.4, 95.9), and on irrigation (92.6, 92.1), while reducing soil moisture error to 3.41 RMSE and 11.1 MAPE and yield error to 0.56 t/ha RMSE and 9.5 MAPE. On Raspberry Pi class hardware the framework achieves 45.9 ms latency and 21.8 FPS, with an INT8 variant reaching 39.2 ms and 25.5 FPS. These results indicate that privacy preserving federation with calibrated probabilities and lightweight explanations can deliver reliable, field ready intelligence for climate smart smallholder farming.

title: Headphone with Safety System that Warn Pedestrians of Imminent Dangers
authors: Merve Yildiz, Ceyhan Yilmaz
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper relates a system integrated with a headset that includes a camera for sensing environmental images and a control unit programmed to warn to the user by interpreting these images and distinguishing vehicles. The proposed system provides pedestrians to be warned of risky events occurring in their surroundings, particularly in areas outside their field of vision, without requiring an external device. On the other hand, when no warning is presented to the user, the headset can operate independently on its own or in conjunction with external devices of the user's choosing.
