title: An Efficiency Optimization Method Based on Optimal Current Control for DPAS-MIMR Underwater WPT System
authors: Zheng Liu, Ruizhi Chen, Dehong Zhou, Zewei Shen, Jianxiao Zou, Xin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Underwater wireless power transfer (WPT) for autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) faces significant challenges, including dynamic misalignment caused by seawater fluctuations, eddy current losses, and wide-range power regulation requirements. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a DC-link parallel AC-link series multi-inverter multi-rectifier (DPAS-MIMR) topology with optimal current control. The proposed system integrates modular topology design with mutual inductance identification, efficiency optimization, and constant-current/constant-voltage charging capability. It can reduce eddy current losses and adapt to seawater fluctuations, which may provide a comprehensive solution for reliable underwater WPT systems. Experimental results show the system can achieve an output power of 2.5 kW with 90.4% efficiency when operating in seawater conditions, validating the feasibility of the proposed system.

title: Design of a Cable-Driven, Self-Aligning Knee Exoskeleton to Eliminate Joints' Misalignment Across Users With Varying Leg Dimensions_supp1-3699123.mp4
authors: Xiangyang Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: One of the primary difficulties in exoskeleton use is the misalignment between the device's mechanical joints and the user's natural biological joints. Knee exoskeletons, widely studied for assisting walking and stand-to-sit transitions, typically require precise adjustment of the rigid frame to match the user's knee joint axis. If not properly aligned, parasitic forces arise at the physical human–robot interface (pHRI), causing significant discomfort. To address this problem, we propose a cable-driven, self-aligning knee exoskeleton that accommodates different wearing positions without the need for accurate pre-use alignment. Platform experiments showed that the exoskeleton adapted well to variations in wearing position in the deactivated mode and transmitted assistive torque with limited changes in parasitic forces when activated. At an assistive torque of 18 Nm, the relative error between the measured and target values was 7.27%. Human-subject experiments further demonstrated substantial reductions in relative sliding distance (RSD) and parasitic forces during activities such as walking and stand-to-sit transitions: the maximum RSD was reduced by 84.9% compared with the Orthosis condition and by 85.1% compared with the Locked condition; the maximum parasitic force was reduced by 89.1% and 89.0%, respectively, and the maximum parasitic torque was reduced by 95.6% and 96.1%, respectively.

title: A Dielectric-Modulated Dual-Material Gate MOS-HEMT for Ultra-High Sensitivity Label-Free Biosensor_supp1-3698812.docx
authors: Hitesh Kumar
publish: N/A
abstract: This manuscript presents a comprehensive performance evaluation of a dielectric-modulated dual-material gate metal–oxide–semiconductor high electron mobility transistor (DMG MOS-HEMT) for various neutral and charged biomolecules incorporating source-side cavity to enable label-free detection. In this structure, dual-material gate electrodes with distinct work functions are employed to achieve a more uniform electric field distribution and to enhance electron velocity in the channel, thereby improving the device sensitivity. The neutral biomolecules are modeled by introducing a dielectric constant within the cavity, whereas charged biomolecules are simulated by varying the interface charge density at the oxide/AlGaN interface. The device structure is implemented and analyzed using the Silvaco ATLAS TCAD simulator. The performance of the device is evaluated by examining variations in drain current (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</sub>), threshold voltage (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</sub>), and channel conductance (g<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>). It has been observed that the proposed DMG MOS-HEMT exhibits superior sensitivity with respect to single-material gate (SMG) MOS-HEMT, with maximum changes of 256 mA/mm in I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</sub> and 2.0 V in V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</sub> for neutral biomolecules, and 76 mA/mm and 0.37 V, respectively, for charged biomolecules. Hence, these results substantiate the promise of DMG MOS-HEMT architecture for next-generation biosensing applications demanding ultra-high sensitivity.

title: Fast Computation of Electromagnetic Scattering by Graphene-Filled Honeycomb Coated Target
authors: Wei-Jia He, Yong-Xiang Zheng, Xue Yang, Bi-Yi Wu, Sheng Sun, Ming-Lin Yang, Xin-Qing Sheng
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this paper, an efficient method is presented for computing electromagnetic scattering from objects coated with thin graphene-filled honeycomb structures. In the proposed approach, the graphene-filled honeycomb coating is first homogenized as an anisotropic medium characterized by 3×3 tensorial relative permittivity and permeability. The homogenized model is then reduced to an equivalent impedance surface described by a 3 × 3 surface-impedance tensor derived from the effective material parameters. Based on this model, a general self-dual combined field integral equation for anisotropic surface impedance, referred to as G-C-SDIE, is formulated. Several representative targets coated with graphene-filled honeycomb structures are investigated. Numerical results are compared with those obtained using the conventional surface integral equation (SIE) method and the hybrid finite element-boundary integral (FE-BI) method to validate the accuracy of the proposed approach. In addition, an electrically large aircraft model is analyzed to demonstrate the flexibility and computational efficiency of the method.

title: CVaR-Constrained Optimisation for Risk-Aware Dynamic Resource Allocation in 5G RAN Slicing
authors: S Mahalakshmi, S Markkandan
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Dynamic resource allocation across heterogeneous network slices in 5G radio access networks requires balancing conflicting quality-of-service (QoS) requirements under time-varying and bursty traffic conditions. Conventional approaches rely on percentile-based latency metrics such as the 95th-percentile delay (p95) to evaluate Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communication (URLLC) performance. While p95 captures typical worst-case latency, it does not bound the severity of extreme delay events, where a small fraction of packets experience disproportionately large latency. To address this limitation, Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) provides a principled measure of tail risk by quantifying the expected delay of the worst α% of packets. This paper proposes a CVaR-constrained Proximal Policy Optimisation (CVaR-PPO) framework that formulates multi-slice resource allocation as a constrained Markov decision process (CMDP), where CVaR0.05 enforces a strict tail-latency constraint on the URLLC slice. A Dirichlet-parameterised policy is employed to generate allocation weights that inherently satisfy the simplex constraint, while an adaptive Lagrangian dual variable dynamically regulates tail-risk violations during online learning. The framework is validated through real-time closed-loop co-simulation, where a PPO agent interacts online with the ns-3.45 5G NR simulator via a TCP socket interface, enabling continuous policy updates based on dynamic network feedback across four service slices. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves the highest overall Service Level Agreement (SLA) satisfaction among all evaluated approaches and is the only method that simultaneously satisfies URLLC, Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X), and enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB) slice-level SLA requirements while maintaining SLA-compliant massive Machine-Type Communication (mMTC) performance. Furthermore, the proposed framework reduces CVaR violation rates by up to 73 percentage points compared to unconstrained PPO. These results confirm that p95 compliance alone is insufficient for URLLC reliability, as methods with similar p95 values exhibit substantially different tail-risk behaviour. The proposed CVaR-constrained formulation enables stable, risk-aware, and SLA-compliant resource allocation for next-generation multi-slice RAN systems.

title: Contact-Aware Soft Robot Design via Differentiable Simulation and Morphological Optimization_supp2-3692920.pdf
authors: Feifei Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Contact frequently occurs in soft robots and significantly influences their deformation characteristics. However, the inherent nondifferentiability and the design-dependent nature of contact behaviors pose major challenges for gradient-based design optimization. Here, we propose an optimization-oriented static, frictionless contact model that acts as a smooth surrogate to approximate emergent contact behaviors across diverse design candidates during optimization, and more importantly remains continuously differentiable across contact state transitions with respect to both deformation states and geometric design parameters. This capability is achieved via a smooth penetration-penalty function and an automated contact region detection scheme. Building on this contact model, we develop a contact-aware morphological optimization framework for pneumatic soft robots, wherein gradients of contact-related objectives are efficiently evaluated using the adjoint method combined with automatic differentiation. Our results validate the model’s accuracy through benchmarking against Abaqus, demonstrate the necessity of contact-aware optimization for bidirectional bending actuators prone to self-contact, and showcase the framework’s capability to proactively exploit contact interactions for functional gains, as evidenced by a vacuum-driven actuator utilizing self-contact for enhanced torsional stiffness and a soft gripper featuring an expanded contact area with the target object. This work paves the way for programming contact-induced mechanical intelligence in soft robots.

title: NeuroThyroid-PQC: A Multivariate Post-Quantum and Hash-Based Cryptographic Framework for Real-Time Numerical Thyroid Biomarker Anomaly Detection in IoMT
authors: Jing Yang, Mohammed Okmi, Sunil Prajapat, Ziwei Li, Lip Yee Por, Ashok Kumar Das, Joel J. P. C. Rodrigues, Xiaojie Ji
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) devices for continuous thyroid biomarker monitoring face dual challenges: maintaining quantum-resistant security while achieving energy efficiency for battery-powered wearables. We present NeuroThyroid-PQC, a framework integrating Rainbow multi-variate signatures, SPHINCS+ hash-based authentication, and BIKE code-based encryption with neuromorphic edge processing for secure thyroid function analysis. Our three-tier post-quantum cryptographic scheme dynamically adapts security parameters based on clinical urgency, from routine TSH monitoring to acute thyroid storm detection. Experimental validation across 250 consumer devices demonstrates 97.2% hypothyroidism detection accuracy, 95.8% hyperthyroidism classification, and 830× energy efficiency improvement over conventional CNN-based approaches. The framework achieves 5.3ms end-to-end latency for encrypted biomarker analysis, enabling months-long continuous monitoring on coin-cell batteries while maintaining 192-bit post-quantum security. Compared to existing lattice-only PQC implementations, NeuroThyroid-PQC reduces communication overhead by 44% and energy consumption by 87%, addressing the critical gap between quantum resistance and edge deployment constraints in medical wearables.

title: Nonlinear Model Predictive Control using a Modified Sequential Convex Programming
authors: Yunshan Deng, Yuanqing Xia, Zhongqi Sun, Jinxian Wu, Jie Lin, Li Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper presents a nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC) scheme based on sequential convex programming (SCP). We propose a modified SCP algorithm for nonlinear MPC that adaptively incorporates the effects of linearization errors into the state constraints, ensuring that the actual state trajectory—generated by the nonlinear dynamics from the returned solution—faithfully satisfies all constraints, even if the SCP process is terminated early. Subsequently, an NMPC framework for the nominal system is developed, eliminating the need for additional optimization. The approach is further extended to systems with additive disturbances by introducing a shrinking horizon and a self-triggering mechanism, which together reduce the problem dimensionality and computational frequency, thereby enhancing real-time performance. Theoretical analysis establishes recursive feasibility and closed-loop stability, even when the SCP procedure is terminated early. Finally, simulation results are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: A Bitmap-Guided Collision Tree Anti-collision Protocol for Large-Scale RFID Tags Identification_supp1-3696454.pdf
authors: Xiaolin Jia
publish: N/A
abstract: During the collision resolution phase, many deterministic RFID anti-collision protocols require tags to repeatedly transmit identical ID segments across multiple query cycles. This redundant transmission leads to high communication overhead, increasing identification latency and energy consumption, thereby constraining system performance and scalability. This paper proposes the Bitmap-Guided Collision Tree (BGCT) protocol to address this limitation. BGCT reduces tag transmissions through a progressive information acquisition mechanism, and enables multi-way collision resolution by integrating vector-based grouping with a bitmap. Theoretical analysis and simulation results demonstrate BGCT’s performance advantages. Specifically, for identifying 10000 tags(ID=96 bits), BGCT reduces the total identification time by 27.8% compared to the best competitor in this scenario, EMDT. For 256-bit IDs, assuming uniform or pseudo-random ID distributions, the progressive mechanism mitigates the dependency of communication cost on ID length, maintaining a low expected overhead in contrast to the linear growth observed in other protocols. Furthermore, under non-uniform ID distributions, BGCT demonstrates a collision resolution efficiency 152.9% higher than that of the competing protocols.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Intelligent Vehicle Adaptive Cruise Control
authors: Hui-bin Yuan, Zhi-xin Yu, Bo Chen, Ruixia Xiong, Xinyi Zheng, Xiaohui Lu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Urban traffic is characterized by frequent speed changes, sudden lane incursions, and heterogeneous vehicle interactions, making adaptive cruise control (ACC) one of the most demanding tasks for intelligent vehicles. Existing DRL-based approaches suffer from fixed-weight reward functions, monolithic experience buffers, and uniform sampling, leading to slow convergence and limited scenario adaptability. This paper presents an integrated SAC-based ACC framework that jointly addresses these issues through: (1) a dynamic reward function that adjusts safety, efficiency, and comfort weights in real time; (2) scenario-specific experience classification that stores and replays samples by driving mode; and (3) a stratified sampling strategy combining recent-experience injection with localized prioritization. The system state fuses bird’s-eye-view semantic segmentation features with longitudinal and lateral motion measurements. Validated in CARLA across six scenarios spanning four core ACC modes—cruising, car-following, cut-in, and cut-out—the method achieves a 38.9% reduction in training episodes over baseline SAC. The trained policy maintains following-distance errors ≤ 1 m, speed-tracking errors ≤ 1 m/s, and peak acceleration generally below 3 m/s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, demonstrating effective and comfortable control under dynamic urban conditions.

title: Category-Guided Vision-Language Model for IoMT-Enabled Medical Report Generation under Heterogeneous Imaging Data
authors: Xinyi Liu, Qiang Jin, Tian-Tian Zhang, Zhi-Ri Tang, Yanhua Chen, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The traditional manual report writing model relies on radiologists to complete reports independently, lacking reference standards and potentially leading to diagnostic omissions or descriptive biases. Existing methods often treat medical report generation as an image captioning task with an encoder-decoder framework. However, purely data-driven approaches show weak correlations between disease features and text descriptions, and the model lacks knowledge of relevant rare diseases. To tackle these challenges, we propose a category-guided structured diagnostic decision framework in the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT). Through collecting medical data from various real-world scenarios, the proposed IoMT platform could generate accurate medical reports and reduce the workload of clinical doctors. During report generation process, the proposed framework introduces a Dual-Guided Prompting Strategy in which local lesion-aware prompts are embedded in the encoder, while global semantic prompts derived from disease classification results guide the decoder. This design enables cross-modal alignment at both local and global levels and enhances category-guided structured report generation. To further improve the diagnosis of long-tail diseases, we introduce a Cross-Modal Knowledge Enhancement module that utilizes positive classification results to retrieve similar sentences from a preconstructed knowledge base, infusing external clinical knowledge into the generation process. Numerous experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that the Dual-Guided Prompting Strategy and the Cross-Modal Knowledge Enhancement module enhance natural language metrics. Specifically, compared to the baseline BLEU-4 score of 0.157, the Dual-Guided Prompting Strategy and the Cross-Modal Knowledge Enhancement module improve the scores to 0.178 and 0.164, respectively, with the final model achieving an overall score of 0.192.

title: Classroom Emotional State Analysis Using Proxy Metrics and Multimodal Data
authors: Tole Bi Yermek, Banu Yergesh, Amanzhol Yelemessov, Madina Yelemessova, Gulmira Bekmanova, Assel Omarbekova, Aizhan Nazyrova, Zhanar Lamasheva
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Understanding the emotional climate of a classroom is an important factor in analyzing student engagement and the effectiveness of the learning process. However, traditional educational analytics mainly relies on exam scores, attendance records, and surveys, which do not capture real-time changes in students' emotional states during lessons. This study proposes an approach for analyzing classroom emotional states using behavioral proxy metrics derived from video recordings of university classes. Instead of direct emotion recognition or student identification, the method analyzes group-level behavioral indicators, including the Attention Index (AI), Engagement Index (EI), Calmness Index (CI), and Group Interaction Index (GI), computed from posture, body orientation, and movement dynamics. Because the archived MP4 files did not contain embedded audio streams, the experimental classification in this study relies on video-derived proxy metrics. To demonstrate how acoustic information could be incorporated in a future multimodal version of the system, an illustrative supplementary analysis was conducted using simulated acoustic descriptors aligned with the annotated video fragments, including Root Mean Square (RMS) energy, fundamental frequency (F0) variability, and Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficient (MFCC)-based spectral variation. The pilot analysis showed that positive classroom states accounted for approximately 50% of the annotated observations, while negative states accounted for approximately 30%. Comparison between manual annotations and automatic classification achieved an agreement of approximately 70%. In addition, correlation analysis revealed a strong relationship between attention and engagement indices (r ≈ 0.99). The results provide preliminary evidence that video-derived proxy metrics can characterize the emotional background of a learning environment and support the analysis of its temporal dynamics during a lesson. The supplementary simulated acoustic analysis further illustrates a possible path for future multimodal integration, but it is not used as evidence of audio-based classification performance in the present dataset. However, larger-scale annotation and validation of the complete archive are required to further assess the reliability and generalizability of the proposed framework. The proposed approach can support educational analytics and the development of intelligent systems for monitoring and improving the learning process.

title: Full-Scale Feature Pyramid Information Propagation for Post-Earthquake Landslide Detection
authors: Huanxu Li, Keke Xu, Xianglei Liu, Jianlong Wang, Guoqing Zhou, Mosi Zhang, Shuaipeng Wang, Mengbo Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate detection of post-earthquake landslides from high-resolution Earth observation imagery is critical for emergency response and hazard assessment, yet remains challenging due to extreme scale variability and complex terrain backgrounds. Landslides in post-seismic scenes range from small, fragmented failures to large, continuous slope collapses, posing significant difficulties for reliable multi-scale representation. To address these challenges, this study proposes a task-driven deep learning framework that emphasizes full-scale information propagation across feature pyramid levels (P2–P5) together with efficient global contextual modeling for post-earthquake landslide detection. The proposed framework enables persistent and bidirectional feature interaction within the feature pyramid, allowing fine-grained spatial details from shallow layers and high-level semantic context from deep layers to be jointly propagated and refined. In addition, a magnitude-aware linear attention mechanism is incorporated to enhance long-range contextual dependencies with improved computational efficiency, making the framework suitable for high-resolution, large-area remote sensing imagery. The proposed method is evaluated on two post-earthquake landslide datasets with different scene complexities and scale distributions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently outperforms representative convolutional- and transformer-based detectors, achieving mean average precision (mAP) values of 0.937 and 0.882 on the two datasets, respectively. Qualitative analyses further indicate improved localization accuracy and reduced false alarms, particularly for small-scale and elongated landslides under complex background conditions. These results highlight the importance of explicitly modeling full-scale feature pyramid information propagation for robust post-earthquake landslide detection and demonstrate the practical potential of the proposed framework for large-scale post-disaster landslide mapping.

title: Synchronization-Free Backdoor Attacks on Edge-Deployed Neural Network for IoT Systems
authors: Bing Zhang, Lei Xie, Kaixuan Zhang, Qixuan Cai, Xin Xie, Xiaoyi Tao, Xu Hu, Xiulong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Deep Neural Networks integrated into sensor-driven embedded systems have become fundamental to edge computing applications. While Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs) are ubiquitous in these architectures due to their cost-efficiency, the reliance on data-driven models expands the attack surface of the embedded computing pipeline. This paper investigates the vulnerability of such systems by presenting a synchronization-free backdoor attack framework. We address two critical threat models in the system lifecycle: training-data poisoning and outsourced training. For the poisoning setting, we exploit the physical-digital signal interface by developing a frequency-domain injection method that utilizes sidelobe perturbations to implant stealthy triggers. For the outsourced scenario, we propose a dual-optimization joint trigger learning scheme that co-optimizes trigger patterns and model objectives, eliminating the dependency on synchronization with the victim’s training process. To ensure attack stealthiness within the operational bounds, a dynamic weighting strategy is introduced to balance attack efficacy with benign inference accuracy. Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that current embedded architectures are highly susceptible to these methods: the frequency-domain poisoning achieves over 60% average attack success, while the outsourced-training attack exceeds 95%, highlighting a critical security gap in sensor-based intelligent systems.

title: Rapid Heating Process for High-Density Nd-Fe-B Magnet Plates via Electrically Heated Powder Rolling: Microstructure and Magnetic Properties_supp1-3699616.pdf
authors: Jungryang Kim
publish: N/A
abstract: In this study, we systematically evaluated the densification behavior, microstructural evolution, and magnetic properties of Nd-Fe-B compacts processed by Electrically Heated Powder Rolling (EPR) in 1 to 5 rolling passes at a constant current of 1000 A and a rolling speed of 0.3 mm/s. The EPR conditions enabled rapid consolidation: the reduction rate increased from 67.0% after 1 pass to 74.0% after 3 passes and 76.3% after 5 passes, corresponding to an increase in relative density from 71% to 91% and 95%, respectively. The highest coercivity of 221 kA/m was obtained at 74% reduction (3 passes), where the Nd-rich phase was relatively well distributed along intergranular regions, while the microstructure still suggested insufficient formation of a continuous nanoscale Nd-rich grain-boundary phase. Because temperature control during EPR is not yet fully optimized, the present processing conditions do not maximize the intrinsic potential of Nd-Fe-B magnets. Overall, the results demonstrate that effective densification and partial sintering of Nd-Fe-B can be accomplished within minutes by Joule heating-driven rolling, highlighting the high efficiency and promise of the EPR process.

title: A Systematic Robustness Analysis of Context Re-Positioning in Long-Context Language Models
authors: Muhammet Tarık Yılmaz, Yusuf Çakır
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This study investigates how contextual position modeling affects the long-context performance of large language models. For context lengths ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4 K}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3 2 K}$</tex> tokens, we present a controlled comparative evaluation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 B}$</tex>-scale models on the RULER QA benchmark. The research compares the effectiveness of Context Re-positioning, incorporated into the OLMo2 backbone, against alternative architectures of similar scale and a suitable baseline model. According to the experimental results, the performance of all models gradually decreases as context length increases. However, the RePo-enhanced model maintains substantially higher performance at intermediate context lengths. At <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 K}$</tex> tokens, RePo achieves a Substring Exact Match of 0.72 and an F1 score of 0.668, outperforming its base counterpart by a wide margin. At <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 6 K}$</tex> tokens, RePo preserves an Exact Match of 0.37 and an F1 of 0.461, while the base OLMo2 model drops sharply to 0.08 Exact Match and 0.125 F1. Although all models exhibit severe degradation at 32 K tokens, the results demonstrate that contextual re-positioning mitigates performance collapse at moderate long-context scales. These findings indicate that long-context robustness is not determined solely by context window size or parameter count but is strongly influenced by positional representation design. The study highlights the importance of position-aware modeling strategies for improving retrieval accuracy and attention stability in long-document scenarios.

title: Field Oriented Control of Outer Rotor Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Using Co-Simulation
authors: Büşra Çelık, Tolgay Kara, Roberto Quintal Palomo
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: In the present study, a co-simulation approach developed between the ANSYS and MATLAB environments is used to perform field-oriented control (FOC) for an outer rotor permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM). The control algorithm created in MATLAB/Simulink is combined with the motor, which is constructed in ANSYS Maxwell using the finite element method (FEM). This method makes it possible for the control structure to function directly on a realistic motor model, resulting in a more accurate depiction of the machine's electromagnetic behavior. The sinusoidal pulse width modulation (SPWM) technique is used to generate the inverter switching signals within the established co-simulation structure. All things considered, the ANSYS-MATLAB co-simulation framework provides an accurate and practical platform for PMSM drive system implementation and research. The obtained findings verify that, under the chosen operating conditions, the suggested SPWMbased FOC structure delivers consistent speed tracking, balanced phase currents, and an accurate torque response.

title: Lung Cancer Classification using YOLOv8-based Nodule Detection and Transformer-Enhanced Segmentation
authors: Happila T, Rajendran A, Sibi P, Logeshram P S, Arshad M, Chandiresh S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Lung cancer is still considered one of the most lethal disorders all over the world, and efficient diagnostic solutions are necessary. This paper hypothesizes the construction of a single deep learning system that combines nodule detectors utilizing the YOLOv8 system, a Transformer with augmentation in the segmentation task, and a multi-stream classifier to provide automated lung cancer diagnosis through CT scans. The YOLOv8 detector detects nodules with multi-scale features and anchor-free bounding box prediction functions, and with the Transformer-augmented U-Net, improves segmentation boundary refinement by short-distance contextual dependencies. The features of both modules obtained are combined and processed by a classification head that predicts levels of malignancy with the conserved confidence and estimation of uncertainty. When tested on benchmark datasets, including LIDC-IDRI and TCIA, the system which was proposed demonstrated higher accuracy of 91 percent, 0.87 mAP (mean Average Precision), and 0.86 Dice coefficient, which was stronger in comparison with the conventional CNN-based models. Also, interpretability systems, such as Grad-CAM and attention visualizations, help improve transparency, enabling clinicians to justify the system's predictions. In general, this strategy will fill the gap between the research-level AI systems and the practical use of AI in the clinical environment, providing a solid, explainable, end-to-end solution to the next-generation lung cancer diagnosis and treatment planning.

title: Comprehensive Evaluation of Vision Transformer Models for Brain Tumor Classification using MRI
authors: Soumyarashmi Panigrahi, Dibya Ranjan Das Adhikary, Madhusree Kuanr, Subrat Kumar Nayak, Nrusingha Tripathy, Ibanga Kpereobong Friday
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: This article discusses the implementation of attention-based transformer models for brain tumor detection using MRI images. Transformers use self-attention to focus on significant parts of an image. This helps the model learn more than traditional approaches. We compare three pre-trained models: ViT-B16, DeiT-B16 and Swin-Tiny. We trained all the models on the Br35H binary classification brain MRI dataset. Different performance measures are applied to measure the model’s efficiency. They are accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, MCC, Jaccard index and calibration metrics, including log loss and Brier score. The findings reveal that transformer models are highly accurate with stable predictions. DeiT-B16 is the most successful of the models evaluated. The results support the idea that attention mechanisms are significant for improving brain MRI image classification and enabling reliable computer-aided diagnostic systems.

title: Real Time Face Recognition-based Smart Door Access Control System
authors: Greeshma Goud Ede, Pranavi Samhita Danda, Sneha Battula, Hema Sumaswi Manchikalapudi, Sambit Kumar Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Innovative security systems are also becoming a necessity in modern homes and workplaces as a result of the inefficiency of traditional security authentication systems that can be lost, copied, or broken. Face recognition can be used as a great way of biometric authentication since it is a reliable contactless method that can be used with regular cameras through computer vision methods.This paper involves the creation of a face recognition system through python and OpenCV. Haar Cascade classifier is used to detect faces and Local Binary Pattern Histogram (LBPH) algorithm is used to recognize faces. There is a graphical user interface, which is Tkinter based and enables the user to collect data, train the model, and do real time recognition. The system offers a cost effective and efficient system of secure verification of identity.

title: Cerebrovascular Scan Analyzer an Intelligent Deep Learning System for Early Hemorrhagic Stroke Detection using CT Scan
authors: Mukkamalla Kesava Narayana Reddy, P Sai Mukesh, N Divya, Nallamothu Seetaiah, Bala Gangadhara Gutam, Sayyed Nagulmeera
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Stroke is a cause of death and neurological long-term disability and in this case, it is crucial that the diagnosis is done in time and correctly in order to achieve clinical intervention. Non-contrast brain computed tomography (CT) scans are commonly applied in the initial assessment of hemorrhagic stroke, but manual interpretation is time-consuming and prone to inter-observer variance, and some of the deep learning methods have overfitting and narrow generalization. In this paper, the author provides the Cerebrovascular Scan Analyzer, an intelligent deep learning-based diagnostic system, and in the case of CT scan interpreting early hemorrhagic stroke, emphasizing on robustness and sound generalization. The given scheme is based on the phased experimental design where a specifically designed convolutional neural network (CNN) would be created and trained to define the learnability of the dataset, and the medically safe data augmentation methods would be used to ensure the stability of the model. To test further the generalization behavior, transfer learning architectures are tested based on a unified training and validation protocol. Balanced class distributions and uniform data splits are applied in the experiments conducted on a publicly available CT brain hemorrhage dataset. Moreover, post-hoc explainability analysis is also conducted to analyze the regions that affect the model predictions and it can be used to assist in qualitative evaluation of model behavior. The research paper has shown that a proper and implementation-oriented deep learning pipeline can be used to enable reliable computer-aided hemorrhagic stroke detection in academic and research-based diagnostic systems.

title: An Intelligent Optimization for Electric Vehicle Charging and Scheduling Using VANETs
authors: Kanakadurga Kasaragadda, Ramkumar Devendiran
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles gradually puts more pressure on the charging infrastructure, making it congested and time-consuming with inefficient energy utilization. This work addresses such problems by proposing an intelligent VANET-based optimization framework to enable adaptive real-time charging and scheduling decisions for large-scale EV deployments. VANET-Aware Predictive Slot Reservation (VAPSR) and Anticipatory Reinforcement Ant Colony Hybrid (ARACH), a novel combined predictive-optimization framework for Electric Vehicle (EV) charging and scheduling in Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks (VANETs). VAPSR provides predictive free-slot maps from Charging Stations (CSs) using short term mobility and occupancy forecasts together with Age of-Information (AoI) correction. ARACH couples Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) heuristics for candidate station scoring with a reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuner to make anticipatory slot-reservation decisions. A slot-based discrete-time model is used to evaluate waiting-time, throughput, and robustness to stale information. This proposed model reduces average waiting time by 22%-49% and increases hourly served EVs by 10-20% compared with ACO, RL, and greedy baselines. The approach is evaluated under varying beacon AoI, arrival rates, and charger availabilities, demonstrating superior prediction accuracy and system utility

title: Interpretable Artificial Intelligence for Cyber Defense: Advances, Limitations, and Future Opportunities
authors: Sammy F, Thanammal Indu V, Saranya R, Priya R V, Lekha Doss, Ezhilvendan M
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: AI is being integrated into cybersecurity to a larger extent and offers improved ways and means of identifying threats including intrusion attempts, malware, and phishing attacks. Nevertheless, many AI models, including deep learning systems, are semantically complex, and this leaves significant questions about reliability, responsibility, and conformity to security applications. This paper focuses on explainability and transparency of security systems based on artificial intelligence and underlines the significance of these two components. Some of the main topics include; self-explanatory models, post hoc explanation methods such as LIME and SHAP and attentionbased models. The real-world use of explainable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> is shown by examples of intrusion detection, malware analysis, and phishing prevention. Some of the issues include model complexity versus accuracy, model size and model deployment, and decisionmaking in real-time, and adversarial perturbations are explained further. This paper also looks at the ethical, legal and regulatory issues, in addition to pointing to the fact that there is a need for a transparent system that is capable of satisfying the compliance needs as well as creating credibility. Last, directions for future work are presented with focus on explainability from the end users' perspective, and call for multi-disciplinary efforts to tackle technical and social issues. The goals of this survey is to provide researchers, practitioners, and policymakers with information to design and implement more effective and trustworthy XAI solutions for cybersecurity systems.

title: Development of Low Power Two Stage Op-Amp using 45nm Technology
authors: Abhay Chopde, Yash Kale, Parshuram Deshpande, Harsh Bide
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a fully compensated low-power two-stage operational amplifier implemented in 45 nm CMOS technology and operating at a supply voltage of 1.8 V. The amplifier consists of a differential input stage followed by a common-source gain stage with Miller compensation, and device sizing is optimized for low-power, high-speed operation. The target specifications included 50 dB gain, 20 MHz unity gain frequency (UGF), >20 V/µs slew rate, 60 dB common mode rejection ratio (CMRR), and 180 µW power, while simulations achieved 45 dB gain, 44.6 MHz UGF, 96.2 V/µs slew rate, 57° phase margin, and full compliance with CMRR and ICMR limits. The results demonstrate that high-performance analog operation can be achieved efficiently in nanoscale CMOS at low supply voltage through appropriate sizing and compensation techniques.

title: Web Application for the Automatic Code Generation of Fullstack Projects Using Generative Artificial Intelligence: GPT-5
authors: Alessandro David-Polanco, Mario Prado-Vargas, Lenis Wong
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: Due to the growing interest in the automatic generation of web applications using Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI), several tools have emerged that promise to reduce development time and improve productivity. In practice, current GenAI- based tools are often limited to frontend generation or restricted technology stacks, which leads to technical inconsistencies between layers, rework, and the lack of integrated quality, security, and accessibility controls. Therefore, this study proposes a web application that, starting from a Design System, generates fullstack projects through the orchestration of GenAI. The model is organized into four phases: (i) analysis of Design System tokens, (ii) GenAI orchestration, (iii) generation of the frontend in Angular and the backend in .NET Core, and (iv) quality and accessibility validation with SonarQube, Lighthouse and WAVE, additionally integrating automatic API documentation and security based on JWT/OAuth2. For validation, a functional prototype was implemented and experiments were conducted with two students, who generated multiple Angular/.NET modules and obtained automatic metrics of bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells, performance and accessibility. The results show that the solution successfully completes the end-to-end flow, produces projects aligned with the Design System and provides objective quality indicators that facilitate inspection and improvement, positioning it as a viable alternative for academic and, potentially, professional contexts.

title: Double Boost Nine-Level Inverter for Renewable Energy Systems with Reduced Circuit Elements
authors: M.Vijayakumar, R. Jeyabharath, M.Chitra, T.Sivaprakasam, K. Saravanan, A. Raja
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This paper introduces the Double Boost Nine-Level Inverter (DB9LI), a novel inverter topology that is intended to address common challenges associated with the extensive use of capacitors, multiple DC power supplies, and semiconductor switches in multilevel inverters. The proposed configuration generates a nine-level AC output voltage by utilizing a single DC input source, eight semiconductor switches, and three capacitors. Notably, the DB9LI increases the overall output voltage by doubling the input voltage level. The inverter implements a pulse width modulation strategy known as Variable Frequency Phase Opposition Disposition (VFPOD) in order to enhance voltage quality. The performance and feasibility of this inverter are evaluated through MATLAB-SIMULINK simulations that are conducted with varying modulation indices. The DB9LI's efficacy in renewable energy applications is illustrated by the results, which include an enhanced output waveform quality and a higher voltage gain with a reduced component count.

title: A Blockchain-based Finite State Machine for Decentralized Warranty Lifecycle Management
authors: S. Gopi, M. Kiran, Maddiboina Srikanth
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The conventional warranty systems do not provide enforceable guarantees on transfer of ownership, handling of claims, and expiration, especially in secondary-market and decentralized scenarios. Existing blockchain and NFT-based solutions primarily represent warranty ownership but fail to enforce correct lifecycle transitions. The present paper proposes an on-chain Finite State Machine (FSM) for warranty lifecycle management. All warranties are represented as stateful digital resources whose transitions-issuance, transfer of ownership, claim initiation, claim resolution, and expiration are implemented through guarded smart contract logic. Instead of increasing state granularity, the FSM uses transition constraints, temporal checks, and role-based authorization to ensure lifecycle correctness. The FSM is executed using Ethereum smart contracts and decentralized document storage through IPFS and tested on Sepolia test network. Results show that a minimal-state FSM guarantees lifecycle properties not found in ownership-only warranty models.

title: Software Reliability Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Ramesh Jana, Manmath Kumar Bhuyan, Chinmay Sahu, Nigar Parween, Chinmaya Rana, Deepak Kumar Mohanty
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: In an effort to improve the prediction accuracy and model interpretability, this paper presents a novel ensemble framework for software reliability prediction. Conventional reliability models are unable to capture nonlinear correlations and have the difficulty in handling high-dimensional, noisy data. The suggested approach groups software modules by code metrics and failure history using blended clustering, which combines K-Means and hierarchical clustering, enabling the emergence of various reliability patterns. Key reliability indicators within clusters are found using a hybrid feature selection technique. Various machine learning models are trained using these features and their predictions are combined using a cross-validated voting system. In terms of accuracy and other metrics, tests conducted on benchmark datasets show better performance than conventional approaches. The results show that the proposed work helps developers prioritize testing and enhance software quality at an early stage.

title: AI-Enabled Women Safety Surveillance with Instant Incident Prediction and Emergency Dispatch
authors: Sriramkumar R, Ankita Avthankar, M.Lakshmanan, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, R.Tamilamuthan, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Urban and semi-urban areas pose a severe threat to women, as any delay in intervening in their protection may have dire consequences. Current security systems with manual activation, if activated, prove utterly incapable in cases of physical confrontation or when a person is under stress. The research suggests an AI-based system for predicting and dispatching without requirement of any intervention, meant for empowering women to maintain their safety. This system would keep the data from different sensors such as accelerometer and gyroscope data and location data from the handheld device and audio data. On top of this, a mixed deep neural network model is proposed for anomaly detection in motion, spectral and feature based from audio signals to predict distress, and behavior profiling to predict route deviation analysis. This implies that a Threat Index Score, assuming the combined values of outliers and residuals are triggered, is generated. Alongside simultaneity of location and context-triggered alerts and registered to-leader poses to keep both law agencies and care-holds in the know. The new approach was subjected to real-world environments, where simulating attacks cut for a variety of activities, yielding several good results in anticipation, fewer false alarms, and rapid penetration time for a reasonable assumption of the system for use in dynamic women safety requirements.

title: Reinforcement-Learning Powered IoT Energy Grid for Adaptive Balancing of Clean and Renewable Power Sources
authors: Kolanchinathan V P, E. Balaji, C R Bharathi, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Recent smart grids are becoming reliant upon varying renewable electricity sources like solar, wind, and micro-hydro systems which bring instability, variability and non-linear supply-demand relationships. Traditional grid controllers that are based on optimization fail to cope with a quick change in the environment, which leads to power overload, energy curtails, and inefficient use of clean resources. This article presents a Reinforcement-Learning Powered IoT Energy Grid that will be used to reach the adaptive balancing of the renewable energy sources by applying distributed sensing, real-time decision-making, and autonomous regulation. The system is fitted with the multi-node IoT based energy meters, decentralized edge controllers, and the hierarchical based RL optimization agent, which can learn the optimal allocation strategies under variable loads. Large scale tests on 600 IoT-enabled energy nodes indicate that the proposed grid allows load imbalance to be reduced by 37 percent and renewable utilization to be more efficient by 41 percent and peak stress events to be reduced by 52 percent over rule-based baselines. The framework exhibits a high level of scalability, resilience to environmental uncertainties as well as the applicability to next-generation clean and sustainable energy infrastructure.

title: Portable IoT-Based Smart Farming Device For Real-Time Fertilizer Optimization And Crop Recommendation
authors: T. Baranidharan, Dharun Pradeep S, Dharaniboopathi M H, Harish V
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The growing necessity of sustainability in the agricultural process has made specific attention to fertilizers control and crop selection methods based on data significant. Conventional farming methods usually depend on the manual observation and generalized use of fertilizers that can cause the imbalance of nutrients in soil, overuse of chemicals, low productivity and the deterioration of soil in the long run. To solve these issues, this paper suggests a small-scale IoT-enhanced smart farming device with TinyML to obtain real-time optimized fertilizer and crop suggestions. This device has an NPK sensor that measures the main nutrients in soils such as nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium alongside a temperature sensor and humidity sensor to capture the environment. In contrast to traditional IoT systems, which rely on cloud processing to a great extent, the proposed system uses lightweight machine learning models that are deployed on the edge through TinyML and allow local, low-power and low-latency analysis. Depending on the chemical makeup of the soil and the current weather conditions, the device will make intelligent suggestions on the fertilizer to be applied and the right crops to be planted. The farmer will be able to make informed decisions in the field by obtaining recommendations via a friendly mobile or web interface. The system shows that the IoT and embedded intelligence can revolutionize the current state of farming through minimizing waste of fertilizers, improving the output and creating more environmentally-friendly farming methods. The given solution is not only more efficient but also offers a scalable, cost-effective and farmer-friendly solution, which will allow filling the gap between high-tech agricultural technologies and their real-life field implementation.

title: Multiple Fault Detection in EV Vehicles using AI to Avoid Fire Accidents
authors: Nivishna S, Sowmiya V, Srimathidevi P, Swathi D, Tharunika DK
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The rapid growth in the adoption of electric vehicles has raised the demand for dependable and effective battery safety mechanisms for preventing fire-related accidents. Battery failures due to overheating, electrical anomaly, or internal fault may result in serious safety hazards, loss of battery life, and injury to passengers. This work proposes an IoT-based battery safety system integrated with artificial intelligence to detect multiple fault conditions arising in an electric vehicle battery. The proposed system continuously monitors essential operational parameters of a battery such as temperature, voltage, current, and emitted gases through strategically located IoT sensors. The data so acquired is analyzed in real time through an intelligent decision-making model to identify abnormal operating conditions that can lead to a fire hazard. Upon detecting unsafe conditions, the system triggers safety actions like battery isolation, power shutdown, cooling activation, and alert generation. In addition, a cloud-based platform enables the transmission of data in real time, facilitating the users and service personnel of the vehicle to access remote information about battery health. This will enhance the early detection of faults, improve operational safety, and increase the overall reliability of the electric vehicle.

title: Sleep Disorder Prediction by using Machine Learning
authors: M. Janardhan, Mantenune Allavuddin, Kurva Umesh, Katika Imran, Shabolu Ashish
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Sleep disorders have become a prevalent health problem which can negatively impact one’s physical and mental state. Using a machine learning method, the sleep and health lifestyle attributes are included for sleep disorders classification. The proposed approach utilizes supervised machine learning algorithms administered using Python and various open source software utilities commonly used in data science for performance gains in predictions. To analyze the Sleep Health and Lifestyle dataset, quadratic discriminant analysis (QDA) and gradient boosting classifier (GBC) were applied. Once preprocessing and feature selection is completed models are trained and evaluated with standard metrics. The experimental results show that Gradient Boosting has a higher accuracy than QDA for early detection of sleep disorders. The developed system is a useful, scalable solution to assist medical practitioners to diagnose or make decisions in time.

title: Design and Development of a Drone-Based Autonomous Seed Dispensing Mechanism for Precision Reforestation
authors: Selim Halabi, Albaraa Alatar, Joseph Shaban, Yesim Oniz
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Large-scale reforestation in remote terrain requires aerial systems capable of precise and reliable seed deployment. Existing UAV seeding approaches often involve severe tradeoffs among placement accuracy, payload mass, and mechanical complexity. This paper presents the design and integration of an autonomous quadcopter featuring a modular, PWM-controlled seed-dispensing payload engineered for precision aerial reforestation. The integrated system is bounded by a Maximum Takeoff Weight (MTOW) of 12.939 kg, supporting a 5.0 kg granular payload capacity to optimize the thrust-to-weight ratio and flight endurance. The dispensing module utilizes a gravityfed volumetric mechanism with a customized internal spiral hopper geometry, designed specifically to prevent mechanical bridging during discharge. Electrical architecture ensures a maximum actuation power demand of only 4.0 W, preventing voltage sag on the primary flight battery. Empirical bench testing of the variable-rate PWM actuation validates high volumetric repeatability, achieving a 94.0 % successful dispensing rate across 50 consecutive actuation trials without mechanical jamming. These results validate the mechatronic viability of the system for sustained, low-impact ecological restoration.

title: An Intelligent Autonomous Robot for Precision Irrigation based on Soil Condition Monitoring
authors: Kanagamalliga S, Vinayagam P, Vignesh R, Sabarivasan V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: An automated smart irrigation and monitoring system was developed to support sustainable agriculture through real-time sensing, wireless communication, and controlled water delivery. Environmental and soil parameters were continuously acquired using a soil moisture sensor, humidity and temperature sensor, and an ultrasonic sensor for distance-based monitoring. The collected data were processed through an Arduino-based control unit, and irrigation decisions were generated using threshold-driven logic to ensure that watering was performed only when required. A motor driver interface was employed to activate the DC water pump safely and efficiently, while a battery-powered supply was utilized to enable portable field operation. System status and sensor readings were transmitted through Bluetooth communication and were displayed on a smartphone terminal to support remote supervision and timely user awareness. Experimental validation was carried out using a functional prototype, and stable real-time monitoring was achieved with consistent sensor updates. Performance evaluation indicated satisfactory accuracy, rapid response time, acceptable battery utilization, moderate speed, and high stability during repeated operational cycles. Water wastage was reduced by ensuring irrigation activation based on actual soil condition rather than fixed schedules. The reliable integration of sensing, decision-making, communication, and actuation was demonstrated, and the developed system was found suitable for precision irrigation applications in smart farming environments.

title: Real-Time DDoS Detection for SDN with Stacking Ensembles in OMNeT++
authors: Boushra Alnaima, Ömer Dakkak
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper demonstrates a real-time DDoS detection platform for software-defined networking environments, with OMNeT++ 6.0.3 discrete-event simulation with a Python-based stacking ensemble inference pipeline. The system integrates a custom C++ controller for live flow feature extraction, ZeroMQ for low-latency inter-process communication, and a three-layer stacking model comprising a decision tree, a random forest, and SVM base classifiers with a logistic regression meta-learner. Features are extracted in microsecond-scale dimensions consistent with the CIC-DDoS2019 dataset training corpus, eliminating the dimensional mismatch that commonly makes failures in live ML deployments. An adjusted attack threshold of 0.8 replaces the default 0.5 to avoid false positives during early flow warm-up. The system supports two attack scenarios: a single-burst manual trigger and a two-phase botnet emulation comprising a command-and-control phase followed by a zombie flood phase. Offline evaluation on a lot of samples, a 13,342 -sample held-out test set, achieves 99.61 % accuracy and an ROC-AUC of 0.9997 with a balanced confusion matrix of 28 false positives and 24 false negatives. This study constructed a dashboard that drives live charting of packet-rate with Matplotlib and Tkinter, topology state visualization, and auto-generated CSV reporting. The platform illustrates that the quality of offline classifiers is not enough to ensure the operational SDN security, but dimensional consistency, threshold calibration, and runtime observability are also vital system-level issues.

title: Thermal Performance and Energy Modeling of a Geodesic Dome
authors: Shreya Acharya, Serdar Celik
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This study focuses on the energy performance and HVAC efficiency of Buckminster Fuller's Dome which is located at the Southern Illinois University Edwardsville campus. The main objective of the research is to calculate the HVAC and lighting loads employing eQuest energy simulation software towards achieving a more sustainable building. The thermal behavior of the Dome is calculated based on its structural properties. HVAC design parameters such as chilled water and hot water coils and ventilation amount were examined to optimize energy consumption and occupant comfort. The study revealed that the geodesic dome can become more sustainable with modifications in building insulation, the HVAC system, and the ligting fixtures utilized. One significance of this research is that it allows to have a broader discussion on sustainable building design and energy-efficient climate control in unconventional structures such as Fuller's geodesic dome.

title: Federated Learning-based Intelligent Intrusion Detection for Secure and Scalable IoT Systems: A Comprehensive Review and Future Directions
authors: Rajesh Kumar K, S. M. Udhaya Sankar, Philomina Jenifer.A, P.Rashmi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) systems that are implemented in a decentralized fashion has significantly increased the attack surface of the modern smart environment, making it necessary to implement the smart and privacy-aware intrusion detection systems. The classical centralized intrusion detection systems (IDS) have also been constrained with very crucial limitations like privacy of information, high level of communication overheads, low level of scalability and low level of adaptation to the various network setups. In order to address these challenges, one of the most promising paradigms in distributed intelligence is federated learning (FL) that enables sharing models, but not data. The article is an elaborate presentation of intelligent intrusion detection architecture of secure and distributed IoT systems based on federated learning. We address the latest advances in the area of privacy-preserving model aggregation, explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) integration, adaptive learning models and zero-day and adversarial attacks protection. We also address the architectural design, communications efficiency, model robust and scalable concerns, with the real-world applications of IoT. Novel tendencies mentioned in the paper include hybrid deep learning structure, graph-based federated learning, secure aggregation algorithm, and automated fragmentation of the heterogeneous networks. Finally, the research topics and future outlook are suggested to present the way forward of creating the next-generation smart and safe intrusion detection systems in intelligent and dynamic IoT systems. The outcomes provide a single perspective to academicians and experts in the field of safe computer usage, intelligent data handling and distributed intelligent systems.

title: From the Pitch to the Virtual Workspace: Using an Autoethnographic Diary to Study Video Analysis as a Situated Practice in Finnish First-Tier Football
authors: Antti Nyman, Tommi Kärkkäinen, Antti Ekonoja, Hesam Mohseni, Daniel Schneider, António Correia
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: As football becomes increasingly data-driven, the role of the analyst has emerged as a key enabler for gaining insight into the needs of teams competing at the top level of national leagues. However, this role has yet to fully mature within the Finnish first-tier league (Veikkausliiga), largely due to infrastructural disparities and a lack of standardized professional pathways that continue to shape the Finnish football ecosystem. Drawing on a reflective practitioner perspective, this research employs autoethnography as method to examine the work practices and sociotechnical needs of a football analyst. We explore the situated challenges of video capture and processing, the weekly rhythms of training and match analysis, and the technological barriers in an environment where roles are often precarious and closely tied to individual coaching tenures. Our observations highlight a structural misalignment between the demands of high-performance sports analytics and the lived realities of analysts working in developing football leagues. This misalignment is illustrated through a detailed account of an analyst workflow in Finland.

title: Design and Implementation of Cloud-Edge Collaborative Mapping and Control System for Mobile Robots
authors: Chao Wang, Runze Gao, Jinshi Liu, Qiwen Li, Moqin Li, Xinyu Tao, Jinhui Liu, Yu Deng, Zhenglin Zou, Yuanqing Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: With the increasing complexity of mobile robot systems, multi-robot collaboration has become essential. However, such collaboration places heavy demands on both computational power and communication bandwidth. Cloud computing offers elastic and scalable infrastructure, allowing robotic functions to be encapsulated and delivered as on-demand services. Meanwhile, edge computing provides low-latency processing near the data source. The integration of these paradigms enables efficient coordination between cloud and edge infrastructures, forming a solution for real-time, scalable, and intelligent robotic control. This paper presents a cloud-edge collaborative system for mobile robots. Based on cloud-native technologies, the cloud control platform is implemented on Kubernetes (K8s) to orchestrate containerized control services, enabling dynamic resource allocation and automated deployment. An edge offloading mechanism is designed to support real-time data exchange and task coordination between robots and the cloud. Furthermore, a collaborative mapping and control framework is developed, where computationally intensive tasks such as map construction and trajectory generation are offloaded to the cloud, while real-time control and data pre-processing are executed at the edge. Experimental results in outdoor environments verify that the proposed framework significantly enhances system scalability and performance. These findings confirm the feasibility and generality of the proposed approach, highlighting its strong potential for large-scale deployment in cloud-based robotic systems.

title: Electromagnetic and Structural Trade-Offs of V-Position Magnet Embedding in Outer-Rotor PMSMs
authors: Daniil Zadorozhniuk, Ilya Petrov, Pia Lindh, Juha Pyrhönen
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: V-position magnet embedding is widely used in interior permanent-magnet synchronous machines (IPM-PMSMs) to improve field-weakening capability, with its design principles largely established for inner-rotor topologies. In outer-rotor permanent magnet synchronous machines, however, magnet embedding directly interacts with rotor structural constraints, fundamentally altering its design implications. This paper investigates the influence of V-position magnet embedding in an outer-rotor PMSM through a controlled study in which a reference surface-magnet design is transformed into a V-position interior-magnet configuration while preserving stator geometry, pole arc, magnet height, and comparable voltage–speed operating conditions. Magnet embedding is parameterized using a geometric variable termed V-depth, defined as the radial distance from the air gap to the magnet edges at the pole center. The results reveal that, in contrast to inner-rotor machines, adjustments to V-depth in outer-rotor PMSMs introduce a pronounced interplay between electromagnetic performance and mechanical constraints. Any increase in magnet embedding depth necessitates corresponding changes in rotor yoke thickness and overall dimensions. This coupling governs not only the achievable torque and field-weakening characteristics, but also introduces tangible trade-offs in structural mass and packaging. Consequently, electromagnetic torque and specific torque follow different trends: while torque may partially recover at larger V-depth values due to increased magnet width, the concurrent increase in structural mass prevents a corresponding recovery in specific torque. These findings demonstrate that magnet embedding in outer-rotor PMSMs cannot be treated as a purely electromagnetic design variable, but must instead be considered as a coupled electromagnetic–structural problem. A moderate V-depth range provides the most favourable compromise between torque capability, mass, and field-weakening performance.

title: Robust Decision-Level Fusion for Multimodal Fall Detection under Controlled Sensor Noise
authors: Carla Taramasco, Pablo Ormeño-Arriagada, Evilasio Costa, Rossana Maria De Castro Andrade, David Araya
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Falls among older adults represent a critical public health issue, being one of the leading causes of severe injuries and accident-related mortality. because of this, Fall Detection Systems face significant challenges due to the complexity of environments and variability in movement patterns. On this matter, Data fusion, including method-level, sensor-level, feature-level, and decision-level approaches, offers a promising method to enhance detection accuracy. However, decision-level fusion remains under-explored, despite its potential to integrate outputs from multiple classifiers to address these limitations. This article presents a comparative study on the performance of decision-level fusion strategies in fall detection systems, employing different configurations of sensors, classifiers, and decision fusion algorithms. A dataset was collected using four sensor modalities: Accelerometer, Infrared Camera, Light Detection and Ranging, and Radar. Three classifiers were employed, and five decision-level fusion strategies were evaluated. Performance was assessed across three levels of simulated noise using four metrics: accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and precision. The implementation of decision-level fusion strategies resulted in improvements in the performance of fall detection systems. Accuracy increased from 85% without decision-level fusion to 93% using Random Forest. Among deterministic strategies, Voting with Accuracy achieved competitive results. Light Detection and Ranging, demonstrated robustness under noise, while other sensors showed varying sensitivity to noise. These results demonstrate the importance of utilizing decision-level fusion strategies, as they lead to improvements in system performance. This enhancement is achieved by leveraging the complementarity of multiple technologies, allowing systems to adapt effectively to diverse and dynamic environments. This functionality is particularly useful in standalone or closed solutions, where such complementarity can address inherent limitations and enhance overall system capabilities.

title: FR3 OFDM Multistatic ISAC Geometry-Aware Multi-Link Fusion and CRLB-Guided Design
authors: Shiv Shankar, Radha Krishna Ganti, J Klutto Milleth
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper presents a geometry-aware multi-link fusion framework for multistatic integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) in urban Frequency Range 3 (FR3) orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) channels, aligned with International Mobile Telecommunications–2030 (IMT-2030) sensing use cases. For each transmitter–receiver link, channel frequency responses estimated from orthogonal New Radio (NR) pilot resources are transformed into a 4-D Delay–Doppler–Azimuth–Elevation cube. Targets are detected using cell-averaging constant false-alarm rate (CA-CFAR) processing followed by angle-domain peak refinement, then back-projected into 3-D space and fused across links via Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) to obtain object-level estimates. Full 3-D velocities are recovered by least-squares inversion of multi-link Doppler measurements. To guide deployment, we derive Fisher-information/Cramer–Rao lower bound (CRLB) expressions that reveal geometry-dependent observability limits and motivate region-of-interest link-selection rules. Realistic propagation is modeled by combining 3GPP TR 38.901 structure with NVIDIA Sionna ray tracing, including deterministic reflections, clutter, and object-dependent radar cross sections. Simulations show ≈ 94% detection probability with ≈ 5% false alarms for up to five simultaneous targets, with median per-axis errors of roughly 4–5 m in position and ∼ 1 m/s in velocity.

title: Cosine-Resonant Modular Multilevel DC–DC Converter With ZVS Capability for High-Efficiency Hydrogen Production System
authors: Aritra Basu, Liangcai Shu, Dongsheng Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The modular multilevel resonant DC converter (R-MMDC) has emerged as a key topology for medium-voltage DC (MVDC) systems, offering superior scalability, MV fault isolation, and operational reliability. However, its overall efficiency is constrained by the hard switching behavior of submodule switches. To overcome this limitation, this article introduces a novel cosine-resonant modular multilevel DC converter (CR-MMDC), which leverages submodule capacitors as intrinsic resonant elements, eliminating the need for conventional series resonant capacitors typically integrated with transformer windings. Due to the specific cosine-resonance pattern, this innovation enables zero-voltage switching (ZVS) across a broad transmission power range, significantly enhancing converter efficiency. In addition, the removal of external resonant components contributes to a more compact system architecture. A thorough theoretical analysis of the CR-MMDC’s operating principles is presented, alongside a systematic design methodology. The proposed converter is validated through detailed simulations and scaled experimental prototypes, demonstrating its effectiveness and practical viability for MVDC applications.

title: A Low Jitter Pulse Generator for Electrically Excited Transient Absorption
authors: Wenzhe Zhang, Hengxuan Li, Bo Li, Fengjia Fan, Xi Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Electrically excited transient absorption (EETA) is a powerful technique for investigating the dynamics of charge carriers in semiconductor devices. Pulse generator plays an important role in the EETA, as it generates electrical excitation with accurate timing and variable output voltage. In order to investigate the nanosecond scale fast dynamics of the semiconductor devices, a pulse generator with a picosecond time jitter is favored. To meet the excitation intensity requirement of the devices under test, a maximum output voltage higher than 10 V is also required for the PG. However, the existing studies and the off-the-shelf pulse generators cannot meet the time jitter and output amplitude requirements simultaneously. In this work, a pulse generator for EETA is proposed with picosecond time jitter and variable output voltage. The proposed pulse generator is composed of trigger delay compensation, time folding alignment delay lines, amplitude tuning circuit, master-slave driver, and other peripheral modules. The externally triggered timing jitter is significantly reduced by introducing trigger delay compensation and time folding alignment delay lines. The output voltage is tunable via both the amplitude tuning circuit and the master-slave driver with low timing jitter. The output voltage of the pulse generator ranges from -10 V to 20 V. The time control resolution of the pulse generator is 5 ps, and a low externally triggered jitter below 15.4 ps is achieved. The proposed pulse generator has been applied in electrically excited transient absorption spectra acquisition to verify its performance, and it is favorable for studying electrically excited transient absorption.
