title: Efficient and Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning with Gradient Control against Data Poisoning_supp1-3699615.pdf
authors: jianhong zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training across decentralized clients while preserving data privacy. However, practical deployments are often limited by significant communication overhead and vulnerability to Byzantine poisoning attacks. Existing defenses typically rely on post-hoc anomaly detection, but executing complex distance metrics or clustering on encrypted, sparsified updates creates a substantial computational burden for the aggregation server. We present a privacy-preserving FL framework that addresses these challenges by integrating Top-k sparsification, non-interactive zero-knowledge proofs (NIZKPs), and homomorphic encryption. Instead of relying on expensive ciphertext distance computations, our architecture uses a pre-aggregation global mask sign vector, generated through majority voting, to filter anomalous updates. This mechanism treats unselected gradient coordinates as explicit zero-votes, which mitigates malicious coalitions attempting to manipulate disjoint parameter subsets. A local error feedback mechanism ensures that heterogeneous client updates align over successive training rounds. Combined with NIZKPs to enforce coordinate-wise magnitude bounds, the framework provides Byzantine resilience without increasing communication costs or compromising privacy. Evaluations on MNIST and CIFAR-10 show that our approach maintains high communication efficiency and robustness. Under a 40% malicious client poisoning attack and a 50% sparsification ratio, the framework achieves final accuracies of 92.14% and 63.20%, respectively, demonstrating its effectiveness in bandwidth-constrained, hostile environments.

title: Research and Experimental Verification of RCS Reduction of Metal Plate Targets by Plasma
authors: Chengwei Zhao, Yulei Fu, Yannian Wei, Tianning Tang, Ruixue Shi, Qing Han, Qiang Wei, Fangwei Lv, Chenchen Wu, Wenchong Ouyang, Zheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: Stealth technology is of great significance for the survival of targets such as aircraft, and plasma stealth is an effective and broadband stealth technology. The stealth effect of plasma mainly depends on the matching relationship between electromagnetic waves and plasma parameters. In this article, based on auxiliary differential equation finite-difference time-domain (ADE-FDTD), simulations were conducted to reduce the radar cross section (RCS) from multiple parameters of plasma, including electron density range, distribution, collision frequency, and thickness. Meanwhile, the analysis of the optimal RCS reduction electron density distribution was studied within a given range of electron densities. At a plasma thickness of 1.67 times the low-frequency wavelength, an RCS attenuation of −11.4 dB was achieved within a 57% bandwidth range. Finally, based on ground simulation of inductively coupled plasma (ICP), a radar scattering system was built, and RCS reduction tests were conducted under plasma coverage in the frequency range of 10–13 GHz. The simulation calculation was carried out based on plasma parameters, and the simulation and test results were in good agreement, with an error of less than 3 dB. This article has important reference value for the stealth of aircraft or other targets.

title: Block Term Decomposition-Guided Frequency Mamba Modulation for Hyperspectral Image Fusion
authors: Yan Li, Chuangjie Fang, Zhijie Chen, Yiqun Meng, Honghui Xu, Hao Wang, Yi-Peng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Owing to the complementary spatial-spectral characteristics of hyperspectral images (HSIs) and multispectral images (MSIs), HSI-MSI fusion has become an effective approach for generating high-resolution hyperspectral images (HR-HSIs). Recent deep learning frameworks, particularly Mamba-based architectures, provide efficient long-range dependency modeling with near-linear complexity. Nevertheless, they often overlook the intrinsic low-rank structure of hyperspectral data and are largely insensitive to frequency-domain information. To address these limitations, we propose block term decomposition-guided frequency Mamba modulation (BFMM), a low-rank tensor modulation framework built upon block term decomposition (BTD). BFMM represents features as a sum of multiple Tucker components, each reconstructed by the multimode product of a learnable Tucker core and low-rank factor matrices, thereby preserving structured spatial-spectral dependencies. A frequency Mamba joint modulation (FMJM) unit learns frequency-aware low-rank Tucker cores through adaptive frequency conditioning, and a Kronecker-rank interaction modulator (KRIM) learns spatial-spectral factor matrices via separable channel-rank guidance in the form of Kronecker products. The modulated core tensors are sequentially contracted with the learned factors through mode-n products, thereby reconstructing low-rank feature tensors whose aggregation yields structurally interpretable and information-dense representations. Across three public datasets, BFMM achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance with a favorable accuracy-complexity trade-off, and is further validated on the WHU-MHF and WV-3 datasets. The source code is available at https://github.com/lirui818/BFMM.

title: Comprehensive Efficiency Optimizations of Variable Flux Memory Machines for Electric Vehicle Tractions Considering Driving Cycles
authors: Wang He, Hao Hua, Wei Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The optimization of variable flux memory machines (VFMMs) considering overall efficiency are investigated. A multiobjective hierarchical optimization method is developed to address the flexibly variable magnetic states (MSs) of variable permanent magnets and the diverse operation conditions of the traction machine. The three-layer optimization is established based on the sensitivity results of design parameters. The typical operation points are clustered via the K-means algorithm to cover the entire operation conditions, based on which the optimization efficiency is boosted. A case study is elaborated to illustrate the optimization process and validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. The results confirm the enhanced overall efficiency thanks to the adjustable MSs of the VFMMs. Moreover, based on the developed optimization strategy, the overall efficiency of the optimized case improves by approximately 1% compared with the initial design, together with the 12% reduction of the active material weight. The experimental verifications based on a small-scaled VFMM prototype are conducted to confirm the analyses.

title: Acoustic Emission Source Localization in Composite Pressure Vessels Based on an Omnidirectional Wave Velocity Matrix
authors: Xiangdong Ma, Xiao Liang, Yanbing Zhang, Shen He, Zhongyuan Xu, Xiao Wu, Hui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Composite pressure vessels (CPVs) are widely employed in high-pressure energy storage systems owing to their exceptional strength-to-weight ratios and corrosion resistance. However, the inherent anisotropy arising from multi-angle fiber winding and complex curved geometries significantly complicates acoustic emission (AE) wave propagation, causing notable inaccuracies in conventional localization methods that assume a homogeneous medium with constant wave velocity. To address this challenge, this study proposes an advanced AE source localization method based on an omnidirectional wave velocity matrix and attenuation factor correction. The omnidirectional wave velocity matrix is systematically constructed from pencil lead break (PLB) experiments performed at various angles and distances, effectively capturing spatial variations in wave propagation velocities. Concurrently, exponential attenuation models for individual propagation directions are derived using nonlinear least squares fitting, compensating for the errors introduced by constant wave velocity assumptions. Experimental validations demonstrate that the proposed approach markedly reduces localization errors, maintaining most errors within 5%. Furthermore, experiments conducted on different CPVs and feature points confirm the high consistency of wave velocity distributions and attenuation characteristics, indicating limited impact from localized fiber winding asymmetries on overall wave propagation behavior. The hydraulic burst test further validates the effectiveness of the proposed method in practical engineering scenarios. Consequently, this method significantly enhances AE source localization accuracy in CPVs, providing critical support and valuable reference for structural health monitoring and defect localization in composite structures.

title: Design of a Geographic Information System for Monitoring the Electrical Distribution Network: A Sustainability Approach
authors: Regina Ibarra Bustamante, Lorena Hernandez Casarrubias, Ivan Bustamante Salaya, Juan Pablo Cárdenas De Dios, Josue Aaron Lopez Leyva
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a Geographic Information System for monitoring electrical distribution networks and transitioning to smart grids. The project was developed in Python for an electrical network in Ensenada, Mexico. The system integrates spatial and electrical data into an interactive web platform. The results demonstrate the system's ability to visualize medium- and lowvoltage assets, query attributes, and enhance situational awareness. The discussion highlights that this solution overcomes the limitations of existing fragmented systems and promotes sustainability by improving operational efficiency, enabling the integration of renewable energy sources, and fostering equitable service.

title: Anomaly Detection and Compensation System on Solar Smart Inverters Using Isolation Forest
authors: Zheyu Zhang, Tafsir Ahmed Khan, Saifur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) are increasingly deployed to meet the growing demand for clean and sustainable energy. Smart inverters serve as the key interface for integrating photovoltaic (PV) systems into the grid by enabling monitoring, communication, and control. However, the rapid proliferation of smart inverters expands the cyberattack surface, increasing system vulnerability. As PV penetration grows, enhanced communication between inverters and control centers becomes necessary for coordinated energy management, but these expanded links create additional entry points for malicious intrusions that may disrupt grid stability. To protect PV output measurements from cyberattacks, Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) have been developed. Most existing machine-learning-based IDSs rely on large datasets or a single master device, creating significant security risks if either is compromised. To address this limitation, we propose a spatial-correlation-based approach that leverages the inherent consistency among neighboring PV panels. An Isolation Forest-based clustering method identifies abnormal inverter behavior without requiring large centralized datasets. The outputs from normal devices are then utilized to reconstruct compromised measurements, mitigating the impact of cyberattacks and enhancing system resilience.

title: Detection of Prompt Attacks in LLMs
authors: Yuktha Vijayakumar, Vishnu Praba A J, Saraswathi Shunmuganathan
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: LLMs (Large Language Models) used in practical implementations have been identified to be very vulnerable to direct prompt injection attacks: when hostile users trigger LLMs by providing input that leads to the LLM ignoring its own instruction and safety protocols/settings. LLMs with existing solutions meant to mitigate prompt injection from being successful, such as static filters and single-stage classifiers, would not be effective against obfuscated, adaptive, and multi-turn prompt injection attacks. This paper describes a new technique called Adversarial Robust Multi-layer Training (ARM-LT) which is real-time, model-agnostic, and designed to detect direct prompt injections at the prompt level. It uses structural prompt segmentation, canonical rewriting of multi-turn prompts, and perplexity based anomaly detection to identify hidden malicious intent before executing the LLM. The ARM-LT framework uses an iterative adversarial training loop in order to provide added robustness to the detection system against adaptive attackers by repeatedly reinforcing the detection system with semantically based adversarial prompt injection attempts. Evaluated a dataset with 78,558 direct prompt injection samples across 6 domains, ARM-LT produces significantly lower attack success rates than traditional machine learning baselines and also supports low inference overhead. This evidence shows that the need for dedicated security layers is critical to ensuring prompt injection is reliably prevented in any LLM integrated system.

title: Token-Budget-Aware Context Selection for RAG Via Integer Linear Programming
authors: Samuel Taiwo, Mohd Amaluddin Yusoff, Kelechi Enyioha
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances large language models by incorporating externally retrieved context into the model's input. However, most RAG systems construct context using heuristic top- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> selection strategies that do not explicitly account for token budget constraints or redundancy among retrieved chunks. As large language models operate under strict context length limitations, naive ranking-based selection may include redundant evidence and limit topical coverage, particularly in tasks requiring cross-document synthesis. This paper formulates context construction in RAG as a tokenconstrained combinatorial optimization problem. Specifically, we model chunk selection as a binary ILP that maximizes aggregate relevance scores subject to a fixed token budget. Unlike heuristic truncation approaches, the proposed framework enables principled selection under explicit constraints and is extensible to structured constraints such as diversity control. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that ILP-based selection achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \times$</tex> improvement in aggregate relevance score and a 66 % win rate in LLM-as-Judge pairwise evaluation over the Top<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> baseline under equal token budgets. The results indicate that structured, constraint-aware context selection provides a more reliable foundation for RAG systems.

title: ABHA's Personalised Clinical Intelligence Assistant
authors: Karthick Rajapandiyan, Sridhar L
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) under India's Digital Health Mission (ABDM) provides citizens with unique digital health identities, enabling secure access to medical records across hospitals, diagnostic centers, and wellness facilities. This integration of prescriptions, clinical notes, imaging data, and vaccination histories creates a foundation for data-driven healthcare innovation. ABCIA is a comprehensive AI-augmented framework transforming ABHA data into actionable clinical insights. Machine learning models detect disease patterns and predict health risks from multimodal datasets, while natural language processing automates clinical history summarization. Interactive dashboards present insights comprehensibly to clinicians and patients. The system uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) integrated with domain-specific Large Language Models for enhanced clinical decision support. Key components include semantic indexing of health records, context-aware retrieval, generative risk stratification, and privacy-preserving federated learning compliant with ABDM standards. Data privacy and regulatory compliance are prioritized throughout the architecture. Experimental validation on real-world lab reports (Vitamin D and B12 trends) demonstrated 100% extraction accuracy on parsed PDFs, producing CSV summaries, Markdown reports, and trend visualizations. ABCIA enhances preventive care, optimizes treatment planning, and enables population-level analytics through automated health summaries and predictive risk modeling. By fusing ABHA's data infrastructure with AI-driven analytics, this scalable approach builds a predictive, personalized, and participatory healthcare ecosystem-positioning it as a transformative enabler for healthcare in resource-constrained settings.

title: Deep Learning-Based Fault Detection Framework for Predictive Maintenance in HVAC Systems
authors: Muhammad Usman, Faizan Hamayat, Alishba Mahnoor, Muhammad Faheem, Haider Ali, Laraib Hamayat
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) systems account for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 - 6 0 \%}$</tex> of a building's energy use while maintaining comfort and air quality. Faults such as sensor drift, coil fouling, and refrigerant leakage degrade performance, increase energy costs, and shorten equipment lifespan. Conventional fault detection methods, such as rule-based, physicsbased, and traditional machine learning-based techniques, suffer from limited feature design, weak temporal modeling, and poor scalability across multiple systems. This paper presents a deep learning-based framework for predictive maintenance of multiple Air Handling Units (AHUs) using multivariate timeseries data from six units. Four architectures, including ANN, LSTM, CNN-BiLSTM, and Transformer, were developed and evaluated under a unified preprocessing pipeline addressing noise, imbalance, and missing data. The Transformer model achieved superior performance with accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9998,0.6402,0.2967$</tex>, and 0.3739, respectively, demonstrating better generalization across multiple AHUs. The results underscore the significance of deep learning, particularly attention-based models, for scalable, energy-efficient HVAC fault detection in smart built environments.

title: Stochastic Multi-Objective Planning of Smart Integrated Renewable Energy System (SIRES) under Demand and Resource Uncertainty
authors: Zeel Maheshwari, Shariqa Tayabee
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This work presents a stochastic multi-objective optimization framework for Smart Integrated Renewable Energy System (SIRES) that jointly delivers electricity, water pumping, and clean cooking services in rural communities. A Monte Carlo uncertainty model is developed to capture hourly variability in renewable resources, hydrological inflows, electricity demand, and water consumption. These uncertainty scenarios are embedded within a modified NSGA-III algorithm that minimizes the annualized cost of the system and life-cycle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions while enforcing probabilistic reliability constraints for both electricity and water supply. A hierarchical dispatch strategy coordinates photovoltaic and wind generation, biogas allocation for cooking and pumping, and seasonal hydropower operation. Results using 1000 Monte Carlo scenarios demonstrate two dominant families of robust SIRES designs: wind-dominant configurations with lower cost and photovoltaic-heavy pumping architectures with reduced reliability variability. The proposed framework enables uncertainty-aware planning of integrated energy and water systems, offering resilient and economically viable solutions for rural development.

title: Neural Network-Driven Optimization of Quality Engineering Metrics for High-Volume Healthcare Claims and EDI Transaction Performance
authors: Devi Manoharan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: High-volume healthcare claims processing requires continuous monitoring and improvement of Quality Engineering (QE) metrics to maintain reliable Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) workflows under heavy workloads. This study presents a neural network–driven framework for predicting claim-level quality risks and evaluating their impact on transaction-level performance within a controlled simulation environment. Healthcare claim records from the Kaggle "Medicare Claims Synthetic Public Use Files" dataset were preprocessed and used to extract structured claim attributes for predictive modeling. A hybrid Deep Neural Network (DNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) architecture was developed to learn both claim-specific characteristics and stage-aware sequential patterns to estimate rejection and validation error probabilities. Because the dataset does not contain operational transaction logs or timing information, a synthetic EDI transaction simulator was constructed to emulate workflow stages, delays, retries, and processing outcomes, enabling computation of QE metrics such as simulated latency, throughput, retry rate, and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance. Predicted risk scores were incorporated into a threshold-based routing strategy to assess improvements in simulated system behavior. The proposed approach achieved 93.4% classification accuracy and demonstrated reductions in simulated failure rates with improved throughput and SLA compliance. Overall, the framework provides a scalable and reproducible method for studying and optimizing healthcare claims processing performance without relying on proprietary operational logs.

title: Osteoporosis and Osteopenia Detection Using RPE-Augmented Vision Transformers (RPE-ViT)
authors: Ananya Maiya, Kinnera Sri Myla, Anisha Raghav, Shruthi Mlj
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Osteoporosis and osteopenia are two progressive bone density disorders that cannot be easily diagnosed at an early stage using X-ray images, as they involve slight changes in cortical thickness and trabecular structures. Although DEXA scans are currently considered the best method for detecting bone mineral density, they are expensive and not readily available for large-scale testing. Knee X-ray images, on the other hand, are commonly obtained in clinical settings, and we propose using them to obtain a cost-effective solution for early detection of osteoporosis and osteopenia. To accomplish this, we propose a Vision Transformer architecture named RPEViT, where we utilize a pre-trained DeiT-Base Patch16-224 model and enhance its performance using relative positional encoding to accurately determine spatial relationships in bone structures within knee X-ray images. To enhance knee Xray images, we utilize CLAHE enhancement, morphological filtering, and masking operations. The model is trained using AdamW optimization, cosine learning-rate scheduling, and Mixup regularization to improve stability on limited medical data. Experimental evaluation on a publicly available knee radiograph dataset achieved an overall classification accuracy of 85 %, with F1-scores of 0.85 (Normal), 0.83 (Osteopenia), and 0.80 (Osteoporosis). Attention visualizations indicate that the model consistently focuses on clinically meaningful bone regions. These results suggest that relative positional encoding improves structural representation in Vision Transformers and shows promise for low-cost opportunistic screening of bone density conditions using routine radiographs.

title: Non-Invasive Multi-Vitamin Deficiency Monitoring System
authors: Naseeya Begum, Rithvika Shekar, Tejasvin Kansal, S Vasanthadev Suryakala, T. Rajalakshmi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Vitamin deficiencies and excess of vitamins called hypovitaminosis and hypervitaminosis respectively, have long been known to cause many acute and chronic diseases. If they are left undiagnosed and untreated, vitamin deficiencies can lead to a range of severe consequences, including neurological, cardiovascular, and immune system disorders. Most of the vitamin level tracking methods are based on blood sampling and analysis, which is accurate but invasive and time-consuming, and thus not suitable for regular monitoring. Current technologies have come far but remain limited in terms of continuous and real-time vitamin deficiency detection. In this paper, a non-invasive approach for vitamin level monitoring based on the analysis of physiological signals has been demonstrated. The proposed system uses a combination of physiological features, such as skin conductivity, heart rate variability and blood oxygen saturation which are correlated with skin hydration, oxidative stress and autonomic responses, to continuously and conveniently classify vitamin B12 and C deficiency. A series of experiments were conducted to draw the relation between physiological parameters and vitamin deficiencies. The developed system was tested to detect the characteristic vital signs in a random set of subjects, and the system accurately identified the vitamin deficiency status of 9 out of the 10 participants involved in the study. This shows the reliability of the system in classifying deficiencies and paves a way for continuous, non-invasive monitoring of vitamin levels, and thus can be applied to preventive diagnostics and personalized medicine.

title: Urban Sustainability Semantic Association with UN SDG Targets: Socio-Technical Systems View
authors: Alekhya Velagapudi, David Tipper, Katrina Kelly-Pitou
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Achieving the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) requires understanding complex interdependencies between targets, particularly for urban sustainability (SDG 11). Treating cities as socio-technical systems (STS), this paper uses Sentence-BERT embeddings and network analysis to quantify the semantic relationships between seven SDG 11 targets and 100 targets from SDG 1-16. Our analysis reveals that affordable housing (11.1) and public space accessibility (11.7) exhibit the strongest connectivity. Interestingly, sustainable urbanization (11.3) shows no significant relationships. Community detection identifies six interdependent target communities, including a housing-public spaces community linked to poverty, health, education, and equality goals. Validation against LeBlanc's prior research confirms our findings. The resulting weighted communities provide planners with a reproducible basis for assessing cobenefits and trade-offs in urban development.

title: QR Code based Indoor Navigation for Mobile Robot
authors: S. Anuvarshini, A. Peniel Winifred Raj, S.Pranesh, S. Kirubalan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Indoor navigation of mobile robots in GPS-denied environments is typically handled by sensor-heavy and computationally intensive approaches such as LiDAR-based simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM). This paper proposes a low-cost, infrastructure-assisted indoor navigation solution for mobile robots using ground-fixed Quick Response (QR) codes. In the proposed system, a monocular camera mounted on the robot and oriented towards the floor is continuously scanning for QR codes placed along pre-defined indoor routes. Each QR code contains a unique symbolic identifier, while the structured navigation data such as directional instructions, motion parameters, and waypoint transitions are defined in a centralized YAML configuration file in a ROS 2 environment. The robot directly decodes the QR code information and makes real-time motion control decisions such as forward motion, turning, and stop actions without the need for pre-constructed maps, localization, or range sensors for navigation and localization. The proposed system is lower in complexity, cost, and computational requirements while ensuring robust indoor navigation. Experimental validation is carried out to evaluate the accuracy, reliability, and robustness of the proposed system under different lighting conditions and QR code placement strategies. The experimental results show that QR code-based navigation is an efficient and viable alternative to traditional sensor-intensive indoor navigation systems for controlled indoor environments.

title: DenseNet Framework for MRI-Based Alzheimer's Disease Classification with Feature Enhancement
authors: A Bajulunisha, Preethi Angaline R, S P Anbukodi, G Suresh, R Mahalakshmi, N Dhivyadevi
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: A study of DenseNet-based deep learning architecture designed to enhance multi-class diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease using MRI analysis. The method includes a comprehensive preprocessing pipeline and a feature enhancement technique that utilizes copula-based distribution alignment and high-fidelity synthetic augmentation to address class imbalance and strengthen minor structural representations. The tight connection of DenseNet optimizes feature reuse, allowing the model to detect subtle neuroanatomical alterations associated with cognitive decline. Quantitative metrics such as FID, SSIM, PSNR, and sharpness difference guarantee the trustworthiness of improved MRI samples used in training. The suggested model, using the Kaggle Alzheimer's Dataset, exhibits robust diagnostic proficiency throughout four phases, attaining an accuracy of 98.44%, precision of 96.97%, recall of 96.88%, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 96.92. These findings validate the framework's efficacy in generating reliable and clinically interpretable predictions. The system integrates dense feature propagation with distribution-aware augmentation, providing a robust and scalable method for early Alzheimer's identification and enhancing subsequent clinical decision-making.

title: Discourse Modeling for Text-based Emotion Recognition Using Fusion Approaches
authors: Manjog Padhy, Rasmita Rautray, Premananda Sahu
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Emotion recognition based on conversation data is an essential element of the intelligent human-computer interaction. However, it is a difficult task to accurately identify emotions from spoken or written dialogue due to the variability of expressions of individuals. Many current methods accept sequence processing of utterances and thus do not cover the discourse-level relationships. In order to solve these shortcomings, in this paper, a novel hybrid framework named Discourse Net Emotion Recognition (DNER) is proposed, which takes both the form of discourse-aware contextual modeling and deep sequence learning. The architecture proposed merges discourse embeddings based on BERT with Bi-LSTM and LSTM network which allows one to effectively track emotional dynamics between multiple turns of conversations. The proposed DNER model is tested on popular benchmark conversational emotion datasets, i.e. IEMOCAP. Comprehensive experimental analysis shows that DNER allows always better result compare to the state-of-the-art methods with an accuracy of 98.34%, a precision of 98.12%, a recall of 97.96% and the average F1 score of 98.20% on all the datasets. These results confirm that adding a discourse modeling and a multi-level feature fusion with ensemble significantly improves the performance of emotion recognition than the conventional sequential approaches. Future work will be to extend the model for multimodal emotion recognition for real-time conversational analysis.

title: Untrashify 2.0: A Real-Time High-Precision E-Waste Audit System Using Efficientdet-D5 and UNU-KEYS Taxonomy
authors: Sakthitharan Subramanian, Prasheth P, Logapriyan R, Sparsh Goyal
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Electronic waste (e-waste) poses a growing environmental and health threat, with millions of tons generated annually containing hazardous materials requiring specialized disposal. This paper presents Untrashify, a real-time e-waste detection and tracking system that combines EfficientDet-D5 object detection with the UNU-KEYS e-waste classification taxonomy (77 classes) to automate hazardous material identification and provide intelligent recycling recommendations. Our hybrid architecture leverages cloud-based training on Google Colab with Roboflow dataset management, while deploying locally via NVIDIA Triton Inference Server for sub-50ms latency on consumer hardware (RTX 4060). The system achieves real-time performance (30 FPS) with automatic flagging of 35 hazardous e-waste categories including lithium batteries, CRT displays, and circuit boards containing heavy metals. Integration with a FastAPI backend and React frontend enables continuous tracking across video streams, aggregation of detection statistics, and generation of recycling facility recommendations. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of deploying state-of-the-art object detection models for practical e-waste management workflows, bridging the gap between AI research and environmental sustainability applications.

title: An AI Enabled Framework for Reliable V2X Communication in Intelligent Transportation Systems
authors: Kodeeswari.K, B.Hemalatha, Sogy K Idicula, Dr.S.Suguna, Hussain. A, Dr.R.Vinoth
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This work suggests an AI based reliability enhancement mechanism for V2X communication in ITS. Traditional IEEE 802.11p and 5G NR V2X systems are suffering from performance loss with high mobility and dense traffic. To enhance efficiency, the system model adopts a LSTM based reliability prediction, DRL driven cross layer optimization, FL assisted with attacker collaboration attack in hierarchical edge–cloud structure and AI assisted anomaly detection. The LSTM is utilized to predict link reliability based on mobility, SINR, vehicle density and historical packet delivery information and the DRL dynamically optimizes power control and resource allocation. In SUMO–NS 3 coupled simulations, remarkable performance gain are exhibited: reliability reaches 97.3% at 140 km/h (9% better as compared to NR V2X), PDR achieves 95.6% in crowded traffic scenario, throughput increases by 18–22% with high interference level, latency jitter lowers to +8% during topological changes, energy cost witnesses a drop at the level of 2.12 mJ per packet. This decision latency of in total 10.6 ms also meets requirements for URLLC, demonstrating real time and scalability feasibility.

title: Low Energy Predictive Routing Framework for Long Lifetime Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: C. Kaushik, Vishnu Vardhan B, M. Venkateswara Rao, R. Sai Preetham Goud
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Network (WSN) are being deployed in various fields of application, including industrial automation, intelligent building systems, and environmental surveillance. Limited battery capacity and unexpected node failures limit the life cycle and stability of operation of such networks. The traditional routing algorithms, like the Minimum Transmission Energy (MTE) routing, choose the routes between two nodes using physical distance between them or the remaining energy capacity, without considering the possibility of future node failures. To overcome this shortcoming, the current paper presents the Data Driven Predictive Network Routing (DDPN) system that combines machine-learning-based node failure prediction, in this case, a Random Forest Classifier, with an energy-aware routing algorithm. Simulation experiments show that DDPN has better Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR), lower latency, and better energy use, as well as longer network lifespan compared to the conventional protocol like MTE.

title: Deterministic Adaptive Testing With Verification: Score Alignment, Convergence, and Efficiency
authors: Rusen Meylani
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Adaptive testing systems often prioritize statistical optimality at the cost of transparency, predictable test length, and operational simplicity. This study presents and evaluates a deterministic, verificationbased adaptive testing model designed to produce interpretable scores while substantially reducing testing time and item exposure. The model employs a fixed number of iterations, strictly ordered difficulty bands, and a multi-step verification procedure to ensure that routing decisions reflect confirmed performance rather than isolated responses. Adaptive scores are computed using a bounded geometric weighting scheme that guarantees rapid convergence and known score resolution. The adaptive framework is content-agnostic and does not rely on domain-specific calibration, making it applicable across subjects, disciplines, and educational levels wherever ordered item difficulty can be established. Theoretical analysis exhaustively enumerated all possible decision sequences and established explicit bounds on adaptive score behavior. Results demonstrated near-perfect correspondence between adaptive and fixed-form scores under idealized conditions, defining an upper limit for empirical agreement. Empirical evaluation with 235 undergraduate examinees showed strong alignment between adaptive and fixed-form scores, preservation of rank ordering, and small, structured score differences consistent with the verification-based design. The adaptive test reduced item exposure by more than sixty percent and testing time to approximately one third of the fixed-form administration. These findings demonstrate that deterministic adaptive testing provides a transparent, efficient, and broadly applicable alternative to probabilistic computer-adaptive testing in applied educational assessment contexts.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Framework for Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy
authors: Berakhah. F. Stanley, Mekala R, K. Sindhubala, Saumiya S, Jeen Retna Kumar R, Mohanakrishnan K
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: An adverse effect of diabetes that affects the eyes by harming the blood vessels in the retina is called Diabetic Retinopathy (DR). At first, it either has no symptoms or causes intermittent vision issues. As it worsens, it affects the two eyes and ultimately results in either partially or total blindness. mostly happens when blood sugar levels are too high to control. As a result, the risk of developing diabetes mellitus perpetually high, for those who already have the condition. The chance of perpetual sight loss can be prevented by initial identification. Hence, the important identification approach is needed. The present research examines a Deep Learning (DL) approach, namely the EfficientNet-B7 classification, which is a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model used for diabetic retinopathy detection at an early stage. The datasets that are used is Diagnosis of Diabetic Retinopathy Detection via Kaggle. The suggested method was attained the accuracy model of 99.39 %. This work's primary goal is to create a reliable system for automatically identifying DR.

title: Advanced Ensemble Learning for Lithium-Ion Battery State of Health in Smart Transportation Fleets
authors: Sai Madhav Yedupati, Nitin Nitin, Manisha T. Shah, Dyllon Dekok, Marc Cahay
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Looking at the growth of electric vehicles in the current situation, it is crucial to precisely estimate the State of Health of Lithium-ion batteries to ensure safety and longevity. Deployed in smart transportation fleets, the framework supports edge-side inference on onboard and depot gateways to allow for SOH-aware dispatching and charging management. Offline evaluations that imitate electric bus duty cycles and depot charging windows demonstrate strong accuracy under temperature and load changes. In this research, a sophisticated ensemble approach is developed using Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting Regression (GBR), and Support Vector Regression (SVR), in combination with polynomial feature expansion. A linear regression meta-model is used to aggregate predictions from the base models, reducing prediction error and demonstrating consistent performance across temperature and load variations, thereby improving reliability under real-world operating conditions.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning Models for S11 Prediction in Microstrip Patch Antennas
authors: Yüksel Tokur Bozkurt, Burak Doğan, Medine Begüm Güzeltaș, Ömer Faruk Açıkgöz
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: In this study, the relationship between antenna geometry and the S11 parameter is examined for an S-band microstrip antenna operating at 2.3 GHz, using a data-driven regression framework. The dataset is composed of 666 simulated samples, each defined by five geometric design variables. Initial observations indicate that patch length has a more pronounced influence on S11 compared to the other parameters. At the same time, Variance Inflation Factor values remain sufficiently low, suggesting that multicollinearity does not pose a significant issue. Despite this, the linear regression model achieves a relatively weak fit <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(R$</tex>-squared <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.274)$</tex>, pointing to the presence of nonlinear relationships that cannot be adequately captured by simple linear assumptions. To better represent this behavior, multiple machine learning models were implemented and systematically compared. The results show that the MLP model yields the lowest prediction error, while Bagging and Random Forest exhibit closely matched and competitive performance. Further statistical evaluation confirms that these three models form a distinct top-performing group with significant separation from the others. From a practical standpoint, Random Forest offers a balanced alternative, combining stable predictive performance with a level of interpretability that makes it particularly suitable for antenna design applications.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Approach to Intelligent Web Vulnerability Discovery and Risk Assessment
authors: Hari Aravind Kishore A, Harry Herald R, Lekshmi S Raveendran
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The fast growth of modern web applications has made them more vulnerable to complex and new types of vulnerabilities, which in turn has exposed the drawbacks of traditional scanners that perform static crawling and depend on signature-based detection. To go beyond the capacity of traditional scanning workflows, we introduce a learner-driven system that not only detects web vulnerabilities but also analyzes the risks brought by them. The core of our solution is a combination of the Open Web Application Security Project Zed Attack Proxy (OWASP ZAP) and a Deep Q-Network (DQN) crawler that dynamically identifies security-critical application paths, a Light Gradient-Boosting Machine (LightGBM) classifier that focuses on prioritizing risks from the point of view of exploitability, and a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based autoencoder that identifies anomalous Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) patterns. Results from experimentation indicate that our method is significantly better than a simple signature-based scanning method in terms of reducing false positives, improving prioritization accuracy and extending scan coverage. An analytical look into Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) shows how they can be used in creating adaptive payloads and finding zero-day vulnerabilities. The findings indicate that the use of deep learning (DL), ensemble learning, and reinforcement learning (RL) together leads to a major increase in the capability levels and effectiveness of contemporary web vulnerability assessment systems.

title: Cognitive Radio-based Adaptive Baseband Chip for Secure IoT Mesh
authors: Surya P, Aravind T, R. Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is expanding quickly, necessitating dependable, secure, and energy-efficient communication architectures that can handle dense mesh networks. Traditional IoT radios are not appropriate for dynamic and mission-critical applications because of their fixed-spectrum operation, heavy interference, and inadequate security. The Cognitive Radio-Based Adaptive Baseband Chip (CR-ABC), presented in this research, is intended to improve spectrum agility, security, and performance in mesh IoT systems. To guarantee safe and interference-aware data transfer, the suggested chip has inbuilt encryption, real-time modulation modification, and a lightweight spectrum-sensing engine. The chip can automatically switch frequency bands and adjust transmission parameters depending on current network circumstances, a hybrid decision-making approach that combines energy detection, machine learning-assisted categorization, and adaptive thresholding. A hardware-accelerated cryptographic unit using chaotic key generation safeguards mesh communication with little computational cost to guarantee end-to-end security. Additionally, the CR-ABC uses duty-cycled computation and low-power DSP blocks to increase battery life in limited IoT nodes. The use of Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) prototyping and IoT mesh testbeds for experimental validation shows significant gains in jamming attack resistance, packet delivery reliability, and spectrum usage. The findings verify that the suggested adaptive baseband architecture offers next-generation IoT mesh deployments a scalable, safe, and energy-efficient solution.

title: Renewable Solar Energy for Desalination: Numerical Investigation of Single Slope Solar Still
authors: N. Senthilkumar, M. Balamurugan, Feroz Shaik, B. Deepanraj
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: A 3D numerical investigation of a single-slope solar still (SSSS) desalination operating under Puducherry (India) climatic conditions was carried out using ANSYS Fluent to analyze coupled heat and mass transfer, internal flow behavior, and freshwater productivity. A three-phase Volume of Fluid (VOF) model with evaporation-condensation was employed, incorporating conjugate heat transfer through copper, glass, and wood components and realistic solar loading conditions. The internal flow was found to be dominated by buoyancy-driven natural convection, with low turbulent kinetic energy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.61 \times 10^{\wedge}-11-3.25 \times 10^{\wedge}-11\ \mathrm{m}^{\wedge} 2 / \mathrm{s}^{\wedge} 2$</tex>) and negligible dynamic pressure contribution, confirming low-Reynoldsnumber enclosure flow. Parametric analysis revealed that increasing basin water depth from 10 to 30 mm reduced daily productivity from 2.65 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.25\ \text{kg} / \mathrm{m}^{\wedge} 2$</tex>.day due to enhanced thermal inertia and suppressed interface evaporation, while an optimal depth of 20 mm provided uniform temperature fields and effective evaporation-condensation coupling. Wind speed showed a marginal yet consistent enhancement in yield, with productivity increasing from 2.83 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.89\ \text{kg} / \mathrm{m}^{\wedge} 2$</tex>.day as wind speed rise from 1 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5\ \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, driven by improved glass cooling and condensation potential. Evaporation driving potential peaked at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.4-2.6 {\times} {1 0}^{\wedge} {4\ P a}$</tex> near the water surface, confirming its dominant role in governing distillate output.

title: ClimateCV: The Potential of Computer Vision Applications in Climate Change Mitigation and Adaptation
authors: Nermeen Abou Baker, Uwe Handmann
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Monitoring climate change requires the analysis of vast Earth observation datasets, which exceed human capacity. Although computer vision (CV) offers transformative potential through automated image analysis, there is a critical gap between laboratory performance and operational reliability. Models struggle with domain shifts, lack uncertainty estimates, and lack workflow integration. This paper explores CV applications in key climate-related areas such as disaster assessment, agriculture and deforestation monitoring. We trace the evolution from convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to modern foundation models, and demonstrate how architectural innovations such as self-attention mechanisms, multimodal learning, and vision-language models (VLMs) enable environmental analysis at scales and speeds that were previously impractical for operational deployment. This is achieved through a systematic examination of operational systems and datasets. We propose five research directions that could maximise climate impact: real-time global monitoring systems, geospatial foundation models, multi-modal sensor fusion, uncertainty quantification for decision support, and addressing critical domain gaps.

title: Innovation in the Pyroligneous Liquor Extraction Process
authors: Marcelo Perotoni, Marcio Silva, Ricardo Gaspar
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This study investigates the extraction of pyroligneous liquor during biomass carbonization from the perspective of sustainable innovation and circular economy principles. The objective was to develop and evaluate a low-complexity methodology that optimizes operational parameters-such as temperature, residence time, and heating rate-to recover pyroligneous bio-oil while preserving charcoal quality. Traditional charcoal production is typically associated with significant atmospheric emissions due to the uncontrolled release of pyrolytic vapors. In this work, conventional “hot-tail” kilns were adapted with gas capture and vapor condensation systems, converting gaseous by-products into a valuable liquid co-product. The proposed system employs aluminum or stainless-steel ducting, water-cooled condensers, and passive flow driven by thermal gradients. Experimental results demonstrate effective recovery of phenolic-rich pyroligneous liquor without compromising charcoal quality. Chemical characterization by GC-MS confirmed the predominance of phenolic and methoxylated phenolic compounds derived from lignin degradation. From an economic perspective, the system showed a short estimated payback period of approximately 20 days under pilot-scale conditions. The results indicate that integrating simple gas recovery technologies into traditional kilns can simultaneously reduce emissions, generate value-added products, and support the transition toward more sustainable and circular charcoal production systems.

title: Automating Secure NAS User Provisioning and Management with n8n Workflows
authors: Ekanth Gowda E, Nidhi Prashant Patil, Shreya Ojha, Shabana Sultana
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Most organizations generate digital data at a very rapid rate, which creates a large amount of complexity for organizations that need to securely store, manage, and retrieve their data. The emergence of third-party cloud service providers has raised significant concerns about the security and privacy of an organization’s most valuable asset (i.e., its information) as well as highest cost component, in addition to concerns of vendor lock-in. Network Attached Storage (NAS) systems have been historically used for on-premises storage, which subsequently provides an organization with sovereignty over its data, but NAS systems only have the capability of performing user provisioning, permission management, and security monitoring functions through manual processes, which can create multiple operational inefficiencies and increase operational time, while exposing an organization to significant amount of risk and creating additional vulnerabilities. The purpose of this paper is to present the design, development, and implementation of an innovative framework that integrates an affordable self-hosted NAS with n8n workflow automation technology to enable the automation of the entire user provisioning process by providing an automated workflow that manages from the initial web-based request for access to securely creating user accounts with specific roles on a Linux server and automatically delivering credentials. The framework developed was compared with the manual methods traditionally used in organizations through an analysis of operational efficiencies, scalability, and security. The results of testing and reported failure modes confirm the elimination of human error when configuring a NAS system, near instant provisioning, and consistently applied granular security policies.

title: An Intelligent Food Authentication System using Multisensor Data and Machine Learning
authors: Akshaya A, Abirami R, N. Ananthi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Food adulteration is still a major global problem that poses serious risks to consumer safety, food quality, and public health. Frequently consumed products such as milk, honey, and edible oils are often contaminated with chemicals, water, sugar syrups, and artificial additives. Traditional laboratory-based detection techniques are slow, costly, and unsuitable for real-time field applications. The objective of this study is to design and develop a portable, low-cost multisensor-based food authentication system capable of detecting adulteration, identifying adulterant type, and estimating contamination levels using machine learning techniques. The proposed framework integrates physicochemical sensors with an ESP32 microcontroller and applies Support Vector Machine (SVM) for binary classification, Random Forest for multiclass adulterant identification, and Random Forest regression for adulteration percentage estimation. Experimental results demonstrate high binary classification accuracy of up to 100% for oil and honey and 96% for milk, multiclass accuracy above 81%, and low regression error,confirming the effectiveness and reliability of the system. A lightweight Flask-based web interface enables real-time analysis and automated reporting, making the system suitable for field-level deployment.

title: Optimal Placement and Coordinated Parameter Tuning of FACTS Devices for Enhanced Power System Performance
authors: E. Parimalasundar, P. Venkatesh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The development of load and interconnection power system networks makes existing power transmission systems heavy, and the operation of the power system tends to be more complicated, less reliable, and less stable as a whole. Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTS) devices are taking a dominant role in the efficient control of power flows and dynamic stability of power system networks. The steady-state models of the four common FACTS devices, which are the most simplified form in general, are taken into account and introduced in this thesis. They are the series thyristor-controlled series compensator (TCSC) and static VAR compensator (SVC), included in the power flow model for expected power flow. They can allow the transmission system to be more reliable and efficient, provide more flexibility and control in operation, and maintain the stability of a power system network. To explore the such FACTS devices mentioned above under normal operation with corresponding traditional models. They can be added to the conventional power flow model or inserted into the Line Flow-Based Power Flow Model (LFB). Two of the most widely used FACTS devices, TCSC and SVC, are deployed in this thesis work for an IEEE 5-bus system and IEEE 30-bus system. The multiple FACTS devices are realised by exchanging the respective variables. The performances of the obtained results are successively compared.

title: CloudImageHunt: Large-Scale Detection and Analysis of Malicious Container Images
authors: Rahul Karne, Akshitha Segireddy, Akhil Joshi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Software dependencies in DevOps pipelines have become so common that they can be considered an industry standard. Container registries make it easy to quickly utilize pre-built code components as part of your application build process and workflow. Unfortunately, by relying on third-party container registries for the source code you’re building with, you’ve also expanded your "software supply chain" attack vector. Threat actors take advantage of this attack vector by creating and uploading harmful container images that contain malicious items like; credential thieves, cryptocurrency miners, DDoS bots, etc... Current security controls are designed to protect against specific vulnerabilities and/or misconfigurations within an organization’s environment, and therefore do little to help protect against malware embedded into container images and/or behavior based execution of those images. To address this, we introduce CloudImageHunt, a modular pipeline and corresponding dataset, to support the large-scale discovery and characterization of malicious container images hosted in publicly available container registries. The CloudImageHunt pipeline collects data from multiple sources, removes duplicates via the container image’s SHA-256 checksum, and applies evidence-based labels to each image by collecting both static information and dynamic information. We will release our dataset containing labeled images with confidence levels and a dataset card, as well as a threat actor technique taxonomy and benchmarking tools. On a separate evaluation set withheld from training, the combination of static and dynamic pipelines showed better detection accuracy than baseline models using either only static signatures or only vulnerabilities. Longitudinal views of the dataset enable us to analyze the prevalence of malicious images, the duration of their persistence in registries, and the frequency with which previously identified malicious images are reuploaded to registries.

title: Concept of Cyber Threat Assessment of Critical Infrastructure Facilities
authors: Oleksandr Novoseletskyi, Olena Voloshchuk, Dmytro Yevseiev, Andrii Cherniavskyi, Oleh Tovma, Daniil Viukhin
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper proposes a unified conceptual framework for cyber threat assessment in socio-cyber-physical systems (SCPS). The framework integrates a hybrid and synergistic threat classifier with an objective evaluation of attacker capabilities, including financial, computational, and energy resources. By systematically addressing the complexities of targeted attacks and social engineering vectors, the proposed methodology bridges the gap between conceptual modeling and practical vulnerability assessment in critical infrastructure facilities. The study introduces a quantitative metric for information secrecy, treating information as an economic asset to evaluate potential destructive impacts on business continuity. Consequently, this approach not only quantifies potential information losses but also identifies the optimal configuration of security system elements required to guarantee regulatory compliance and a predefined security level. Ultimately, the contribution highlights a coherent strategy for dynamically assessing the current security state of critical infrastructure in conjunction with predictive threat analysis.

title: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Algorithms for AQI Prediction in Key Indian Cities
authors: Subhashree Mohapatra, Amit Kumar Keshari, Jai Govind Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of industries and technology, air pollution is also progressing in a rapid rate. It has now become a major issue that requires immediate attention and action. In this direction, this study is carried out to predict the air quality index (AQI) for four major cities in India, Delhi, Gurugram, Hyderabad, and Kolkata. Here, three machine learning (ML) models are trained and tested, namely Linear Regression (LR), Support Vector Regression (SVR), and Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM). The data is retrieved from the Kaggle repository, from which 12 air pollutant values along with the AQI are used for experimental purposes. Among the three ML models, SVR generates the best result for three cities, Delhi, Hyderabad and Kolkata, whereas LSTM performs best for Gurugram. The best prediction result is obtained by SVR, 13.22 of MAE, 18.48 of RMSE and 0.97 R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> for Kolkata. The outcome of the models can be improved more by collecting some real time air quality data, using optimization techniques for parameter selections and also using various other advanced models.

title: An Optimized Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Early Detection of Skin Cancer
authors: Abhilash Pati
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Skin cancer is still one of the most common types of cancer in the world. Environmental changes, lifestyle choices, and extended exposure to ultraviolet (UV) radiation are all contributing factors to the rising number of cases. Squamous cell carcinoma, basal cell carcinoma, and malignant melanoma were the three main types of skin cancer that were the focus of this study. What causes it, what increases the likelihood of it happening, current medical treatments, and preventative measures are all covered. To improve diagnostics, a hybrid machine learning (ML) architecture was developed. There are six methods for classifying data: recursive feature elimination (RFE), a recursive feature selection method, and the Firefly Optimization Algorithm (FOA) for optimization. The suggested optimized ML-based hybrid model achieved a 98.04% accuracy rate. The receiver operating characteristic (ROC) study, which discovered an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.974, added credence to the model. These results show how combining feature selection, optimization, and classification techniques can increase the accuracy of skin cancer diagnosis. This study shows that by improving diagnosis speed and accuracy, ML-based methods can lower mortality and morbidity rates. It also demonstrates how crucial early detection of skin cancer is to understanding its mechanism.

title: Fetal Health Detection using CatBoost with Feature Selection techniques
authors: Rajashree Dash, Aditi Tiwary, Prakriti Krishna, Mansi Baranwal
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The crucial stage of pregnancy emphasizes how important it is to care for a new life. The well-being of a fetus is strongly connected with maternal health, so it necessitates through examination of its different stages to mitigate complications. Cardiotocography (CTG) serves as a critical tool for observing and predicting fetal health. This study proposes a predictive framework utilizing the CatBoost algorithm for fetal health classification. Three Feature Selection (FS) techniques such as Analysis of Variance (ANOVA), Mutual Information (MI), and Correlation Coefficient (CC) are applied with CatBoost to extract the most significant features from the CTG data, and to improve its predictive performance. A comparative analysis performed on the UCI CTG dataset clearly demonstrates the impact of FS on the model performance in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The results highlight the potential of the proposed approach in providing a reliable and efficient solution for fetal health monitoring and risk prediction.

title: Random ReLU–Based Support Vector Machine Classifier for Frequency-Domain Data
authors: Perera A W B M, Fernando W A M, Edirisinghe P M, Premasiri M H S
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Effective classification of small-scale frequency domain datasets relies on computationally expensive machine learning methods, which are not ideal. This paper proposes a random Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU)-based feature expansion that augments the original input, enabling an efficient One-vs-Rest (OvR) linear Support Vector Machine (SVM) to model nonlinear decision boundaries to improve classification accuracy. The method projects standardised inputs onto Gaussian random directions, applies a ReLU nonlinearity to introduce sparsity and representational capacity, and concatenates the resulting features with the original input before training the linear SVM model. The proposed method was evaluated on two benchmark datasets, including the United States Postal Service (USPS) dataset and the Free-Speech Digit Dataset (FSDD), across ten random seeds to ensure robustness. Experiments demonstrated that our method outperformed the linear SVM in test accuracy and the Random Forest classifier across all evaluation metrics, achieving mean test accuracies of 0.9716 on USPS and 0.9704 on FSDD, both confirmed as statistically significant improvements via the Wilcoxon signed-rank test. While the method yields slightly lower accuracy than the SVM with a Radial Basis Function kernel, it maintains comparable training time due to the OvR strategy, and the tunable feature dimension provides a flexible accuracy efficiency trade-off adaptable to different resource budgets.

title: Strategies for Light Management in Thin and Flexible c-Si Solar Cells Utilizing Copper Oxide (CuO) Nanoparticles
authors: A. Sandeep Kumar Reddy, Arijit Bardhan Roy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Researchers are very interested in flexible and thin crystalline silicon (c-Si) solar cells because they are lightweight, flexible, and could be used in wearable and portable devices. Nevertheless, their decreased thickness restricts optical absorption, resulting in lower efficiency. In order to overcome this difficulty, sophisticated light management techniques are necessary. In order to improve light absorption and trapping in thin c-Si solar cells, this work investigates the usage of copper oxide (CuO) nanoparticles scattering and plasmonic like behaviour help to increase optical path lengths within the active layer, their significant absorption coefficient and tuneable bandgap enable better photon harvesting over a wide spectrum range. The suggested structure reduces the amount by over 15%. CuO nanoparticles can maximize light coupling, reduce reflection losses, and increase photocurrent production as shown by the study’s design strategies, material integration methods, and optical simulations. The suggested tactics show a possible technique to achieve sophisticated nanostructured light management schemes in c-Si solar cells that are mechanically flexible, lightweight, and efficient.

title: Novel Control Strategy for High Efficiency Power Conversion in Electric Vehicle Drive Systems
authors: Karanam Deepak, G Pandu Ranga Reddy, Mandla Thanusha, Md Meeran, Perugu Venkatesh, Tappeta Abhilash
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: With increasing electrical vehicles being integrated into power systems, power electronic interfaces are being subjected to increasing demands and requirements in terms of efficiency, dynamic response, and reliability. Power converter systems interfacing electrical energy storage systems and electric propulsion are major factors in determining the overall efficiency and reliability of electrical vehicles. This paper aims to present an effective and efficient power conversion framework implemented using intelligent embedded control techniques and strategies, focusing on electric vehicle drive systems and their potential to perform simulations to assess their functionality. The structured framework includes the integration of intelligent and intelligent power converter systems to improve DC-to-DC conversion and motor drive using inverter techniques. Parameters such as power losses, current ripples, and stability are achieved using a detailed explanation of the system configuration, control techniques, and power conversion principles. Extensive validation of the proposed methodology is provided in MATLAB/Simulink for a broad range of operating scenarios, from load and reference variations. In fact, simulation results clearly point out considerable improvements in conversion efficiency, smooth current profiles, and faster dynamic response compared to conventional control methodologies. Thanks to its modular structure and feasibility of implementation, the presented framework represents a reliable and scalable solution for advanced electric vehicle power train systems.

title: A Federated Digital Twin Framework for Predictive ICU Ventilator Monitoring using IoT Data
authors: Theeraja A, Swetha M, C Gnana Kousalya, A S Loganathan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: ICU ventilators play an important role in assisting patient for breathing. Even small problems in ventilators, like sudden pressure changes, air leaks, tube blockages, or incorrect settings, can put a patient life in danger. But in an ICU, it is very hard for nurses and doctors to watch every ventilator at all times for its proper functioning. That is where our project enters the picture. We are developing an intelligent, predictive monitoring platform based on IoT, Digital Twin and Federated Learning to monitor continuously the ventilator performance. The idea is straightforward: early detection of problems, minimization of risks, and the need to assist teams in healthcare at the time when they need it the most. In our system, IoT sensors continuously collect real-time data such as airflow, pressure, oxygen levels, and patient breathing patterns. A digital twin, which is a virtual copy of the ventilator and the patient’s lung behaviour, uses this data to show how the system is working. It simulates normal conditions and identifies abnormal patterns that may indicate danger. Each hospital has its own local machine learning model that learns from its ventilator data. Instead of sending patient data outside the hospital, which can be unsafe, we use Federated Learning. This method sends only the model’s learning (model updates) to a central server, not the raw patient data. The global model combines model updates from many hospitals and becomes more accurate and smart in predicting future failures.

title: Energy Efficiency Improvement by Compressor Waste-Heat Recovery: an Industry Case Study
authors: Nuno Soares Domingues, Daiva Stanelytė
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This study presents an investment-grade measurement and verification (M&V) assessment of a compressor waste-heat recovery (WHR) retrofit at a Portuguese food-processing facility. Permanently installed, calibrated sensors recorded one-minute electrical and thermal data over a 12 -week production period. A multiple linear regression baseline model, using production throughput and ambient wet-bulb temperature as predictors, was developed in accordance with IPMVP Option B and ISO 50001/50006. The verified mean recovered thermal capacity was 165 kW (design: 170 kW), yielding annualized thermal savings of 1.26 ± 0.05 GWh (95% confidence interval), equivalent to a 7.8% reduction in process heat demand. Associated benefits include avoided emissions of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$307 \mathrm{t} \text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e} \cdot \text{yr}^{-1}$</tex> and fuel cost savings of € 54.6 k … y r<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>, corresponding to a simple payback of 1.7 years. Combined metering and modeling uncertainty was 3.6%, satisfying investment-grade requirements. These results confirm that compressor WHR, supported by high-resolution metering and rigorous analytics, provides a cost-effective decarbonization pathway for the agri-food sector. Complete datasets, calibration records, and the uncertainty analysis are provided to ensure transparency and reproducibility.
