title: Enhanced Radar Deceptive Jamming Based on Time-Domain Modulated Metasurface
authors: Heng Xu, Hong Xu, Haosheng Fu, Qin Pan, Xiaoyang Zhou, Hui Chen, Yinghui Quan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Traditional digital radio frequency memory (DRFM)-based jammers retransmit high-power copies of radar waveforms, which may serve as radiating beacons. These emissions are subject to detection and localization by radar. Additionally, the inherent processing delay also causes the deceptive jamming to be temporally separated from the real target. In contrast, time-domain modulated metasurfaces (TMMs) apply zero-latency modulation to the incident return without additional radio-frequency (RF) transmission, embedding the jamming response directly into the reflected waveform. This paper formulates TMM deceptive jamming as a matched-filter output shaping problem in which the returned energy is concentrated at a prescribed false-target delay bin while redundant peaks are suppressed. A constant-modulus fractional p-norm model is established, and a majorization minimization algorithm with closed-form updates is derived for efficient sequence design. Simulations and X-band 1-bit TMM measurements show that linear frequency modulated (LFM) and phase-coded modulation (PCM) waveforms exhibit clearly different quantization behaviors. LFM benefits substantially from 2-bit phase control, whereas PCM already approaches saturated performance with 1-bit modulation. Anechoic chamber measurements are consistent with the main simulated waveform-dependent trends

title: Data-Driven Repetitive Control via Noisy Data Learning: Experimental Validation on Robotic Manipulator
authors: Yijia Li, Xiongxiong He, Xianhua Ou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of periodic disturbances in discrete-time nonlinear systems by proposing a direct data-driven repetitive control strategy. This framework treats the periodic disturbance term as an augmented state of the nonlinear system. A Fourier-series-based semi-definite programming (SDP) approach is developed to approximate and compensate for the periodic disturbance through disturbance rejection techniques. The proposed scheme enables learning from contaminated data while providing an estimation of the robust positive invariant (RPI) set. Numerical simulations and robotic manipulator experiments validate the effectiveness of the method in mitigating periodic interference, demonstrating its potential for practical applications in control systems engineering.

title: Measurement as Modeling: The Bayesian Bridge between Artificial Intelligence and Metrological Traceability
authors: Ignacio Lira
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The rapid integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into instrumentation has created a paradox: while AI-driven “soft sensors” offer unprecedented predictive power, they frequently fail to meet the fundamental requirements of metrological traceability. Measurement is not merely data acquisition; it is a model-based process that constructs a bridge between empirical reality and information. Current AI methods—often functioning as “black boxes”—emphasize scalability at the expense of metrological rigor. They typically lack the explicit structural models needed to validate this bridge, which risks non-compliance with the Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement (GUM). This paper proposes that the solution lies not in forcing AI into the linear constraints of classical propagation, but in fully embracing the Bayesian approach of GUM Supplement 1. We argue that the training phase of a machine-learning algorithm is metrologically equivalent to a massive Type B evaluation and must be treated as such across the model lifecycle—including data curation, training, validation, deployment, and re-calibration—so that uncertainty statements remain current and defensible. By addressing the Errors-in-Variables imperative and reinterpreting neural-network weights and true inputs as random variables with probability density functions, and by using practical Bayesian surrogates (for example, Bayesian neural networks, ensembles and Monte Carlo dropout, or diffusion-based input posteriors), we establish a framework that disentangles aleatoric from epistemic uncertainties. In doing so, we restore the epistemic link essential for traceability in the digital age.

title: Toward Efficient Edge AI With Heterogeneous Computing and Multilevel Optimization
authors: Jingyuan Li, Xinyu Cai, Yuan Dai, Wenbo Yin, Lingli Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The rapid progress of artificial intelligence (AI) has brought increasing demands on hardware accelerators, particularly as modern models combine dense linear operations with a growing number of irregular, nonlinear, and control-intensive operators. While tensor cores and systolic arrays offer high throughput for regular computations, they often struggle to efficiently support the diverse operations emerging in recent model structures. Coarse-grained reconfigurable arrays (CGRAs), with their spatial parallelism and reconfigurability, may serve as a natural complement to dense accelerators in such heterogeneous workloads. In this work, we propose EUREKA, a heterogeneous acceleration framework that integrates tensor cores with CGRAs through a unified instruction set, cross-architecture data scheduling, tailored hardware support for nonlinear operators, and optimizations at the instruction, task, and operator levels to exploit parallelism. At the software level, we introduce a hierarchical compilation strategy that combines graph-level optimizations with tensor-level scheduling techniques. To address the large design space of hardware–software co-optimization, we further develop a Bayesian optimization-based exploration scheme enhanced with kernel compression methods, which provides an efficient means of identifying promising hardware configurations and scheduling strategies. Experiment results on representative AI benchmarks show that EUREKA improves execution efficiency, achieving an average <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$12.6\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> normalized performance gain over state-of-the-art frameworks.

title: Optimal Siting and Operation of Hydrogen Stations in Distribution Networks: Voltage Support, Seasonal Storage, and Market-Driven Scheduling
authors: Christina V. Grammenou, Aggelos S. Bouhouras
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The need to reduce carbon emissions, combined with the increasing and often uncontrollable penetration of Renewable Energy Sources (RES) in distribution networks (DNs), introduces significant operational challenges, including voltage deviations and energy curtailment. This highlights the importance of developing and deploying advanced energy storage solutions. Hydrogen Stations (HSs) represent a promising option for storing surplus energy generated by RES. In this paper, a comprehensive framework for the optimal siting and operation of HSs is proposed, aiming to enhance network performance while ensuring economic viability. Each HS consists of an electrolyzer, a hydrogen storage tank, and a fuel cel l (FC), enabling both energy storage and ancillary service provision. Given that many European countries have not yet established the necessary infrastructure for hydrogen transport and trading, hydrogen is utilized exclusively for ancillary services and seasonal storage. Specifically, dual objective mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) optimization models are developed, to address technical and economic objectives, over a one-year planning horizon. The first model focuses on minimizing voltage deviations across network buses, through the optimal siting and operation of HSs, while the second model aims to minimize the total operating cost (OC) under different electricity market scenarios. The methodology is applied to a 33-node Medium Voltage (MV) DN using real load, RES generation, and market price data. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves a significant reduction in voltage fluctuations (up to 50.48% compared to the baseline case), effectively mitigating both overvoltage and undervoltage conditions. Furthermore, the economic analysis reveals that the viability of HS investments strongly depends on the pricing structure of ancillary services, with favorable scenarios leading to annual revenues exceeding €1 million and payback periods below ten years. The proposed framework demonstrates the capability of HSs to support grid operation and facilitate their integration into future ancillary service markets, thus providing a promising solution for DNs with high-RES penetration.

title: Safety Guaranteed Control Synthesis With Multiple Backup Policies and a Varying Reachable Time Horizon_supp1-3699159.mp4
authors: H. Jin Kim
publish: N/A
abstract: For safety-critical control tasks, control barrier functions (CBFs) are powerful tools for designing safety filters that ensure safety of the systems. However, obtaining a valid CBF that defines a sufficiently large safe set usually requires extensive handcrafting due to input constraints, which should be considered for most systems, especially robotic applications. To address this challenge, this letter proposes a general framework for constructing a safety filter that employs multiple backup policies. We generalize the existing backup CBF (BCBF) approach, where the proposed quadratic program (QP) renders the corresponding safe set forward invariant. The proposed method enforces CBF-like derivative constraints only at critical instants along the backup trajectory. Also, we introduce a varying reachable time to the backup set, instead of a fixed time horizon. These modifications yield a less conservative and computationally efficient safety filter while maintaining formal safety guarantees through nonsmooth analysis. Furthermore, the proposed framework is easily extended to the use of multiple backup strategies, which can effectively enlarge the resulting safe set. Real-world collision avoidance experiments validate the scalability of the overall framework and confirm its reduced conservativeness.

title: Three-Dimensional Curved Thin-Wire FDTD Method for Simulating Towed Antennas in Seawater Half-Space
authors: Chenpeng Dai, Mingyue Yang, Gaoqi Dou, Jiayu Zhang, Shiyu Wang, Kuisong Zheng, Tianhui Fu, Shimin Feng, Gao Wei
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this letter, the three-dimensional (3-D) curved thin-wire finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) method is proposed and discussed in detail. The method improves the computational stability via wire grouping, and the correctness of the proposed method is verified by comparing with FEKO software and SI-FDTD algorithm. Moreover, the towed antennas in seawater half-space are simulated using the proposed method. The results demonstrate that the shape of the towed antenna affects the underwater energy distribution in the near region, while it has little impact at longer distances.

title: Fairness-Aware Machine Learning Framework for Bias Mitigation in Large-Scale Data Analytics
authors: Shreekant Salotagi, Tanvir H Sardar, Debrupa Pal, Madhumala R B, Shreyas Rajendra Hole, Sridhar S K
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The paper presents a fairness-conscious machine learning system as a systematic process of identifying, estimating, and addressing bias in data analytics systems on large data sets. The three strategies are proposed to be combined into a single framework, fairness-regularised objective function, which penalises disparate impact in model training, adversarial debiasing module, which learns to decorrelate predictions by protected attributes, and adaptive instance reweighting mechanism, which dynamically reassigns importance to samples according to subgroup-specific error rates. Our framework, as evidenced by extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets that include income prediction, recidivism assessment, credit scoring, facial attribute classification, and clinical outcome prediction, decreases the statistical parity difference between our framework and unconstrained baselines by 78.4 per cent and decreases the equalised odds difference by 80.5 per cent whilst maintaining 99.4 per cent of the original predictive accuracy. Moreover, we include a scaling analysis that proves sub-linear growth in the number of computational overheads with the increased datasets of over five million records. The framework offers practitioners interpretable fairness diagnostics and adjustable trade-off parameters, creating a viable basis of responsible AI use in enterprise data analytics systems.

title: Hierarchical Hybrid Transformer for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Bora Başaran, Deniz Polat, Gökçe Nur Yılmaz, Ali Berkol
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This study proposes five lightweight image SuperResolution (SR) architectures, Restormer, SwinIR, HINet, and two hybrid ensemble models (Hybrid-1 and Hybrid-2), to restore heavily compressed JPEG images. The proposed Hybrid-2 model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the LIVE1 dataset with only 41,347 parameters, attaining Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) values of 37.68 dB and 0.9921, outperforming existing approaches including Swin2SR, ART, and ART+. These results demonstrate that strategically combining complementary lightweight architectures can surpass models with millions of parameters on highly degraded images, enabling practical deployment on mobile, embedded, and Internet of Things (IoT) platforms.

title: Wear Studies on Silicon Nitride Reinforced Magnesium Composites Adopting Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: C.K. Dhinakarraj, K. Gajalakshmi, Vivekanandhan Mohan, N. Senthilkumar
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Dry sliding wear of an AZ31 magnesium composite reinforced with 4 wt.% <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Si}_{3} ~\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex> was evaluated using a Taguchi L9 design by varying axial load (AL), rotation speed (RS) and time duration (TD). <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AZ}_{31}+\text{Si}_{3} ~\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex> composite was fabricated via Disintegrated Melt Deposition procedure under inert condition. Wear loss (WL) increased markedly with severity, indicating load-controlled ploughing and subsurface deformation. Conversely, the coefficient of friction (CoF) decreased with increasing AL and RS, consistent with mechanically mixed/tribo-oxide layer formation and interfacial smoothing, while friction force (FF) followed normal load. Grey relational analysis (GRA) converted outputs into a single metric; grey relational grades ranged from 0.536-0.774 (before fuzzy) and 0.586-0.751 (after fuzzy). A Mamdani type-1 fuzzy inference system further stabilized multi-response ranking. ANOVA of GRG showed a shift in dominance after fuzzy integration, with RS contribution increasing from 15.24% to 42.87%, and error reducing from 15.67% to 9.78%, demonstrating improved robustness for multi-objective wear optimization. This wear resilient composite can be used in automotive and aviation industries as a light weight durable material.

title: Attention-Driven Underwater Image Enhancement Framework via Edge-Aware Feature Refinement
authors: Debapriya Das Gupta, Arka Bairagi, Sobhan Kanti Dhara
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Underwater images suffer from unique challenges during restoration, due to absorption and scattering of light. It results in significant loss of fine details, color cast, blurriness, and irregular haze. Existing methods struggle to capture the fine details in complex underwater scenarios. To tackle these difficulties, we propose a novel Edge-guided Feature Refinement (EFR) module using channel attention and integrate this in a vision transformer based architecture. It is precisely designed to recover the blurred details. We also used a window based multi head self attention driven encoder-decoder architecture to enhance both local and global details in underwater images and also reduce the computation complexity. For validation of our approach, we performed evaluations on various paired and unpaired datasets using popular underwater image restoration metrics. By introducing the EFR module, our methodology successfully achieves the performance of the state-of-the-art techniques in various metrics.

title: Flood Prediction Framework Using an Ensemble Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Douglas Kilyobas Titus, Benson Yusuf Baha, Daniel Godwin
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Floods constitute one of the most devastating natural calamities in the world, which result in massive loss of life, destruction of infrastructure and gross economic impact. The states of Adamawa, Borno and Gombe are especially prone to regular flooding in Nigeria, but there are few reliable and data driven prediction systems in the area. The paper designed and tested an ensemble machine learning model that predicts floods based on hydrometeorological variables collected by the Nigerian Bureau of Statistics and the satellite based Power API of NASA, which runs between 2019 and 2025. The dataset consisted of 4,383 records containing 13 original climate and environmental variables, which had been increased to 86 features with the help of temporal, lag, interaction, and polynomial feature engineering. The data gaps were filled with mean imputation and Box-Cox transformation was used to normalize very skewed data like precipitation, solar radiation, and wind speed. Five machine learning classifiers such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, Artificial Neural Network (ANN), XGBoost, and LightGBM were trained as baseline machine learning classifiers. These were also compared with four ensemble strategies including hard voting, soft voting, bagging and stacking. Randomized search was used as the hyperparameter optimization tool and 5fold cross validation was used to evaluate the model performance on various metrics such as Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1score, AUC-ROC, and RMSE. It was found that overall accuracy was above 85 % and that the flood event detection (recall) was low in all classifiers, a result of a large class imbalance; flood occurrences only made up around 12.5 % of the data (549 flood days and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 8 3 4}$</tex> non-flood days). LightGBM showed the superior overall performance with a cross validation F1-score of 0.1131 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathbf{0. 0 2 8 1}$</tex>, closely followed by XGBoost with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 9 8 5} \pm \mathbf{0. 0 2 8 4}$</tex>. The results confirm the feasibility of satellite derived climate information to operational flood forecasting and the necessity to combat class imbalance with methods like Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) or cost sensitive learning.

title: Secure and Intelligent Indoor Navigation System with Real Time Re Routing
authors: Kanagamalliga S, Srigitha S Nath, Lokesh M B, Sathiya Priyan R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Indoor navigation has been increasingly required in complex buildings such as hospitals, malls, airports, and academic campuses where GPS signals remain unreliable. In this work, an advanced indoor navigation architecture was designed to provide accurate localization and efficient route guidance under dynamic indoor conditions. Multi modal user interaction was supported through touch, voice, QR, NFC, and beacon-based inputs to improve accessibility and usability. Localization was enhanced using sensor fusion methods by combining Wi Fi RTT, BLE, UWB, IMU, and camera information, while drift was reduced through dead reckoning correction. A semantic map and digital twin representation were utilized to store multi floor 3D information, points of interest metadata, and accessibility constraints. Graph construction was performed using weighted multi criteria modelling to represent distance, time, congestion, and user needs, and hierarchical graphs were employed for scalable navigation across large infrastructures. Hybrid path planning strategies were applied using Dijkstra and A star variants with landmark based acceleration, while rerouting was enabled when context changes occurred. Learning based intelligence was incorporated to model user behaviour and predict traffic flow for improved decision making. Security, privacy, and fault tolerance requirements were also addressed to ensure reliable deployment.

title: A Hybrid CNN-EfficientNet Model for Enhanced and Early Skin Cancer Diagnosis
authors: Meenakshi L, Lokesh J, Meganaathan G, Meher Harshith Guptha V
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Skin cancer has become a significant health issue in the world, with its number of cases increasing and its clinical outcomes becoming serious in event of late diagnosis. Knowledge assessment of skin lesions is not always reliable because there is a great difference in the nature of the lesions and the lack of skilled dermatological workers. The recent advances in deep learning allowed the automated interpretation of dermoscopic images, which can be useful in the early screening of skin cancer. The suggested hybrid deep learning architecture assumes a sequence of learning features based on the use of a convolutional neural network and subsequently the application of the EfficientNet architecture, which is applicable to find discriminative visual features to early and accurate detect skin cancer in dermoscopic lesions. The model has been experimentally tested on publicly available datasets of skin lesions and shown to converge and offer steady classification accuracy between benign and malignant lesions. Moreover, the trained model is deployed as a web-based application which gives real time diagnostics prediction and a confidence score. The results have led to a conclusion that hybrid CNN EfficientNet model is an effective and attractive tool which can be used in helping to screen skin cancer in the clinical environment early.

title: Artificial Immune Systems Approach for Attractor Reconstruction of Chaotic Maps under Periodicity and Quasiperiodicity Regimes
authors: Akemi Gálvez, Suash Deb, Andrés Iglesias
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Recovering the shape and structure of an attractor from observations of a system state is a fundamental problem in dynamical systems. In this paper, we address this problem for discrete chaotic systems that can be well approximated by cubic polynomial functions. This task is formulated as a nonlinear leastsquares optimization problem, which is difficult to solve using classical mathematical techniques. To tackle this issue, we apply artificial immune systems, a popular evolutionary approach for continuous optimization. The method is applied to the reconstruction of periodic and quasiperiodic orbits from three 2D chaotic systems: the Hénon, Duffing, and Burger maps. Computational experiments show that the approach successfully recovers both periodic and quasiperiodic orbits with good numerical accuracy. We also discuss the main limitations of the method and outline directions for future work.

title: Benchmarking AI-Generated Text Detection Tools in Academic Writing: An Experimental Evaluation
authors: Mohammed H. Alaqad, Ghadeir Alkindi, Mohamad Hussin, Ahmad Arifin Sapar, Lama Anwar Alaggad
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper presents an experimental evaluation of five widely-used AI-generated text detection tools GPTZero, Turnitin AI Detection, Copyleaks, ZeroGPT, and Originality.ai assessed against a purpose-built corpus of 180 academic writing samples comprising human-written, AI-generated, and hybrid texts. Using metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and false positive rate (FPR), findings reveal significant performance variability across tools, with Originality.ai achieving the highest overall accuracy (84.4%) and ZeroGPT the lowest (69.4%). Critically, all tools demonstrated substantially reduced accuracy on hybrid texts (54.2%–70.8%), underscoring the limitations of current detection mechanisms. False positive rates ranging from 15.6% to 30.6% raise substantial concerns for equitable academic integrity enforcement. A chi-square analysis confirmed statistically significant performance differences across tools <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\chi^{2}=24.17, \text{df}=4, \mathrm{p}<0.001)$</tex>. These findings carry important implications for institutional policy on AI use in academic writing.

title:
RTL Design and Implementation of the I
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    C Communication Protocol
authors: Akella Madhav, Jayakrishnan K R, Ramesh Bhaktavatchalu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This project aims to develop and verify the Single Data Rate (SDR) mode communication core of the I3C (Improved Inter-Integrated Circuit) protocol using a Verilog HDL environment. I3C is a critical heterogeneous bus standard, offering superior speed and functional density compared to the legacy I2C protocol. The design focuses on modeling the Master controller’s functional logic, specifically to handle the complex Dynamic Address Assignment (DAA) procedure. This involved rigorous implementation and verification of the bus’s Arbitration logic to guarantee robust resolution of Process Identifier PID conflicts. A innovation within DAA is the deployment of a constrained pseudo-random address selection mechanism, ensuring the Master assigns unique Dynamic Addresses (DAs) efficiently while maintaining protocol adherence and avoiding address space collisions. The core communication sequences are successfully tested and verified against different input sets. This includes validating fundamental Broadcasting commands, the complete DAA state machine, and directed Private Read and Write transactions utilizing the newly assigned DAs and support for In-Band Interrupts. The results confirm the robust design and correct functional operation of the SDR core, establishing a stable foundation for further development of High Data Rate (HDR) modes.

title: EEG-Based Epilepsy Diagnosis During Sleep using Support Vector Machine Model
authors: Dhana Lakshmi B, Murugan S, Muthazhagan B, Jayanthi G, Vani R, Elankurisil Arasan
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Epilepsy is a prevalent neurological condition marked by recurring seizures, necessitating prompt and precise diagnosis. Electroencephalography (EEG) is the principal instrument for observing cerebral activity and identifying epileptic occurrences, especially during sleep when atypical patterns are more evident. This research presents an EEGbased approach for diagnosing epilepsy during sleep using a Support Vector Machine (SVM) model. The Siena Sleep EEG dataset comprises 944,640 samples from 20 EEG channels, accompanied by binary labels denoting epileptic and nonepileptic patients. EEG data underwent preprocessing, and pertinent features were identified to encapsulate temporal and spectral attributes. The SVM model was trained and assessed use an 80:20 training-testing division. The method attained an overall accuracy of 99.35 % in differentiating between epileptic and non-epileptic patients. The study of the confusion matrix revealed little misclassification, underscoring the model's trustworthiness. The findings illustrate the capability of SVMbased models in automating epilepsy detection during sleep, providing a quick and efficient instrument for enhancing clinical diagnosis and patient care.

title: Kidney Stone Prediction Using Logistic Regression on Urine Analysis Data
authors: M Abirami, G Bhuvaneswari, Bandaru Mounika, Sandra Johnson, S Srinivasan, Arivazhagan H
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Kidney stones are a common urological condition that may result in intense pain, urinary tract blockage, and further health issues if not identified promptly. Anticipating kidney stone development might provide prompt diagnosis and preventative measures, alleviating patient suffering and minimizing healthcare strain. This research examines the use of logistic regression to forecast the occurrence of kidney stones using urine analytical data. The dataset comprises 79 urine samples, each assessed for six critical biochemical and physical parameters: specific gravity, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, osmolarity, conductivity, urea concentration, and calcium concentration. The logistic regression model was developed to identify individuals as either having kidney stones or not, depending on these characteristics. The model had an accuracy of 96.2%, indicating robust predictive potential and dependability in differentiating between individuals with and without kidney stones. The data suggests that regular urine biochemical indicators might successfully serve as a non-invasive prediction technique for kidney stone identification. This approach may aid healthcare practitioners in early diagnosis, prompt management, and tailored preventative interventions, therefore enhancing patient outcomes and reducing the risk of problems related to kidney stone development.

title: Data-Driven Decision Framework: Electric Motorcycle Archetype Clustering for Retrofit-as-a-Service
authors: Amir Alqedra, Siti Fauziah Toha, Muhammad Abdullah, Ahmad Syahrin Idris, Rosdiazli Ibrahim, Salmiah Ahmad
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Retrofitting internal combustion engine (ICE) motorcycles into electric motorcycles can accelerate electrification in motorcycle-dominant regions, but retrofit planning is often manual and inconsistent. This paper proposes a Retrofit-as-a-Service (RaaS) decision framework that (i) clusters motorcycles into retrofit archetypes using specifications reflecting packaging constraints and performance demand, and (ii) maps each archetype to interpretable EV component tiers (motor, battery energy, voltage class, and controller tier) using transparent, physics-informed constraints under bounded uncertainty. A dataset of motorcycle specifications (model years <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2021-2025; \leqslant 900 \text{cc}$</tex>) is processed and clustered using K-means, with the number of archetypes selected via an internal validity sweep (best silhouette <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.370$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{K}=5$</tex>). The resulting archetypes are profiled and illustrated using centroid-nearest representative motorcycles. The recommendation module then produces archetype-level tier sets and conservative feasibility indicators, including lower-bound range relative to the minimum required range. The proposed approach supports scalable, repeatable decision support for motorcycle retrofit planning and provides a foundation for future refinement when retrofit outcome data becomes available.

title: Personalized Patient Diet Conversation Modeling using deep learning
authors: R. Aruna, Krishnappa Gari Chandravamsi, Gangineni Suneel Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Individualized diets have been crucial in contemporary healthcare, particularly for patients with chronic diseases such as diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular, and kidney disease. Correct nutrition management has a great influence on treatment,health and well-being. However, current diet recommendation models tend to be not very personalized and interactive, which restricts their efficacy. In this project, a deep learning-based conversational system is proposed based on intelligentness and the introduction of a personalized diet recommendation in a natural and multi-turn conversation. Collect user-related information including medical history, allergies, age, BMI, glucose levels, and dietary preferences and interpolate these data with nutritional databases and disease-specific instructions. The system supports dynamic context-driven interactions that are realistic to human consultations, using a transformer-based neural architecture. Each suggestion is validated using a rule-based verification module to verify compliance with clinical standards and prevent errors in the suggestions. The system, which is implemented with the help of modern NLP systems, such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Hugging Face Transformers, is tested against metrics such as accuracy, BLEU score, perplexity, and user engagement.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Deep Learning for Safety-Oriented Triage of Occult Wrist Fractures in Radiographs
authors: Hardik Agarwal, Ekagra Mishra, Siddharth V, Suresh Annamalai
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Occult fractures of the scaphoid and distal radius remain difficult to detect on initial wrist radiographs, and false-negative interpretations can delay treatment, leading to nonunion, avascular necrosis, and long-term functional impairment. Existing deep learning systems for radiograph analysis are typically designed as closed binary classifiers that must output a definitive normal or abnormal label for every input, with no mechanism to express diagnostic uncertainty. This design assumption is misaligned with clinical practice, where ambiguous wrist radiographs routinely prompt immobilisation and referral for CT or MRI rather than a forced binary decision. This paper introduces an uncertainty-aware triage pipeline for wrist radiographs that replaces the forced binary output with a three-way policy: confident normal, confident abnormal, or deferred for advanced imaging. A ResNet-50 backbone fine-tuned on the wrist subset of MURA v1.1 is augmented with Monte Carlo (MC) dropout inference. Across T = 30 stochastic forward passes per study, the sample mean provides a calibrated abnormality score while the sample variance quantifies epistemic uncertainty. A data-driven uncertainty threshold γ routes high-variance studies directly to CT/MRI referral. At a 25% deferral rate, the system reduces missed fractures from 295 to 221—a 25.1% reduction—while accuracy on auto-diagnosed cases remains essentially unchanged. These results show that uncertainty quantification is a practical and clinically meaningful mechanism for improving patient safety in AI-assisted fracture screening.

title: Advanced Quantum Aware Modeling of Ultra-Scaled Gate-All-Around FETs
authors: Deepapriyaa T, Charvy Amrisha J, Mathan N, N M Nandhitha
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: As CMOS technology scales toward sub-5 nm dimensions, gate-all-around (GAA) field-effect transistors (FETs) have emerged as the leading device architecture due to their superior electrostatic control. At such ultra-scaled dimensions, quantum mechanical confinement significantly alters carrier distribution, threshold voltage, and transport characteristics. In this work, a comprehensive TCAD-based investigation of stacked GAA nanowire and nanosheet FETs is presented, with emphasis on quantum confinement effects captured using density-gradient quantum potential models. Three-dimensional device structures are constructed using Sentaurus Structure Editor and simulated using Sentaurus Device. Steady-state gate charge, transfer, and output characteristics are analyzed, and key performance metrics including threshold voltage, subthreshold slope, transconductance, output conductance, and ON/OFF current ratio are extracted. The results demonstrate that nanowire GAA FETs provide superior electrostatic integrity and reduced leakage due to strong confinement, while nanosheet GAA FETs exhibit enhanced drive current and transconductance owing to reduced confinement and larger effective channel width.

title: Vision Transformer-based Classification Approach for Driver Drowsiness Detection
authors: D. Ganesh, M. Sunil Kumar, P.Teja Sree, Mekala Srujana, Mosa Sai Charan, P.Chandu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Driver distraction due to drowsiness is one of the major causes of accidents on roads and threatens road safety. Traditional systems for detecting drowsiness involve intrusive sensors and/or manually engineered features, inefficient for real-time systems. In this work, we propose a non-intrusive driver drowsiness detection method using Vision Transformers to infer relevant face expressions like eye closure, yawning, and head position. The proposed system is trained and tested on the NTHU Driver Drowsiness Detection (NTHU-DDD) database, consisting of varied driving scenarios along with light and facial expression variations. The proposed method, with the help of Vision Transformers, uses global spatial dependency among the facial components, hence being more accurate in detecting drowsiness for ensuring road safety. Results demonstrate that our proposed method performs better than conventional CNN models and can be employed effectively for real-time driver monitoring.

title: Cognitive Test Agent
authors: Santhosh Sahaya Raj, Sundaresan Sambandan, Sreenivasa Komaramkandth
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Writing Automated Test Cases (ATCs) consumes around 30% of a software release's effort, with each test case often taking three to five days. Most of that time is dedicated to coding, reviewing, and debugging scripts, rather than to actual testing. The proposed Cognitive Testing Agent (CTA) aims to eliminate this traditional and time-consuming testing approach. CTA understands plain-text test descriptions and executes the tests directly, without generating any code. Underneath, an LLM Multi-Agent System (LMAS) breaks each description into subtasks and delegates them to five specialized agents through Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers connected to the Access Controller. We validated this approach on a COAX broadband Device Under Test (DUT). Compared to traditional methods, the testing effort was reduced from approximately 30% of a release to around 5%.

title: Bottom-Up Predictive Modeling for EV Adoption and Grid Planning: A Data-Driven Framework Leveraging Artificial Intelligence
authors: Alec Zhixiao Lin, Isaac Chen Fu, David Zhongxuan Ruan
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Transportation electrification is a cornerstone of the transition to clean energy. As households, businesses, and public agencies increasingly replace gasoline-powered vehicles with electric vehicles (EVs), the demand for charging infrastructure accelerates, driving a substantial increase in electricity consumption. To support effective grid planning, utilities must leverage granular geographic data to anticipate when and where EVs will emerge or expand across the service territory. This requires a suite of predictive models, including: 1) Detection models to identify existing EVs that remain unaccounted for in utility databases. 2) Propensity models to estimate which customers are most likely to adopt EVs in the near future. 3) Forecasting models to project future energy demand growth in specific service areas, enabling proactive infrastructure investment. 4) EV load disaggregation model to improve the accuracy in estimating incremental demand attributable to EV charging. This paper presents a bottom-up blueprint that integrates diverse datasets to build targeted predictive models aligned with distinct operational and planning needs. In addition to machine learning techniques, the paper explores innovative applications of graph theory, survival analysis and the Bass diffusion model to enhance predictive accuracy and streamline the modeling process. The framework and data sources presented in this paper are adaptable for a range of modeling applications, including the adoption of solar panels, battery storage, and other green energy technologies.

title: An Explainable AI-Based Hybrid CNN-BiLSTM Framework for Early Detection of Mental Health Disorders Using Social Media and Survey Data
authors: Om Patro, Krishnamoorthy N
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Human feelings tend to change based on different situations and conditions, affecting our mental and psychological states. When stress reaches a point of being unbearable and uncontrollable, it may result in serious psychological and physical disturbances. Early detection of mental health problems is very important since early intervention can avoid worsening the condition, ultimately taking individuals towards a healthier and happier life. The most prevalent mental illnesses to exist are anxiety, depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder and so on. Improvement in mental health can lower risks considerably in the form of suicidal tendencies and ensure general well-being. More improvement can be obtained by using deep learning models such as CNNs and platforms such as TensorFlow. Researchers have employed several data sources, such as questionnaire data and social network data collected online, to identify mental health problems. This paper mainly focuses on assessing methods that provide better performance in mental health disorder prediction by combining both social network data and mental health datasets. The paper also discusses explainability methods to improve model interpretability, which can improve model accuracy and reliability. For data imbalance remediation, while SMOTE and ADASYN algorithms have been used, investigating superior techniques like Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) may yield even more realistic synthetically augmented data. Additionally, the deployment of Explainable AI techniques, e.g., LIME and SHAP, will greatly help towards comprehending the decisions made by the model, facilitating early intervention, enhancing problem-solving, and minimizing false positives.

title: Comprehensive Modeling of Thermal Tension for Highly Precise Web-Tension Control in Roll-to-Roll Multilayer Manufacturing
authors: Nagesh Pandey, Sivaranjini Mohanan, Jongsu Lee
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Roll-to-roll multilayer manufacturing system offers a promising alternative for the mass production of flexible and large-area electronic devices. In this system, thermal deformation of the web, caused by fluctuations in the drying temperature, can cause significant tension errors that may exceed the controllable range of tension controllers. In this study, we developed a comprehensive model to accurately estimate the tension error caused by fluctuations in drying temperature in an industrial-scale roll-to-roll machine. We established a dynamic mass conservation model that accounted for continuous mass variations in the web resulting from thermal strain changes, serving as the foundation for the proposed model. In addition, we defined a correction factor to represent the impact of web temperature on thermal strain, based on the drying temperature and its profile in the drying span. The model was compared with a conventional model and verified experimentally on an industrial-scale roll-to-roll system and achieved superior estimation performance, with an average accuracy of 0.95 in the time domain and 0.92 in the frequency domain. Subsequently, we analyzed the effects of machine parameters. Finally, we determined the maximum permissible drying temperature fluctuations required to keep the target level of tension disturbance in two industrial-scale roll-to-roll machines.

title: Dual-Mode Transduction–Induced Noise Correlation for Enhanced Fused Resolution in A Single Resonant Accelerometer
authors: Chengxin Li, Fan Wu, Hemin Zhang, Chun Zhao, Bernardo Pereira Madeira, Chen Wang, Mustafa Mert Torunbalci, Lieven De Strycker, Michael Kraft
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Signal fusion between various sensors has been explored to enhance the robustness and resolution, leveraging their complementary characteristics. In general, a higher correlation between sensors leads to more efficient fusion. This work presents a silicon resonant accelerometer (SRA) that enables tunable noise correlation between capacitive and piezoresistive dual transductions. The accelerometer comprises a double clamped beam resonator with piezoresistive gauges and capacitive electrodes, allowing simultaneous transduction outputs of both signals. Through tuning the thermal bias applied to the piezoresistive gauges, the transduction gain of the piezoresistive signal can be tuned, enabling real-time control of the correlation between the two signals. The real-time frequencies demodulated from the signals with variable correlation are fused by using a general 2nd-order Kalman filter algorithm. Experimental results demonstrate that the fused output achieves a resolution of 0.49 µg/√Hz. This represents an improvement of approximately 10.45 dB compared with capacitive detection alone. Meanwhile, the fused signal preserves the broad bandwidth of ∼1150 Hz provided by piezoresistive detection. This highlights the effectiveness of noise correlation manipulation for enhancing sensor performance by output signal fusion.

title: A cloud detection method for remote sensing images combining optical images and SAR images
authors: Chengjie Hu, Bo Wang, Qinghong Sheng, Jun Li, Xiao Ling, Xiang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate cloud detection in optical remote sensing imagery remains a persistent challenge, particularly for semi-transparent thin clouds and high-reflectance terrestrial surfaces where spectral signatures often overlap. This paper proposes SAR-OPT-CDNet, a novel multimodal fusion framework that synergistically integrates optical spectral information with the cloud-penetrating structural cues from synthetic aperture radar (SAR). To effectively bridge the modality gap, we develop a Multimodal Feature Fusion Module (MFFM) that explicitly disentangles and aggregates joint representations and cross-modal differential features under a channel-spatial attention mechanism. Furthermore, we introduce Mamba-inspired Visual State Space (VSS) Blocks to facilitate efficient multi-directional contextual modeling with linear computational complexity, significantly enhancing the delineation of irregular cloud boundaries and the detection of fragmented cloud structures. Experimental results on the newly established NUAA-SAR-OPT-CD dataset—comprising temporally paired Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 imagery—demonstrate that our method achieves a state-of-the-art overall accuracy (OA) of 99.13% and an F1-score of 0.9178. Qualitative and quantitative analyses confirm that the proposed framework substantially reduces omission errors in thin-cloud regions and suppresses false alarms over high-reflectance surfaces, offering a robust and efficient solution for complex remote sensing applications.

title: Monolithic 3D Voltage Converter Enabled Fine-Grain Dynamic Voltage Frequency Scaling in Many-Core Processors
authors: Jungyoun Kwak, Faaiq Waqar, Shimeng Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: The continued scaling of CMOS technology amplifies the challenges of delivering power efficiently to many-core processors. Dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) offers substantial energy savings, but its effectiveness depends on the voltage settling time, efficiency, and integration cost of the underlying regulators. This paper presents a system-level evaluation of monolithic 3D (M3D) per-core DC–DC converters for fine-grained DVFS in many-core CPUs. Using workload traces extracted from gem5 and an energy modeling framework that accounts for regulator efficiency, power delivery network (PDN) parasitics, transition overheads, and area, we compare five representative configurations across 8-, 16-, and 32-core systems. Results show that off-chip converters with global DVFS achieve only ~24% average energy reduction, on-chip per-core DVFS improves this metric to ~41%, and M3D per-core DVFS delivers the highest savings at ~43% while also minimizing die area by up to 20% relative to on-chip converters and 38% relative to off-chip solutions. These findings underscore the importance of localized regulation and vertical integration for designing scalable, energy-efficient processors in future generations.

title: Descriptor: A Simulated Multi-Tenant Quantum Execution Environment Attack Dataset (QAD)
authors: Pasindu Udugahapattuwa, Chamitha De Alwis, Uditha Wijewardhana, Madhusanka Liyanage
publish: IEEE Data Descriptions
abstract: The advancement of quantum computing has introduced novel cybersecurity risks, vulnerabilities, and challenges. However, there remains a significant gap in the availability of comprehensive, large-scale datasets that capture quantum-native characteristics, such as labeled attacks, multi-attack scenarios, hardware-noise modeling, and multi-tenant environments. This article describes the quantum attack dataset (QAD), a findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable (FAIR) resource that systematically synthesizes large-scale quantum attack scenarios over a simulated multi-tenant quantum execution environment under controlled noise and attack models. QAD implements seven attack scenarios: swap injection, gate insertion, depolarizing noise, amplitude damping, measurement tampering, pulse-level attacks, and thermal relaxation, selected to reflect prevalent threats in noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ)-era devices. The dataset is organized into two complementary views: a 77-feature set derived from circuit structure and measurement statistics, and generation metadata capturing attack parameters and hardware profiles, released separately to prevent label leakage. Two one-million-sample datasets are provided: a balanced configuration for method development, and an unbalanced configuration reflecting a literature-informed threat scenario distribution. Technical validation using four machine-learning baselines reveals that the discriminative challenge is concentrated in two attack families, swap injection (F1 ≈ 0.82–0.84) and pulse-level attacks (F1 ≈ 0.87–0.89), while five of the eight classes saturate at F1 = 1.00 across all models. The meaningful baseline is a noise-blind operating point (weighted F1 ≈ 0.88) that excludes a generation-time correlate; a fullfeature upper bound (weighted F1 > 0.97) is also reported but should not be read as evidence of uniform task difficulty. Data files, schemas, and scripts are hosted in a persistent repository with detailed metadata, quality checks, and usage notes, enabling researchers to reproduce pipelines, extend attack scenarios, and apply QAD to future quantum-resistant security studies.

title: A Seamlessly Reconfigurable ANPC-DAB Converter Operating Within an Ultra-Wide Voltage Range of 250–2000 V
authors: Hamid Raheem, Min-Jae Jang, Minsung Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article presents an active-neutral-point-clamped (ANPC) dual-active-bridge (DAB) DC/DC converter, which is capable of operating under an ultra-wide voltage gain <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$M$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. The proposed modulation operates an ANPC-DAB converter with 3-level voltage <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$v_{ab}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (when <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$M$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$<$</tex-math> </inline-formula> 1), with 4-level voltage <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$v_{cd}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (when 1 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\leq$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$M$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$<$</tex-math> </inline-formula> 2), and with 3-level voltage <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$v_{cd}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (when <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$M$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\geq$</tex-math> </inline-formula> 2). Seven distinct inductor current patterns are observed according to different secondary-side voltages and power levels. When it operates as an ANPC-DAB converter with 4-level voltage <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$v_{cd}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, the duty ratio is selected by a voltage matching controller while the phase is determined by the expected output power. This trait simplifies the control scheme. To accomplish heat balancing among the switches, we applied alternative switching modulations in two switching periods. We also applied different dead-times for the switches that experience different zero-voltage switching currents. The ANPC-DAB converter and its switching modulation scheme were verified experimentally using a 250 V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math> </inline-formula> 2000 V/10 kW hardware prototype that was fed 800 V input.

title: Physics-Informed Ensemble Learning for Detection of Malfunctioning Ocean Bottom Pressure Gauges: A Case Study on the S-Net Network
authors: Oleg V. Ponomarev, Nikita V. Belyakov, Sergey V. Kolesov, Andrei M. Volgarev
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: In this study, we analyze bottom pressure time series from 139 operational stations of the S-net network to detect potential anomalies. As of 12 March 2025, 15 stations were identified as malfunctioning: S1N09, S1N13, S1N21, S2N06, S2N18, S2N20, S2N25, S3N02, S3N04, S3N22, S4N10, S4N24, S5N10, S5N12, and S5N16. Notably, the failure of station S2N25 was captured in real time during observations. The analysis revealed that increased depth is statistically associated with a higher risk of sensor malfunction (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p < 0.05$</tex-math></inline-formula>). To enable automatic identification of malfunctioning pressure gauges, we introduce an ensemble learning model that combines three complementary reconstruction techniques—Seasonal Auto Regressive Integrated Moving Average forecasting, physics-based tidal prediction using the TPXO model, and interstation similarity analysis—evaluated with two error metrics (mean squared error and DTW). Their outputs are integrated by a lightweight random-forest meta-classifier, yielding an interpretable and data-efficient hybrid model. The model aggregates prior knowledge of the physical characteristics of the signal with machine learning to classify stations as either operational or malfunctioning. The average MCC-score on the test set reached 0.90 with a standard deviation of 0.06. Because the proposed model is fully interpretable and computationally inexpensive, it is suitable for real-time deployment within existing tsunami warning infrastructures.

title: Agentic AI for SAGIN Resource Management: Semantic Awareness, Orchestration, and Optimization
authors: Linghao Zhang, Haitao Zhao, Bo Xu, Hongbo Zhu, Xianbin Wang
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: Space-air-ground integrated networks (SAGIN) promise ubiquitous sixth-generation (6G) connectivity but face significant resource management challenges due to heterogeneous infrastructure, dynamic topologies, and stringent quality-of-service (QoS) requirements. Conventional model-driven approaches struggle with scalability and adaptability in such complex environments. This paper presents an agentic artificial intelligence (AI) framework for autonomous SAGIN resource management by embedding large language model (LLM)-based agents into a Monitor-Analyze-Plan-Execute-Knowledge (MAPE-K) control plane. The framework incorporates three specialized agents, namely semantic resource perceivers, intent-driven orchestrators, and adaptive learners, that collaborate through natural language reasoning to bridge the gap between operator intents and network execution. At the core of the framework is a hierarchical agent-reinforcement learning (RL) collaboration mechanism, wherein LLM-based orchestrators dynamically shape reward functions for RL agents based on semantic network conditions. Validation through uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted AI-generated content (AIGC) service orchestration in energy-constrained scenarios demonstrates that LLM-driven reward shaping achieves 14% energy reduction and the lowest average service latency among all compared methods. This agentic paradigm offers a scalable pathway toward adaptive, AI-native 6G networks, capable of autonomously interpreting intents and adapting to dynamic environments.

title: In-Context Learning for Deep Joint Source-Channel Coding Over MIMO Channels
authors: Meng Hua, Wenjing Zhang, Chenghong Bian, Deniz Gündüz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Large language models have demonstrated the ability to perform in-context learning (ICL), whereby the model performs predictions by directly mapping the query and a few examples from the given task to the output variable. In this paper, we study ICL for deep joint source-channel coding (DeepJSCC) in image transmission over multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems, where an ICL denoiser is employed for MIMO symbol estimation. We first study the transceiver without any hardware impairments and explore the integration of transformer-based ICL with DeepJSCC in both open-loop and closed-loop MIMO systems, depending on the availability of channel state information (CSI) at the transceiver. For both open-loop and closed-loop scenarios, we propose two MIMO transceiver architectures that leverage context information, i.e., pilot sequences and their outputs, as additional inputs, enabling the DeepJSCC encoder, DeepJSCC decoder, and the ICL denoiser to jointly learn encoding, decoding, and estimation strategies tailored to each channel realization. Next, we extend our study to a more challenging scenario where the transceiver suffers from in-phase and quadrature (IQ) imbalance, resulting in nonlinear MIMO estimation. In this case, the context information is also exploited, facilitating joint learning across the DeepJSCC encoder, decoder, and the ICL denoiser under hardware impairments and varying channel conditions. Numerical results demonstrate that the ICL denoiser for MIMO estimation significantly outperforms the conventional least-squares method, with even greater advantages under IQ imbalance. Moreover, the proposed transformer-based ICL framework, integrated with contextual information, achieves significant improvements in end-to-end image reconstruction quality under transceiver IQ imbalance.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Enabled Persistent Surveillance with Energy-Aware UAV-UGV Systems for Disaster Management Applications_supp3-3696943.mp4
authors: Md Safwan Mondal
publish: N/A
abstract: Integrating Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) with Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) provides an effective solution for persistent surveillance in disaster management. UAVs excel at covering large areas rapidly, but their range is limited by battery capacity. UGVs, though slower, can carry larger batteries for extended missions. By using UGVs as mobile recharging stations, UAVs can extend mission duration through periodic refueling, leveraging the complementary strengths of both systems. To optimize this energy-aware UAV-UGV cooperative routing problem, we propose a planning framework that determines optimal routes and recharging points between a UAV and a UGV. Our solution employs a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework built on an encoder-decoder transformer architecture with multi-head attention mechanisms. This architecture enables the model to sequentially select actions for visiting mission points and coordinating recharging rendezvous between the UAV and UGV. The DRL model is trained to minimize the age periods (the time gap between consecutive visits) of mission points, ensuring effective surveillance. We evaluate the framework across various problem sizes and distributions, comparing its performance against heuristic methods and an existing learning-based model. Results show that our approach consistently outperforms these baselines in both solution quality and runtime. Additionally, we demonstrate the DRL policy's applicability in a real-world disaster scenario as a case study and explore its potential for online mission planning to handle dynamic changes. Adapting the DRL policy for priority-driven surveillance highlights the model's generalizability for real-time disaster response. More details are available on our website: https://sites.google.com/view/ecucps.
